name: Student Theatre Council
description: The Student Theatre Council is a coalition of the student theatre organizations of The George Washington University. It provides a forum for discussion and conflict resolution amongst its constituent organizations, it allows those involved in student theatre at The George Washington University to more easily combine resources and coordinate group actions for representing our common interests to the university, and it governs, maintains and improves the venues in which the member organizations perform. The Student Theatre Council has the continuing mission of fostering the growth and vitality of George Washington University student theatre community, and of providing educational and cultural programs of the highest quality to the George Washington University student body as a whole.
summary: The Student Theatre Council is a coalition of the student theatre organizations of The George Washington University: Fourteenth Grade Players, GW Shakespeare Company, Generic Theater Company, and Forbidden Planet Productions.

name: TAMID at GW
description:
TAMID at GW's program is designed to offer experiential business consulting experience. Beginning with an interactive classroom curriculum, members will progresses to hands-on experience with companies in the heart of the startup nation, Israel. 

GW TAMID is comprised of dedicated GW students from all majors and colleges, faiths, and political backgrounds who are brought together through their shared goals of advancing their skillsets through professional experiences. Beyond the robust business experiences that students engage in, TAMID at GW values forming a college community and continues to create an organization that fosters long lasting relationships well after college. 

 

TAMID is characterized by the following four programs:

Education: Once accepted into TAMID, new members go through an education program for first semester that prepares them to contribute to a consulting or investing team. With an emphasis on practical experiential learning, the education program is hardly like sitting in a classroom and listening to a lecture. New members learn a variety of skills in workshops and competitions, as they delve into a variety of fields within consulting, investing, and entrepreneurship. Each lesson is designed to be fun and engaging; no prior knowledge is required. 

Consulting: TAMID provides pro-bono advisory services for the most exciting and innovative startup  companies coming out of Israel. By leveraging both our experience as university students and our formal classroom education, TAMID Consulting teams are able to offer effective and efficient consulting services through market research, market strategy, branding, and business development strategy. The GW chapter works with four Israeli startups every year and will be working with Skyler AI, SafeUP, Sign Now, and Lior Shoham LTD this semester.

Investment Fund: The TAMID Investment fund members work in groups conducting due-diligence on stocks to advise the TAMID national portfolio by submitting stock pitches. Every semester the chapter competes in an organization-wide trading competition, giving each member’s research a national impact. The fund members also have opportunities to interact with financial experts and GW alumni who work in the financial industry.

The Fellowship: Health conditions permitting, every TAMID student can apply to go on an eight week internship program in Israel. Participants are assigned to an Israeli company based on their interests, and work with the company on a capstone project by the completion of the fellowship. Along with interning, members have the opportunity to explore the state of Israel through fun actives while meeting other TAMID members from across the country

 

TAMID Group: A Bridge Between Future Business Leaders & Israel

 

*Updated May 2025
summary: TAMID provides undergraduate students hands-on business experience by engaging with the Israeli economy.

name: The GW Vibes
description: Three-time ICCA semi-finalists, the Vibes are GW’s premier a cappella group. We’re known to perform for the GW community and beyond, and this past February, the Vibes finished in the top 3 of the 2023 ICCA Mid-Atlantic Quarterfinals! Listen for our award-winning song on the Best of College A Cappella 2019 album, and check out our new EP ‘Down to Earth,’ out now on all streaming platforms! The Vibes are a big family that always aims to entertain and has a great time doing it.
summary: Three time ICCA Semi-Finalists, The Vibes are GW’s premier A Capella group. The Vibes enjoy performing for our GW community and beyond. More than just a bad-ass A Capella group, the Vibes are a big family.

name: Kappa Sigma Fraternity
description: Kappa Sigma, as one of the oldest and largest fraternities in North America, believes in the total development of today's college man, fostering a values-based fraternity environment on our principles of Fellowship, Leadership, Scholarship, and Service.
summary: Kappa Sigma, as one of the oldest and largest fraternities in North America, believes in the total development of today's college man, fostering a values-based fraternity environment on our principles of Fellowship, Leadership, Scholarship, and Service.

name: Physical Therapy Student Organization
description: The George Washington University (GWU) Physical Therapy Student Organization (PTSO) was established to provide a mechanism for students of the Doctor of Physical Therapy (DPT) program to interact, fundraise, and serve the George Washington University community. Additionally, this organization will provide an opportunity for collaboration between representatives from each class within the DPT program, as well as between the DPT program, GWU, and its community.
summary: The George Washington University (GWU) Physical Therapy Student Organization (PTSO) was established to provide a mechanism for students of the Doctor of Physical Therapy (DPT) program to interact, fundraise, and serve the George Washington University c...

name: Sikh Student Association
description:
The Sikh Student Association at GW is a vibrant and inclusive community dedicated to fostering a sense of belonging, cultural appreciation, and service among Sikh students and allies on campus. Founded on the principles of Sikhism, the association aims to create a supportive environment where members can connect with their faith, heritage, and fellow students.

Through a variety of events, including discussions, workshops, social gatherings, and community service initiatives, the association provides opportunities for members to deepen their understanding of Sikh values, traditions, and history while also promoting dialogue and understanding across diverse backgrounds. 

Join our GroupMe! https://groupme.com/join_group/109513962/XsZPbHI6
summary: To promote awareness of Sikhi on campus and to foster a better sense of community amongst the Sikh students at GW.

name: L'Chaim Jewish Student Group
description: L'Chaim Jewish Student Group seeks to be an "inclusive home away from home" for Jews on campus and offers a wealth of social, educational, and spiritual programs at GW. L'Chaim fosters Jewish pride on campus and has a positive impact on each student's experience at GW.
summary: L'Chaim Jewish Student Group seeks to be an "inclusive home away from home" for Jews on campus and offers a wealth of social, educational, and spiritual programs at GW. L'Chaim fosters Jewish pride and has a positive impact on students' time here at GW.

name: GW Net Impact
description: Net Impact GW is an student-led civic and social impact organization dedicated to empowering students to drive meaningful change through business, policy, and community leadership. As part of the global Net Impact network, our chapter connects GW students to a worldwide community of young changemakers focused on social enterprise, sustainability, and responsible leadership. We host workshops, speaker events, and hands-on consulting projects with mission-driven organizations, allowing members to develop practical skills in ethical business, nonprofit strategy, and impact innovation. Looking ahead, Net Impact GW is expanding its partnerships, impact consulting opportunities, and collaborations with other campus groups to broaden our reach and deepen community engagement. We believe students have the power to reimagine systems—and we are building the network to make that possible.
summary: GW Net Impact’s mission to help GW students, especially graduate students, to make a sizable positive impact on today's environment, social and corporate governance issues is at the intersection of people, planet and business.

name: GW RAGE - Reproductive Autonomy and Gender Equity
description:
** Formerly GW Voices for Choices

GW RAGE is the Reproductive Justice and sex positivity organization at George Washington University in Washington, DC. Founded in 2001, we exist to educate the university community about reproductive justice, to lead reproductive justice activism on campus and in the DC community, and to serve as a partner to local and national reproductive justice, health, and rights efforts. GW RAGE aims to uphold all aspects of the reproductive justice framework as established by SisterSong, including all intersections of feminism such as racial and economic justice. RAGE also strives to educate the university student body about sexual and reproductive health and promote a culture of sex positivity.

RAGE advocates for reproductive justice, health, and rights on campus and beyond. Our advocacy includes both work to support local and national efforts for reproductive justice and work on campus to promote reproductive justice at GW.

RAGE promotes access to sexual and reproductive healthcare on campus including sexual health materials, abortion, and emergency contraception. We are committed to continuing to expand and promote access to the full range of sexual and reproductive healthcare for everyone on GW’s campus.

RAGE seeks to educate the GW community on reproductive justice, abortion justice, sexual health, and more. Through educational events, trainings, and tabling RAGE provides GW students with information on reproductive justice and sexual and reproductive health.
summary: GW RAGE is the reproductive justice and sex positivity organization on GW's campus! If you are also pro-abortion and passionate about reproductive justice, welcome!

name: GW Quiz Bowl Team
description:
Quiz bowl is a buzzer-based team competition that tests players' knowledge of all sorts of subjects, including history, literature, science, geography, economics, philosophy, pop culture, and more! Here at GW, we keep quiz bowl open to students of all levels of experience and backgrounds. Our practices are informal and open to all students. For those looking to get more involved in competitions, we also compete in tournaments against other Mid-Atlantic schools throughout the academic year. Please consider stopping by one of our weekly practices and do not hesitate to contact us for more information! All are welcome.

 

If you are interested in joining, please fill out our interest form.

For more info, check out our LinkTree at https://linktr.ee/gwquizbowl.
summary: The GW Quiz Bowl Team is an academic trivia organization open to players of all levels and backgrounds. We compete with other schools in the Mid-Atlantic region, and have plenty of fun along the way!

name: Sons of Pitch
description: Founded in 2003, Sons of Pitch is GW's premier a cappella group and reigning Champion of Battle of the A Cappellas. SOP is comprised of tenor, baritone, and bass vocal parts. In 2019, SOP sang at the National Cherry Blossom Festival in front of thousands of attendees and for a national broadcast, and in 2019, 2023, and 2024, SOP sang the National Anthem at Nats Park for Washington Nationals baseball games. In 2025, SOP advanced to the Mid-Atlantic Semifinals at the International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella for the first time. In 2020, SOP released their debut EP, and their second EP, “With You I Belong”, was released in April of 2023. Follow them on Spotify at "GW Sons of Pitch"!
summary: Sons of Pitch is GW's premier a cappella group and reigning Champion of Battle of the A Cappellas. It is comprised of Tenor, Baritone, and Bass vocal parts. Founded in 2003, Sons of Pitch proudly represents 20 years of a cappella at GW.

name: Agape Campus Christian Fellowship
description: Agapé Campus Christian Fellowship is dedicated to sharing the message and power of God’s unconditional love to GWU through a lifestyle of revival consisting of worship, prayer, discipleship, and fellowship. We have weekly bible studies and campus meetings, and Sunday worship services. We are so excited to meet you and looking forward to a great year together!
summary: Agapé Campus Christian Fellowship is dedicated to sharing the message and power of God’s unconditional love through a lifestyle of revival consisting of worship, prayer, discipleship, and fellowship.

name: The Jewish Student Association
description: The Jewish Student Association serves as the foundation for Jewish life on GW's campus by representing the interests of all Jewish students at GW, working with other student groups and campus administration to support and celebrate the Jewish community, serve as a unified voice for the Jewish community, act in pursuance of Jewish laws, traditions, and values, and partner with Hillel at the George Washington University to achieve those goals.
summary: The Jewish Student Association serves as the foundation for Jewish life on GW's campus by representing the interests of all Jewish students at GW, working with other student groups and campus administration to support and celebrate the Jewish community...

name: GlobeMed at GWU
description: GlobeMed at GW is a student-led organization that strives to unite the GW and DC community in engaging with grassroots leaders to cultivate informed global health advocates, raise awareness on global health inequities, and learn through service with global-community partnerships. Our partner organization, Set Her Free, is a non-profit based in Kampala, Uganda, that works to improve the health of vulnerable young women and girls by equipping them with the skills and resources needed to live independent, self-determined lives free from exploitation. GlobeMed works with Set Her Free to support their 4 focus areas, which are: Education and Development, Protect and Empower, Sexual and Reproductive Health, and Climate Change Adaptation. Specifically, GlobeMed supports Set Her Free through advocacy, fundraising events, and partnership in long-term projects. Throughout the year, GlobeMed at GW also raises awareness about a variety of other public health issues through on-campus events including our annual Gala and Hilltop Conference. These events have focused on several important public health topics including mental health, disaster management, sexual and reproductive health, and health equity. Every member of this chapter plays an important role in our organization and is able to pursue interests related to creative design, event planning, grant writing, and health promotion. If you're interested in joining or have more questions, please reach out! Contact our co-presidents using the email linked below.
summary: GlobeMed at GW strives to unite the GW and DC community in engaging with grassroots leaders to cultivate informed global health advocates, raise awareness on global health inequities, and learn through service with global-community partnerships.

name: Humanitarian Mapping Society
description: The purpose of the George Washington University Humanitarian Mapping Society is to use open source data and GIS technologies to map potential disaster areas, provide resources for emergency management, connect its members with open source and GIS organizations in the Washington, D.C. area, and serve any upcoming needs of our project partners and the open source mapping community.
summary: The purpose of the George Washington University Humanitarian Mapping Society is to use open source data and GIS technologies to map potential disaster areas, provide resources for emergency management, connect its members with open source and GIS organ.

name: Sigma Delta Tau
description: Sigma Delta Tau was founded on March 25th, 1917 at Cornell University and our motto is “Patriae Multae Spes Una,” meaning “One Hope of Many People.” Sigma Delta Tau aims at promoting academic achievement, building leadership skills, instilling a sense of philanthropy, and creating a lifetime bond of friendship. SDT at GW hosts multiple philanthropy events a year such as Sig Delt Soccer, Sig Delt Sweetheart, Eat Your Way Through DC and bake sales among others. Our largest philanthropy is Sig Delt Soccer week, which includes events like bake sales, profit shares, penny wars, EAT your way through DC, and our final event, the soccer tournament. Every fraternity and sorority participates in this week and it is a great way to meet other Greeks! All of our fundraising goes directly towards our national philanthropies, Prevent Child Abuse America and JWI. While balancing academics, philanthropy, and all of our individual interests, the sisters of Sigma Delta Tau all have one thing in common… our love for SDT. From our unforgettable formals, themed crush parties, and memorable sisterhood events, the girls of SDT love to have fun together! Inter-Greek relations are very important to the sisters of Sigma Delta Tau as well. We love to hold philanthropy and social events with other chapters in the Greek community. All of our events create life-long, everlasting memories with both our sisters and friends.
summary: Sigma Delta Tau was founded in 1917 at Cornell University with the motto “Patriae Multae Spes Una,” meaning “One Hope of Many People.” We promote academic achievement, leadership skills, instilling a sense of philanthropy, and forming lifelong friendships

name: GW Troubadours
description: The Troubadours - affectionately known as “The Troubs” - is a co-ed a cappella group based at The George Washington University. Founded in 1925, generations of Troubadours have given countless performances for adoring fans worldwide. The original “Traveling Troubadours” toured internationally to entertain U.S. military personnel on remote overseas bases, and the organization has since developed into a modern, student-run a cappella troupe. The Troubs continue to perform original arrangements of songs from every conceivable genre, and their talent and unique sound has earned high praise from industry experts and community members alike. The Troubadours have recorded 8 studio albums and a single, Crazy, released in 2025. All albums can be found on all major streaming services.
summary: The Troubadours - affectionately known as “The Troubs” - is a co-ed a cappella group based at The George Washington University. Founded in 1925, generations of Troubadours have given countless performances for adoring fans worldwide.

name: Sustainable Urban Planning Student Organization
description: In association with George Washington University, we are a cohort of students who envision a future of healthy urban landscapes that are socially equitable, economically resilient and environmentally just. It is our mission to execute advocacy and outreach in the field of sustainable urban planning. We consist of a five member Executive Board, supported by four Committees, and represent over 50 students associated with the Sustainable Urban Planning Program in George Washington University's College of Professional Studies.
summary: We are a cohort of students who envision a future of healthy urban landscapes that are socially equitable, economically resilient and environmentally just. It is our mission to execute advocacy and outreach in the field of sustainable urban planning.

description: CSI is an international honor society that values academic and professional excellence in counseling. We promote a strong professional identity through members (professional counselors, counselor educators, and students) who contribute to the realization of a healthy society by fostering wellness and human dignity. Our mission is to promote scholarship, research, professionalism, leadership, advocacy, and excellence in counseling, and to recognize high attainment in the pursuit of academic and clinical excellence in the profession of counseling.
summary: CSI is an international honor society that values academic and professional excellence in counseling. We promote a strong professional identity through members (professional counselors, counselor educators, and students) who contribute to the realizati..

name: National Council of Negro Women- GWU Section
description: The National Council of Negro Women, Inc. (NCNW) is a council of national African American women's organizations and community-based sections with a mission to lead, develop, and advocate for women of African descent as they support their families and communities. NCNW fulfills this purpose through research, advocacy, and national and community-based services and programs on issues of health, education, and economic empowerment in the United States and Africa. With its 39 national affiliates and more than 240 sections, NCNW is a 501(c)3 organization with an outreach to nearly four million women. The George Washington University Section's purpose is to propel the mission of NCNW within the GW community.
summary: The National Council of Negro Women, Inc. (NCNW) is a council of national African American women's organizations and community-based sections with a mission to lead, develop, and advocate for women of African descent as they support their families and ...

name: Pi Beta Phi Fraternity
description: Welcome to the DC Alpha Chapter of Pi Beta Phi! Located in the heart of our nation’s capital, we have  104 dedicated members who make up a vibrant, unique, and diverse community. Our members are leaders both inside and outside of Pi Beta Phi. Apart from their involvement and commitment to our chapter, our members are active in organizations such as GW Women in Business, She’s the First GW, The Hatchet, GW Program Board, Dance Marathon, Alternative Breaks, student theater, music groups, and club sports. Our community also consists of NCAA Division 1 athletes, Student Association leaders, and undergraduate researchers. Because of our unique location, our members take advantage of our nation’s capital, interning at places such as the U.S. Congress, the Smithsonian, DC Public Schools, and at non-profit, consulting and lobbying firms throughout the city. One of the core aspects of our membership is supporting our national philanthropy, Read > Lead > Achieve, which supports children’s literacy. With events like Lemonade for Literacy and Pi Phi Gives You Wings, we participate in philanthropic activities all year long. Many of our members also tutor children in DC through programs such as Reading Partners and Jumpstart.
summary: Welcome to the DC Alpha Chapter of Pi Beta Phi! Located in the heart of our nation’s capital, we have over 110 dedicated members who make up a vibrant, unique, and diverse community.

name: Out for Business
description: Out for Business’s (OFB) mission is to build community among the lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, questioning, intersex, and other non-heterosexual (LGBTQI+) members of the George Washington University School of Business (GWSB) community and to raise awareness among all GWSB faculty, staff and students of the issues facing LGBTQI+ individuals in society.
summary: Out for Business’s (OFB) mission is to build community among LGBTQI+ members of the George Washington University School of Business (GWSB) community and their allies by providing personal support and professional guidance.

name: Young Americans for Freedom
description:
Young Americans for Freedom (YAF) was founded on September 11, 1960, by William F. Buckley Jr. in his childhood home in Sharon, Connecticut. The signing of the Sharon Statement, which, in 400 words, is described as the seminal document in the establishment of the modern conservative movement. During the 1960s and 1970s, YAF was instrumental in the campaigns for Goldwater and Reagan’s bid for the Republican nomination for the presidency, where the latter served as YAF’s honorary chairman from 1965 until his passing in 2004. Since its founding, YAF has been responsible for the creation of many influential political groups, from ACU, CPAC, and the National Journalism Center. 

 

YAF at GWU was founded in 2006 by Sergio Gor, and we continue to strive to be the intellectual hub for conservatism on campus. We welcome any ideas that people may hold and encourage them to share and develop them through productive collaboration and conversation with their peers. We are also active in campus culture and events. We are most well-known for hosting guest speakers such as Steve Forbes, Michael Knowles, Ben Shapiro, Senator Rand Paul, and many more on campus. In addition, we also hold panels and debates for students to discuss and share their beliefs in an intellectual setting. Every semester, we hold numerous retreats and conferences locally and nationally for GW students to attend, where they can hear and meet some of the most influential voices in American politics. We create a community to meet new friends, get involved, hear speakers, and share ideas that are united by values, not party politics. 

 

Young Americans for Freedom is part of the larger Young America’s Foundation. It is a 501(c)(3) Non-Profit organization and does not endorse or participate in any political campaigns.
summary: We inspire enlightened conservative thought and political conversations, fostering ideological development in the face of intellectual complacency. We foster public engagement, student panels, and public speakers.

name: Psi Chi, the International Honor Society in Psychology
description:
Psi Chi, the International Honor Society in Psychology, was founded to encourage, stimulate, and maintain excellence in scholarship in psychology, and to advance the science of psychology. Membership is open to undergraduate and graduate students of psychology who meet the minimum qualifications listed below. The Psi Chi chapter at GW provides activities to complement and enhance the Psychological & Brain Sciences (PBS) curriculum, and offers practical experience and fellowship through affiliation with the chapter.

Requirements:

AS OF FALL 2023:

Psychology GPA requirement: 3.5

Overall GPA requirements: Seniors—3.67; Juniors—3.61; Sophomores—3.5

Must be a second semester sophomore (or equivalent to 45 course credits including AP & IB),

Must have at least 9 credits in psychology courses

 

Please contact GW Psi Chi at gwupsichi@gmail.com.

 

*Please do not join unless you are a new inductee or current member without access. Membership in this organization is intended for current members of Psi Chi only. Please feel welcomed to use the email above for questions!

Here is the central application link if you meet the requirements listed above.

 

We're excited to meet you!
summary: Psi Chi, the International Honor Society in Psychology, was founded to encourage, stimulate, and maintain excellence in scholarship in psychology, and to advance the science of psychology.

name: GW Bhangra
description:
GW Bhangra was founded in the early 90s, making us the oldest competing collegiate bhangra team in the nation. Bhangra, a beautiful traditional folk dance of celebration, originates from Punjab. We compete around the country, though most of our time is devoted to performing around campus in the GW community, and spreading awareness of Punjabi culture through the DMV area. We also perform at private events as per request (just simply email gwbhangra@gmail.com).

We are not just a dance team, but a family, and we welcome people of all backgrounds, nationalities, and interests to add to our already diverse and fun-loving team. We love Bhangra and we strive to spread our passion for it everywhere we perform.
summary: GW Bhangra, founded in 1992, is the oldest collegiate bhangra team in the nation. We compete around the country, perform on campus and throughout the DMV. Bhangra is a Punjabi folk dance of celebration. Our team members practice between 9-15+ hours/week.

name: GW Club Boxing
description:
Registered under USIBA and Team USA Olympic Boxing, the mission of GW's Club Boxing team is to deliver the enjoyment and physical fitness of boxing in a safe and constructive environment. We teach the art of boxing, from the fundamentals to the advanced techniques in a safe atmosphere. Our coed team aims to compete in local, state, national, and international competitions, and is open students of all skill levels.

NOTE: the organization is on hold with the university, so practice for the Spring 2026 semester is currently pending. For the most up to date information join our GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/109301417/1Fq3hWGc or follow us on Instagram @gwboxing
summary: GW's boxing team aims to teach, train, and compete in the art of boxing, from newcomers to national champions, in a safe and fun environment.

name: GW UNICEF
description: The George Washington University's chapter of UNICEF supports UNICEF's mission statement to protect children's rights through fundraising, advocacy, and volunteering. GW UNICEF focuses on events and opportunities that are not only related to global advocacy, but also local service. The UNICEF organization is involved in providing humanitarian and development aid to children and people worldwide.
summary: The George Washington University chapter of UNICEF supports UNICEF's mission and other efforts in support of the world's children, through fundraising, global advocacy, and volunteering.

name: Japanese Cultural Association
description:
The Japanese Cultural Association (JCA) at GWU is a student-led org that unites individuals of all backgrounds to build leadership and cultural understanding through Japanese language, traditions, and current issues.

Our main goals are:

  1. To unite students at The George Washington University who have an interest in and enthusiasm for Japanese culture, language, and traditions.
  2. To foster awareness and understanding of Japanese culture in the GWU community through events.
  3. To encourage interest in multiple aspects of Japan and its culture, including but not limited to traditions, politics, society, art, language, food, etc.
  4. To provide a supportive and comfortable environment for all individuals studying Japanese language or culture.
  5. To conduct outreach to Japanese-related organizations in the Washington D.C.-Maryland-Virginia area.
  6. To promote informed engagement with contemporary and historical issues relevant to the Japanese diasporic community and other marginalized communities through educational events, cross-organizational collaboration, and cultural observance.
summary: JCA is an organization for GWU students of all backgrounds interested in Japanese culture, language, & traditions.

name: Graduate Consulting Club
description: The Graduate Consulting Club (GCC) will provide resources for graduate students at the George Washington University (GWU) to enhance their understanding of consulting and support informed career decisions in the field. GCC will assist its members in successfully navigating internship and full-time recruitment processes and prepare them effectively for consulting careers. Furthermore, GCC will collaborate with consulting firms and industry professionals to facilitate networking and recruitment opportunities. The club will also represent the George Washington University School of Business within the consulting community in the District of Columbia and across the United States by strengthening the university's brand and engaging alumni to enhance commercial and federal opportunities for all students on campus.
summary: The GCC will provide resources for graduate students at the George Washington University (GWU) to learn more about consulting and help them pursue a career in the industry.

name: Alpha Epsilon Phi
description: The mission of Alpha Epsilon Phi Sorority is to inspire and support exemplary women dedicated to friendship and a lifelong commitment to Alpha Epsilon Phi while building on the vision of our Jewish founders.
summary: The mission of Alpha Epsilon Phi Sorority is to inspire and support exemplary women dedicated to friendship and a lifelong commitment to Alpha Epsilon Phi while building on the vision of our Jewish founders.

name: GW College Democrats
description: We’re proud to be the largest student organization at GW and the largest chapter of the College Democrats in the country. Our organization hosts a variety of events throughout the year. This includes bringing speakers to campus, engaging in debates, organizing campaign trips, providing community service opportunities, and hosting membership events. We partner with other GW student organizations to foster a civically engaged community and promote a progressive agenda at GW.
summary: We’re proud to be the largest student organization at GW and the largest chapter of the College Democrats in the country. Our organization hosts a variety of events throughout the year. This includes bringing speakers to campus, engaging in debates, or..

name: Eta Society
description:
The Eta Society for Global Affairs is a professional organization dedicated to promoting leadership of our members in international affairs. We strive for intellectual education that will facilitate growth and encourage our members to pursue careers and curiosity in global service. By this we refer not only to the United States Foreign Service, but extend the definition to include careers in diplomacy, law, banking, education, consulting, journalism, non-governmental organizations, inter-governmental organizations, multinational corporations and much more.

Through professional development and personal growth, our community promotes participation and leadership in foreign service. Together we serve the community, explore world cultures, and build professional and social relationships. We share ideas and experiences in order to understand and stimulate unique, yet great, minds.

The Eta Society promotes professionalism while maintaining an unbreakable sense of membership among our members involved in this truly unique professional organization. We welcome all individuals considering a career in the Foreign Service, as well as those who have passionate interests in other areas of international affairs. Our history is built on the foundations of our previous organization, Delta Phi Epsilon Foreign Service Sorority, and while changing our name was one step of many in upholding our commitment to inclusivity, our organization is built on the same principle of the motto “latreuo” which is Greek for “I Serve”.

For more information, please visit our website at: https://etasocietygwu.com
summary: The Eta Society for Global Affairs is a professional organization dedicated to promoting leadership of our members in international affairs.

name: GW Men's Club Ultimate
description: The GW Men's Ultimate Frisbee Team is a club sport team at the George Washington University in Washington, D.C. The club practices 2 to 3 times a week and travels to 5-7 tournaments per semester. These tournaments are located across the country, including Atlanta, San Diego, Las Vegas, the Carolinas, and elsewhere. We are a very competitive team, with the goal of making Nationals every year! GW made an appearance at Nationals in 2004, and missed a 2005,2006 & 2017 berth by one game. Despite our high level of competition, we remain committed to spreading interest in the sport, and thus, we welcome all athletes who have prior experience as well as those who don't. We invite anyone who is willing to make the commitment to come to practice and get better week in and week out. We also field a less competitive team designed for those new to the sport, looking to improve on their skills to play on the next level, or just to have fun! You don't need to have any experience, all you need is the right attitude. You'll have fun, play hard and make some great friends.
summary: The GW Men's Ultimate Frisbee Team is a club sport team at the George Washington University in Washington, D.C. The club practices 2 to 3 times a week and travel to 5 to 7 tournaments per semester. These tournaments are located across the country, incl..

name: Chinese American Student Association
description: CASA, or the Chinese American Student Association, is a student organization that is dedicated to promoting Chinese culture throughout the GWU community and the DMV area via cultural, social, and professional events.
summary: All members of CASA must be students of the George Washington University. To be a member of CASA, a person must have filled out a membership form at one of the general body meetings (GBMs) or at student organization fair either in the beginning of the ...

name: Tourism For Tomorrow at GW
description: The mission of Tourism for Tomorrow is to further the educational and professional development of its members while fostering community among students, staff, alumni, and faculty as well as industry professionals, thought leaders, and trendsetters. We advocate on behalf of our members on issues concerning the tourism industry and The George Washington University School of Business Master of Science in Tourism, Hospitality, and Event Management (GWSB MSTHEM) program. TFT strives to cultivate future leaders in the tourism industry in the fields of sustainable tourism, hospitality, events and meetings, sports management, innovation and technology, and related fields.
summary: The mission of Tourism for Tomorrow is to further the educational and professional development of its members while fostering community among students, staff, alumni, and faculty as well as industry professionals, thought leaders, and trendsetters.

name: Club Sports Council
description: The purpose of the Club Sports Council is to represent club sports to the University and community and act as a united voice on their behalf to improve relations between teams and the University now and in the future. The CSC will allocate funds for all club sports on behalf of the SGA, and continuously evaluate the funding needs of teams throughout the year, approving additional funding when necessary.
summary: The purpose of the Club Sports Council is to represent club sports to the University and community and act as a united voice on their behalf. The CSC will allocate funds for all club sports on behalf of the SGA.

name: Kesem at GW
description: Kesem at The George Washington University is a non-profit organization completely run by passionate GW student leaders that provides a year-round support network and free summer camp for children coping with a parent's cancer in the DC Area. Through Kesem, our campers are given the opportunity to build their self-esteem, be surrounded by a peer support network, and learn they are not alone as they face their parent's cancer. Our flagship program, Camp Kesem, is a 1-week sleepaway camp in Maryland that takes place in August annually and is completely free of charge for the families we serve. If you are interested in getting involved, please email gw@kesem.org
summary: Kesem at The George Washington University is a non-profit organization run by passionate GW student leaders that provides a year-round support network and free summer camp for children in the DMV who are coping with a parent's cancer.

name: Public Health Student Association
description: The primary purpose of PHSA is to increase the value of public health students’ education and experiences through events that promote student involvement, facilitate professional development, and inspire a sense of community. The activities of PHSA will enhance the image of the Milken Institute School of Public Health. PHSA is a graduate umbrella student organization comprised of the students at the Milken Institute School of Public Health. PHSA will collaborate with the sub-organizations at GWSPH on a regular basis to ensure that objectives are aligned. As the Graduate Umbrella Organization of the Milken Institute School of Public Health, PHSA will serve concurrently as a free-standing student organization and as a supervisory organization to provide information, guidance, budgetary advice, co-sponsorships (as available), event promotion, and other services for all sub-organizations.
summary: The primary purpose of PHSA is to increase the value of public health students’ education and experiences through events that promote student involvement, facilitate professional development, and inspire a sense of community.

name: Trachtenberg Student Organization
description: The Trachtenberg Student Organization (TSO) is an organization created and run by graduate students to represent and advocate for the needs of Trachtenberg students. TSO is led by an executive board elected by the student body.
summary: The Trachtenberg Student Organization (TSO) is an organization created and run by graduate students to represent and advocate for the needs of Trachtenberg students.

name: GW Student Government Association
description: The GW Student Government Association (SGA) is the primary advocacy and allocation group for the student body at George Washington University. We represent all 27,000 graduate and undergraduate students on campus. The mission of the SGA is to further the interests and promote the welfare of all students at GW. Our responsibilities are defined in four ways: to advocate, allocate, advertise, and assist.
summary: The GW Student Government Association (SGA) is the primary advocacy and allocation group for the student body at The George Washington University. We represent all 26,000 graduate and undergraduate students on campus.

name: Real Estate Investment & Development Organization
description: REIDO was established to complement the Real Estate Concentration offered through the GW MBA program. It aims to further familiarize students who have a common interest in real estate investment and development with real estate issues and organizations in the Washington DC area. REIDO is also dedicated to providing students with relevant events throughout the academic year with which to enrich their academic experience at the business school, and serves as a link to GW alumni in the real estate industry. The club is open to all full or part time MBA students interested in real estate investment or development.
summary: REIDO was established to complement the Real Estate Concentration offered through the GW MBA program. It aims to further familiarize students who have a common interest in real estate investment and development with real estate issues and organizations...

name: Health Policy Student Association
description:
The purpose of the Health Policy Student Association (HPSA) is to increase the value of students' experiences in the Health Policy-related programs at GW through related social, educational, and career-oriented events that promote student involvement, professional development, and inspire a sense of community. HPSA is open to any GW graduate student interested in health policy!

For more information, check out our Facebook group!
summary: The purpose of the Health Policy Student Association is to increase the value of students' experiences in the Health Policy-related programs at GW through related social, educational, and career-oriented events that promote student experience.

name: The George Washington University Association for Computing Machinery Student Chapter
description: The GW student chapter of the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM), as a chartered local chapter of the National ACM, a network of 80,000+ computing professionals, exists to enrich the academic and extracurricular life of students interested in Computer Science. GW ACM is the primary CS community org at GW, hosting department-wide events like CS Game Nights, Professor Talks, Coffee with Professors, and the CS Freshman Welcome. Through multifaceted campus speakers and other educational and scientific opportunities, ACM seeks to increase the computing knowledge of GW students.
summary: GW ACM (Association of Computing Machinery) aims to bring the computer science community together through gatherings. We host department-wide events like CS Game Nights, Professor Talks, Coffee with Professors, and the CS Freshman Welcome.

name: Residence Hall Association
description: The GW Residence Hall Association is a group of passionate student leaders dedicated to making every residence hall feel like home. Our 24 elected Hall Councils build community by putting on fun, engaging events in their halls and advocating for the residential needs of their neighbors. Our Committee Corps focuses on large-scale events and campus-wide advocacy surrounding issues such as facilities, dining, safety, accessibility, and more. Our Executive Board works directly with campus leadership to improve the residential experience and bridge the gap between students and administration. Together, RHA works to build an inclusive campus experience where everyone feels welcome. To learn more about RHA, visit gwrha.com.
summary: RHA is a group of student leaders dedicated to making every residence hall feel like home. Our 24 elected Hall Councils build community by putting on fun, engaging events in their halls and advocating for the residential needs of their neighbors.

name: Graduate Marketing Club
description: The Graduate Marketing Club is an open forum for GWU students interested in the field of marketing. Our goal is to provide students with valuable opportunities to network, learn, and grow through active engagement with expert professionals in the field.
summary: The Graduate Marketing Club is an open forum for GWU students interested in the field of marketing. Our goal is to provide students with valuable opportunities to network, learn, and grow through active engagement with expert professionals in the field.

name: The George Washington Williams House
description: The George Washington Williams House is the medium for African American culture on the campus of The George Washington University.Our household holds several programming activities throughout the year that promotes service and community building. We participate in the Black Heritage Celebration Day of Service on an annual basis. Beyond participation, our house has even sponsored some of the previous events mentioned. Our household has also been known for promoting voting rights to local schools in the area by physical campus visits and voter registration drives.
summary: The George Washington Williams House is the medium for African American culture on the campus of The George Washington University.Our household holds several programming activities throughout the year that promotes service and community building. We pa...

name: Capitol Letters
description: Formerly known as Wooden Teeth, Capitol Letters is the oldest literary magazine on campus, founded in 1977. We accept poetry, prose, and art submissions every year from GW students, GW Online High School students, alumni, faculty, and staff—all members of the GW community. Every year, we publish the magazine digitally as well as print beautiful hard copies to share the GW community's talent and creativity. We disseminate them for free and donate a few to the library for our joint archival mission. Furthermore, throughout the year, we print smaller, more informal zines to foster continuous creativity and raise awareness for the magazine and our contributors.
summary: Capitol Letters is the oldest literary magazine on campus, founded 45 years ago. We accept poetry, prose, and art submissions every year, and print beautiful hard copies of the magazine to share the GW community's talent and creativity.

name: Mu Beta Chapter of Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc.
description: The Mu Beta Chapter of Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated was chartered on April 20th, 1975 on the campus of the George Washington University by nine dynamic women. It was the first Black greek lettered organization at the George Washington University. Founded on January 13, 1913, Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated was established by twenty-two collegiate women at Howard University. These students wanted to use their collective strength to promote academic excellence and to provide assistance to persons in need. The first public act performed by the Delta Founders involved their participation in the Women's Suffrage March (March 1913; Washington, D.C.). Delta Sigma Theta was incorporated in 1930, and the women of this organization have never strayed from the mission their founders created, which is now their legacy.
summary: Founded on January 13, 1913, Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated was established by twenty-two collegiate women at Howard University. These students wanted to use their collective strength to promote academic excellence and to provide assistance t...

name: Società di Cultura Italiana
description: The Societa' di Cultura Italiana is an organization for GW students that are interested in the Italian language and culture. We organize Italian film screenings, dinners at Italian restaurants like Il Canale, study abroad in Italy information sessions in coordination with the GW Study Abroad Office, Italian cultural events like Carnevale celebrations, and Italian-American events in coordination with the National Italian American Foundation. We also attend events at the Italian Embassy and provide GW students with information regarding internships and scholarships at the National Italian American Foundation, Casa Italiana, the Youth and Diplomacy Language Institute, the Department of State, and the Senate as well as House of Representatives. We also provide updates on Italian politics and resources to help students with their Italian studies like one-on-one tutoring through the GW Department of Romance, German & Slavic Languages & Literatures.
summary: The Societa' di Cultura Italiana (Italian Cultural Society) is an organization for GW students that are interested in the Italian language, history, and culture. Join us to celebrate, learn, and indulge in all things Italian!

name: Engineers Without Borders
description: Engineers Without Borders at The George Washington University is a collegiate, student-run organization that allies with communities at home and abroad to devise unique and sustainable solutions for underserved areas. Our goal is to empower partner communities to meet their basic needs and challenge our students to extend their engineering knowledge beyond the classroom. EWB at GWU is one of over 250 chapters worldwide with a commitment to making the world a better place, one calculus problem at a time. We currently operate programs in Bhutiya, India; Yumbe, Uganda; and in the DC Metro Area.
summary: Engineers Without Borders at The George Washington University is a collegiate, student-run organization that allies with communities at home and abroad to devise unique and sustainable solutions for underserved areas.

name: Allied in Pride
description: Through advocacy, social events, service, community, and support for lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, and queer/questioning students (LGBTQ+), Allied in Pride aims to build community and create a safe and welcoming environment for GW's LGBTQ+ population. Allied also works to raise awareness on LGBTQ+ issues at GW and in the DC Metro Community. We also seek to promote pro-LGBTQ+ policies at the university level.
summary: GW's largest LGBTQ+ social and advocacy organization

name: The George Washington University Naval Reserve Officers Training Corps Student Organization
description: Capital Battalion NROTC is a unique and exciting experience that adds military training, opportunities, and knowledge to the typical undergraduate program. The program is comprised of midshipmen and prior-enlisted Sailors and Marines from GW, Catholic University, Georgetown University, and Howard University.
summary: Capital Battalion NROTC is a unique and exciting experience that adds military training, opportunities, and knowledge to the typical undergraduate program. The program is comprised of midshipmen and prior-enlisted Sailors and Marines.

name: GW Black Graduate Business Association
description: The Black Graduate Business Association empowers historically underrepresented graduate business students through access to education, leadership development, and career advancement. We celebrate Black excellence, foster cross-disciplinary community, and create spaces for lasting impact in business and beyond. We welcome all allies who are committed to uplifting our mission and driving meaningful change.
summary: The Black GBA is committed to creating educational and professional growth opportunities for individuals historically underrepresented in business, supporting them as students, entrepreneurs, and professionals throughout their careers.

name: GW Women's Club Basketball
description: The George Washington University Women’s Basketball Club (GWWCB) was created to give women of the University the opportunity to play competitive basketball against teams outside of the GW community. The Women’s Club Basketball team serves to promote the value of teamwork, sportsmanship, and competition while also promoting the maintenance of an active lifestyle. Most importantly, GWWCB strives to provide a team and family-like atmosphere for all of its members.
summary: The George Washington University Women’s Basketball Club (Team) was created to give women of the University the opportunity to play competitive basketball against other universities' teams. This involves more commitment and skill level than intramurals.

name: GW Jazz Orchestra
description:
The GW Jazz Orchestra is The George Washington University’s premiere jazz ensemble, helping to bring the tradition of big band jazz music into GW’s Foggy Bottom campus. Founded in 2015, the band is entirely run by passionate students who work to perform a variety of genres and styles. The GWJO repertoire reflects the constantly evolving tastes of young musicians combined with a deep respect for great composers and arrangers across history. The band stands as a pillar of the music culture at GW, providing a unique experience for musicians and audiences alike through the big band format. The GW Jazz Orchestra continues to challenge its members and fans to expand their musical palates year after year. 

 

The GWJO performs on-campus and off-campus at every available opportunity. From showcase sets with the full group, to small combos providing light atmosphere at banquets and events, this band knows how to provide the right entertainment for any kind of venue.
summary: The GW Jazz Orchestra is the premier jazz ensemble at GWU, bringing the tradition of big-band jazz to campus. Founded in 2015, the band is entirely run by passionate students who work to perform a variety of genres and styles year-round.

name: National Residence Hall Honorary
description:
The Capital Chapter of the National Residence Hall Honorary (NRHH) represents the top 1% of students living in the residence halls at GW. Our membership is comprised of campus leaders, hall staff members, hall council members, residents, and individuals who have made a significant impact in the residence halls of Foggy Bottom and Mount Vernon. The primary mission of NRHH is recognition of the successes and achievements made in the residence halls. Each month, NRHH accepts nominations for "OTMs" (Of-The-Month Awards). These awards cover the programming and people that make the halls of GW an amazing place to live and learn. From recognition of Community Facilitators, Educational Programming, Community Service Activities, and a variety of other categories, NRHH truly represents leadership within our campus community.

 

Submit an OTM here: OTM Submission Form
summary: The Capital Chapter of the National Residence Hall Honorary (NRHH) represents up to the top 1% of students living in the residence halls at GW. We are campus leaders dedicated to service and recognition initiatives to improve the GW campus experience.

name: GW Alternative Breaks
description:
Alternative Breaks are substance-free, community service-learning experiences typically during the university's fall, winter, and spring breaks. 

Interested in Alternative Breaks? Check out our applications for Trip Leaders, Participants and Learning Partners- Learn More!

Our student-led program features service experiences that provide students the opportunity to become active citizen leaders while building lasting connections with people from various communities through a mutually-beneficial experience. Each experience has its own unique service area and pre-trip education meetings allowing students to become informed about the community and issue area with which they will be working.

Each service group will participate in educational meetings every other week in the fall and weekly meetings in the spring to discuss their specific issue area focus and how their community partners engage in the DC community. Additionally, all participants will learn about the basics of DC, racial justice, and the mission and vision of the Alternative Breaks program in their orientation programs.
summary: Alternative Breaks empowers and challenges students to understand their role as proponents of democratic citizenship through direct & indirect service, service-learning, and reflection, while encouraging growth, social awareness, and active citizenship.

name: Alpha Delta Pi
description: The oldest secret society in the world for women, Alpha Delta Pi is home to more than 265,000 women at 155 active collegiate chapters and more than 150 alumnae associations. As founder Eugenia Tucker Fitzgerald penned in 1851, ADPi remains dedicated to "the mental, moral, social, and domestic improvement of its members" as founding principals: Sisterhood + Service + Service Knowledge + Character.
summary: The oldest secret society in the world for women, Alpha Delta Pi is home to more than 235,000 women at 153 active collegiate chapters and more than 150 alumnae associations.As founder Eugenia Tucker Fitzgerald penned in 1851, ADPi remains dedicated to ...

name: AIESEC in Washington, DC
description: AIESEC in Washington, DC is the local chapter of the largest youth-led nonprofit in the world. We are a community of young people, passionately driven by one cause: peace and fulfillment of humankind’s potential. We do this by focusing on youth professional development through volunteer, intern, and teaching experiences to over 110 countries, enhancing cross-cultural understanding.
summary: AIESEC in Washington, DC is the local chapter of the largest youth-led nonprofit in the world. We focus on youth professional development, mainly by facilitating cross-cultural exchange experiences in over 110 countries.

name: Transfer Student Association
description: The Transfer Student Association is a student-led initiative dedicated to engaging transfer students at George Washington University academically, socially, and through extracurricular activities. Our organization serves as a central hub where transfer students can find community, access resources, and get involved in all aspects of the GW experience, helping them thrive across campus.
summary: The Transfer Student Association is a student-led initiative dedicated to engaging transfer students at George Washington University academically, socially, and through extracurricular activities.

name: The International Affairs Society
description: Founded in 1993, the International Affairs Society (IAS) is the largest non-partisan student organization on campus. We are dedicated to promoting a better understanding of international affairs as they concern the students of GWU. The IAS is largely composed of international affairs, political science, economics, and business majors but also has members from across GW's various schools with varying interests. Our student body spans from all corners of the globe as we embrace a truly international perspective.
summary: Founded in 1993, the International Affairs Society (IAS) is the largest non-partisan student organization on campus. We are dedicated to promoting a better understanding of international affairs as they concern the students of GWU. The IAS is largely c...

name: Campus Outreach
description: A student organization that seeks to glorify God by building laborers on the campus for the lost world. We seek to build laborers with a commitment to the Great Commission (Matthew 28:18-20) by helping students understand the gospel of Jesus Christ and training them to become mature followers of Christ. Our desire is then to see students leave college fully equipped to labor in the harvest field of a lost world for the glory of God.
summary: A student organization that seeks to glorify God by building laborers on the campus for the lost world. We seek to build laborers with a commitment to the Great Commission (Matthew 28:18-20) by helping students understand the gospel of Jesus Christ and...

name: Visiting Artists and Scholars Committee
description: VASC is a student organization run through the Fine Arts and Art History Department at GWU. Each semester, we invite artists or scholars from around the United States to speak about their current work. All events are free and open to the public, no RSVP is necessary. If you are interested in joining our committee please email vasc@gwu.edu for more information.
summary: VASC is an organization that brings artists and scholars to campus to deliver lectures or participate in panel discussions where they discuss their current work or debate relevant topics.

name: Student Government Association (Legislative Branch)
description: The Student Association Senate is comprised of over 42 Senators representing all of the different undergraduate and graduate schools at the George Washington University. The SA Executive Vice President serves as the Senate chairperson and presides over the meetings. The Senate is made up of four committees (Leadership, Finance, Academic Affairs, and Student Life) that are responsible for advocating on relevant issues. The Senate is also responsible for allocating over $1,000,000 to more than 400 student organizations.
summary: The Student Association Senate is comprised of over 30 Senators representing all of the different undergraduate and graduate schools at the George Washington University. The SA Executive Vice President serves as the Senate chairperson and presides over...

name: GW Program Board
description: GW Program Board plans the biggest and best events on campus. We are the only student organization that dedicates itself to enhancing the student experience and that caters to all of the GW community, connecting directly with students of all levels, alumni, families, friends, faculty, and staff. We are what happens!
summary: The George Washington University Program Board shall work with students, faculty, staff, and alumni in developing and implementing a broad cultural, intellectual, social, public service and recreational program for the University community

name: Delta Tau Delta
description: Social fraternity dedicated to lives based on Truth, Courage, Faith, and Power. Delta Tau Delta is the place for leaders to hone their skills, youths to grow, students to find support and guidance and men to find their paths to a life of excellence.
summary: Social fraternity dedicated to lives based on Truth, Courage, Faith, and Power.

name: GW Armenian Students Association
description:
The goal of the GW Armenian Students Association is to unite GW Armenian students and connect members of the Armenian heritage with one another.  We are all bound by our history and heritage and the Armenian community is a valuable relationship one can have in their life. This group enables unconditional acceptance for all Armenians and a strengthening of the GW Armenian community. 

The GW Armenian Students Association is committed to preserving and strengthening the Armenian community throughout the DMV area. It is necessary to collaborate with and form lasting relationships with our community members at other Armenian Student Associations in DC universities.
summary: We aim to unite GW Armenian Students and connect members of the Armenian heritage with one another.

name: George Washington Club Tennis
description: GW Club Tennis was founded in 2005. It is a coed team that competes at tournaments in and around the DMV. The club centers around players with a history of competitive play who want to continue developing their game throughout college. For more information, including registration for Spring 2026 tryouts, please see our Instagram page (@gwclubtennis).
summary: GW Club Tennis, founded in 2005 is a coed team with 35-45 members. The team practices four times a week and plays matches both home and away. Club Tennis is for those who played competitively and wish to continue to do so on a team.

name: Alpha Phi Omega
description: We're GW's only gender-inclusive community service fraternity, based in the principles of Leadership, Friendship, and Service. We do service on campus, across DC (at over 30 organizations in the DMV), and around the nation (including two service trips to nearby cities each year). We also host a variety of fellowship events to develop our bond as brothers as well as leadership events to promote leadership development within and outside the brotherhood. We are a chapter of the national Alpha Phi Omega fraternity.
summary: We're GW's only gender-inclusive community service fraternity, based in the principles of Leadership, Friendship, and Service. We do service on campus, across DC, the DMV area and around the nation.

name: Food Recovery Network
description:
The GW Food Recovery Network focuses on eliminating food waste and hunger by collecting foods that would otherwise be wasted from GW-related and external events and restaurants. That food is donated to shelters or redistribution centers in order to feed people who are in need. Members of FRN undergo food safety training and work together bring the salvaged food to its final destination. 

If you are interested in becoming a volunteer, WATCH THIS TRAINING VIDEO VIDEO  and COMPLETE THIS QUIZ! Once you are finished with this, send an email to gwufrn@gmail.com with your full name and a screenshot of your completed quiz. This is so we can verify that all of our volunteers have watched the video and are ready to safely recover food.
summary: The GW Food Recovery Network fights both food waste and hunger by collecting leftover perishable foods that would otherwise be wasted from GW events and donate it to shelters or redistribution centers.

name: Human & Organizational Learning Student Association
description: The Human & Organizational Learning (HOL) department at The George Washington University (GWU) consists of several academic programs ranging from certificates to master’s and doctorate degrees. Students’ opportunities for interacting with students from other academic programs within the department are limited. The HOL Student Association therefore strives to create opportunities for such interactions to occur, with an aim of creating a sense of community among the students of the various academic programs within the department of Human & Organizational Learning.
summary: The Human & Organizational Learning (HOL) department consists of several academic programs ranging from certificates to master’s and doctorate degrees. HOLSA creates opportunities for these programs to network and learn collectively.

name: GW MotherFunkers
description: The GW MotherFunkers, a competitive co-ed a cappella group, was founded in 2012 at The George Washington University with the desire to focus mainly on the performance of Contemporary Pop and Top 40s hits. We are proud to represent a diverse cross section of the GW student body, and look forward to many years of musical success on and off campus. Our group is characterized by our unique take on A Cappella arrangements and a redefining of what A Cappella music "should" sound like. We compete annually at the International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella (ICCA) and are always working to record and release our arrangements professionally on all streaming platforms.
summary: The GW MotherFunkers, a competitive co-ed A Cappella group, was founded in 2012 at The George Washington University with the desire to focus mainly on the performance of Contemporary Pop and the representation of diverse singers across campus!

name: GW Club Softball
description:
Our purpose is to give GW students who are not on the varsity softball team an outlet to play fastpitch softball! We provide an opportunity for students to get in some physical activity, work on their skills, meet new people, and be involved at GW.

 

We practice on Fridays and Sundays on Henry Bacon Ball Field or in Lerner. Feel free to stop by!
summary: Our purpose is to give GW students who are not on the varsity softball team an outlet to play softball! We provide an opportunity for students to get physical activity, work on their skills, meet new people, and be involved at GW.

name: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc. Mu Delta Chapter
description: The purpose of Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated® is to cultivate and encourage high scholastic and ethical standards, to promote unity and friendship among college women, to study and help alleviate problems concerning girls and women in order to improve their social stature, to maintain a progressive interest in college life, and to be of Service to All Mankind.
summary: The purpose of Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc. is to cultivate and encourage high scholastic and ethical standards, to promote unity and friendship among college women, to study and help alleviate problems concerning girls and women in order to improv...

name: GW Men's Club Basketball
description:
The Men's Club Basketball team is an organization committed to bringing together non-collegiate basketball players who wish to continue to compete at a high level against various club basketball teams across the nation.

https://youtu.be/xhyfL9V148I
summary: The Men's Club Basketball team is an organization committed to bringing together non-collegiate basketball players who wish to compete at a high level against various club basketball teams across the nation.

name: Medical Center Student Council
description: The Medical Center Student Council (MCSC) serves as an umbrella organization representing all School of Medicine and Health Sciences student groups in the GW Student Association. Responsibilities include supporting and distributing funding to student organizations, planning SMHS-wide activities such as Formal and Follies, and serving as a collective body to unite the MD, PA, and PT programs. The MCSC also serves as a united voice for SMHS-wide issues to GW University, GWU Hospital administration, and the GW community.
summary: The Medical Center Student Council (MCSC) serves as an umbrella organization representing all School of Medicine and Health Sciences student groups in the GW Student Association. Responsibilities include supporting and distributing funding to student o...

name: GW Badminton Club
description:
The GW Badminton Club enables students to play badminton on campus and organize weekly practices. The club organizes and participates in local tournaments to provide an outlet for competitive students, but is focused on providing those who are really passionate about badminton a group of equally interested individuals to play with. Players of any level are welcome and the club lends out rackets and shuttles to make practices as open and accessible as possible. The club is open to all the GW community and prides itself in fostering an inclusive community for all.

Groupme : https://groupme.com/join_group/71332381/Hz6BOAPL
summary: Encourage students to play badminton on campus, also to provide those who are really passionate about badminton a group of equally interested individuals to play with.

name: The George Washington University Chinese Students and Scholars Association
description: The GWU Chinese Students and Scholars Association (CSSA) is a student organization dedicated to the goal of promoting social, intellectual and cultural activities for Chinese students and scholars at GWU as well as other interested members in the GWU community. It will also strive to provide opportunities and benefits for its members by facilitating the exchange of information between China and Chinese students studying abroad. GWUCSSA encourages all (especially newcoming) Chinese students and scholars in GWU to get involved in organizing CSSA events and to join our organization. Chinese Students and Scholars Association is an officially registered student organization of the George Washington University. Founded in 2004, GWUCSSA is one of the largest and most active student organizations at the George Washington University, now with nearly 1,000 members who are mainly GWU students, scholars, faculty and staff members originally from China. As a home to GWU's Chinese community, GWUCSSA is dedicated to the goal of promoting social, intellectual, and cultural activities for Chinese students and scholars on GWU. Currently, GWUCSSA has been an influential force in sponsoring and organizing activities on the George Washington University campus and in the Chinese community of Washington D.C. area. GWUCSSA committee is also striving to provide opportunities and benefits for its members by facilitating the exchange of information from social, cultural, and education interaction among students of all backgrounds.
summary: The GWU Chinese Students and Scholars Association (CSSA) is a student organization dedicated to the goal of promoting social, intellectual and cultural activities for Chinese students and scholars at GWU as well as other interested members in GWU.

name: TEDxFoggyBottom
description: TEDxFoggyBottom brings together some of the world’s incredible innovators, thinkers and doers to inspire and collaborate with the Foggy Bottom community. It is an independently organized TED conference run by students. TEDxFoggyBottom features presenters as well as different interactive experiences outside the theater, altogether creating a full-day riveting experience.
summary: TEDxFoggyBottom brings together some of the world’s incredible innovators, thinkers and doers to inspire and collaborate with the Foggy Bottom community. It is an independently organized TED conference run by students.

name: GW Sirens
description: Founded in 2002, The GW Sirens are a treble voice acapella group at The George Washington University bonded by their love of music and each other! The Sirens arrange all of their own music, singing covers from a wide variety of genres, and are passionate about recording and releasing music to uplift the GW community. Check out their most recent EP "Fall From Grace" on all streaming platforms now, and follow them on instagram @gwsirens to stay up to date on all performances and fundraising events!
summary: Founded in 2002, The GW Sirens are a soprano-alto acapella group at The George Washington University bonded by their love of music and each other! The Sirens arrange all of their own music, singing working from a wide variety of genres!

name: Alpha Delta Phi Society
description:
•  

 

  •  
  •  
  •  
  •  

The Alpha Delta Phi Society is a gender-inclusive literary and arts society dedicated to developing character, scholarship, and enduring friendships. Our mission is to bring together a diverse and supportive community, foster intellectual and personal growth, and cultivate passionate individuals of good character and integrity. At our core, we value literature and the arts in the broadest sense of the terms; we're writers, artists, critical thinkers, gamers, movie-watchers, pet-lovers, and we're kind of nerdy. As a living, breathing organization rooted in a tradition of shared values, we proudly redefine what it means to be a Greek-letter organization.

The Alpha Delta Phi Society, formed by a group of chapters that withdrew from the Alpha Delta Phi Fraternity in 1992, is an independent and legally separate gender-inclusive organization not affiliated with the Fraternity.

To contact us please dm our instagram (@capitaladps)
summary: The Alpha Delta Phi Society Capital Chapter is a gender-inclusive social organization. We have a strong literary and artistic tradition that incorporates a diverse array of interests to help form a community and family at GW.

name: GW Panhellenic Association
description: The Panhellenic Association is comprised of ten chapters which make up more than 1,000 members. Together we represent almost 12% of the undergraduate woman-identifying population at GW. Panhellenic is the governing board of the National Panhellenic Conference sororities at GW and serves as a vital communication link between the Panhellenic chapters on campus. The Panhellenic Association seeks to provide a fruitful Greek experience to GWU students. We work to ensure that opportunities are being provided and that chapters are being acknowledged for their service to the community. Panhellenic helps member chapters realize their individual goals and unites them under the halo of Fraternity and Sorority Life. If you have any questions, are interested in partnering with us, or are considering participating in formal recruitment, please contact us at panhel@gwu.edu.
summary: The Panhellenic Association is comprised of ten chapters and one associate chapter which make up more than 1,600 women. Panhellenic is the governing board of the National Panhellenic Conference sororities at GW.

name: GW Table Tennis
description:
The George Washington University Table Tennis Club strives to unite members of the GW community through the sport of table tennis. You need not be competitive to enjoy the sport! The club is both a social and athletic club, and seeks to promote diversity, good sportsmanship, and a healthy and active lifestyle. The club also seeks to foster community bonds by engaging GW students and those in our surrounding community through social engagements and service activities. The club is associated with the National Collegiate Table Tennis Association and members can participate in tournaments including Divisionals, Regionals, and National tournaments.

 

Follow our instagram @gw_table_tennis and join the group me Here for the most frequent updates, including practice times and competitive team tryouts!
summary: The George Washington University Table Tennis Club strives to unite members of the GW community through the sport of table tennis. You need not be competitive to enjoy the sport! The club is both a social and athletic club, and seeks to promote diversity

name: Society of Women Engineers
description: SWE is an organization established to promote opportunities for women in engineering by means of professional development and outreach. In addition to general body meetings, we attend a national conference each year, and hold various other networking and professional events to allow students to have a chance to reach out and make connections.
