summary: SWE is an organization developed to promote opportunities for women in engineering by means of professional development and outreach. We attend national/local conference(s) each year, and hold various other networking activities. We also hold GBMs.

name: The George Washington University Intercollegiate Equestrian Team
description: The George Washington University Intercollegiate Equestrian Team is an organization dedicated to fostering undergraduate students’ interests in horses, horsemanship, and formal intercollegiate competition. The team encourages independent growth for all its members and promotes the development of leadership skills in a supportive atmosphere.
summary: The George Washington University Intercollegiate Equestrian Team- GW Club Equestrian

name: GW Pitches
description: In 1996, The GW Pitches became the first all-treble a cappella group at The George Washington University. The GW Pitches is an entirely student run organization composed of 15-20 vocally diverse singers from year-to-year. From Boygenius to FLO to SZA to Kali Uchis, our members arrange an eclectic mix of songs and medleys. The group holds rehearsals twice per week and organizes performances on a monthly basis. The GW Pitches give a final concert at the end of each semester, and take part in the two largest a cappella extravaganza performances at the university – Acapalooza in the fall and Battle of the A Cappellas in the spring. We have also had the privilege over the years to compete in the Quarterfinals of the International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella, which is always an excellent experience.
summary: In 1996, The GW Pitches became the first all-treble a cappella group at The George Washington University. The GW Pitches is an entirely student-run organization composed of 15-20 vocally diverse singers. We personally arrange all our own repertoire.

name: Strategic Crisis Simulations
description:
Strategic Crisis Simulations is a student-run organization at the George Washington University affiliated with the Elliott School of International Affairs. Our organization focuses on providing interactive and engaging simulations on issues such as U.S. foreign policy, diplomacy, security, intelligence, emergency management, and humanitarian assistance. 

 

Founded in Spring 2009, SCS offers GW students interested in international affairs the opportunity to broaden their knowledge base, develop marketable skills for future employment, and build a network of mentors in the foreign policy and national security fields. 

​

SCS works with institutional partners in the GW and DC community to develop simulations. In the past, we have collaborated with NATO PDD, the Elliott School's Leadership, Ethics, and Practice Initiative, Space Policy Institute, and Capital Battalion ROTC. SCS is also the recipient of the George Washington University's 2018 Center for Student Engagement Learning Through Action Award.
summary: Strategic Crisis Simulations is a student organization affiliated with the Elliott School of International Affairs that facilitates realistic wargaming and crisis management simulations for the GW community.

name: George Washington Men's Lacrosse
description: GW Men's Lacrosse is a competitive team in the National College Lacrosse League Division 2.
summary: GW Men's Lacrosse is a competitive team in the Men's Collegiate Lacrosse Association.

name: Sigma Psi Zeta Sorority, Inc.
description: Sigma Psi Zeta is a multi-cultural, Asian-Interest Greek organization. We are a cultural, social, educational and community service oriented sorority. We were founded in SUNY Albany on March 23rd, 1994, and have over 43 sisterships across the nation. Sigma Psi Zeta was founded on the pillars of integrity, legacy, scholarship, professionalism, philanthropy, sisterhood, service, culture, community, citizenship, and activism. We are the first to be established on the East Coast, and the Pi Chapter is the first Asian-Interest Sorority to charter at The George Washington University.
summary: Sigma Psi Zeta is a multi-cultural, Asian-Interest Greek organization. We are a cultural, social, educational and community service oriented sorority. We were founded in SUNY Albany on March 23rd, 1994, and have over 32 Sisterships across the nation.

name: Emergency Medical Response Group (GW EMS)
description:
The Emergency Medical Response Group provides emergency medical services to The George Washington University community as a student-run volunteer service. As Nationally Registered EMTs, we strive to be available 24/7 during the academic year to respond to all medical emergencies on campus. EMeRG will continue to provide superior enhanced basic life support to all members of the GW community, on both the Foggy Bottom and Mount Vernon campuses, free of charge.

For those interested in joining EMeRG, we have an application process that opens every Spring and Fall! Keep a look out for information on our instagram @gwemerg regarding eligibility and dates to apply!
summary: The Emergency Medical Response Group will continue to provide services to The George Washington University community as a student-run volunteer service. We strive to be available 24/7 during the academic year to respond to all medical emergencies.

name: Phi Alpha Theta History Honor Society, Phi Chi Chapter
description:
We are a professional society whose mission is to promote the study of history by the students of The George Washington University through encouraging scholastic excellence in all members; preparing members for the rigors of graduate study, promoting mentor relationships with faculty members, and fostering awareness across the local area of historically significant events and issues.

We plan to host many fun events through the school year, including (but not limited to) movie nights, field trips, study hours, trivia, and tours of the DC area!
summary: We are a professional society whose mission is to promote the study of history by the students of The George Washington University through encouraging scholastic excellence in all members; preparing members for the rigors of graduate study, promoting m...

name: Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu
description:
GW Club Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu is open to anyone interested in learning about the sport of Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu. Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu is a martial art and self-defense system that focuses on grappling, or ground fighting. Club members will learn basic fundamentals and be able to practice and learn in a safe and instructive environment. Beginners are welcome! Practice T/Th:7-9PM, Open Mat Sun: 1-3PM. 

Check out our IG: https://www.instagram.com/gw_bjj

Join our GroupMe for updates: GW BJJ
summary: Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu is a martial art and self-defense system that focuses on grappling, or ground fighting. Club members will learn basic fundamentals and be able to practice and learn in a safe and instructive environment. All skill levels are welcome!

name: Theta Chapter of Latinas Promoviendo Comunidad/ Lambda Pi Chi Sorority, Inc.
description:
The first Latina focused sorority founded at an Ivy-league institution, Latinas Promoviendo Comunidad/ Lambda Pi Chi Sorority, Inc. was established on April 16, 1988 at Cornell University. The Sorority Founders strove to establish an organization that would transcend their collegiate experience and consolidate their strength as responsible leaders of the community. They worked diligently to develop an organization whose mission and goals would encompass the experiences of all women, but especially Latinas.

The Theta Chapter of Latinas Promoviendo Comunidad/ Lambda Pi Chi Sorority, Inc. was established December 6, 1997. It is a Latina-based but not Latina-exclusive sorority that represents a tradition of strong, proud women committed to the three ideals upon which our organization's foundation stands - La Comunidad, La Cultura Latina, and La Hermandad. Las Hermanas strive to promote progress and development in their communities. They highlight the contributions of women and people of color with an emphasis on the Latina and other opportunities for academic and professional advancement to members of historically under-represented groups.
summary: The Theta Chapter of Latinas Promoviendo Comunidad/ Lambda Pi Chi Sorority, Inc. was established December 6, 1997. It is a Latina based but not Latina exclusive sorority that represents a tradition of strong, proud women committed to the three ideals...

name: [insert here] improv
description: Have you previously done improv? Do you want to start learning improv? Does your mom at least tell you that you are funny? Well, look no further, as the only all-improv comedy group at GW, [insert here] focuses on performing and practicing improvisational comedy to not only develop the improv skills of the group's members, but to also entertain spectators at our monthly improvisational comedy performances. [insert here] focuses on reinforcing the building blocks of improv in our performances, while creating funny scenes and strong performances. Follow us on instagram at @inserthereimprov to keep up with the group and feel free to email or DM us with any questions!
summary: The purpose of [insert here] is to perform and practice improvisational comedy to further develop the skills of the students in [insert here] and to entertain spectators with improvisational comedy performances.

name: Theta Tau Engineering Fraternity
description:
Theta Tau is the oldest, largest, and foremost coed Fraternity for Engineers. Since its founding at the University of Minnesota in 1904, over 40,000 have been initiated over the years. With emphasis on quality and a strong fraternal bond, the Fraternity has chapters only at ABET accredited schools.

For our Connections

We forge lifelong bonds of friendship, a journey that develops and delivers a network of lasting personal and professional relationships. We foster an inviting, safe, and social environment in which our members become lifelong friends.

For our Profession

We develop and nurture engineers with strong communication, problem-solving, collaboration, and leadership skills that we demonstrate in our profession, our community, and in our lives.

For our Communities

We are known for our service to our college, university and the larger community. Our service projects create a unifying environment for learning and personal growth for our members.

The purpose of Theta Tau is to develop and maintain a high standard of professional interest among its members, and to unite them in a strong bond of fraternal fellowship.

“Whatsoever thy hand findeth to do, do it with thy might;…”
summary: Theta Tau is a professional engineering coed fraternity which supports a culture of professional development, community service and close-knit brotherhood.

name: Kappa Delta
description: Kappa Delta Sorority was founded in 1897 in Farmville, VA. Today, Kappa Delta has more than 300,000 initiated members, 168 collegiate chapters, and over 200 alumnae chapters. Our values are friendship & loyalty, personal integrity, lifelong learning, and selfless service.
summary: Kappa Delta Sorority was founded in 1897 in Farmville, VA. Today, Kappa Delta has more than 300,000 initiated members, 168 collegiate chapters, and over 200 alumnae chapters.

name: GW Club Field Hockey Team
description: We, the GW Field Hockey Team, gather to play field hockey competitively within the National Field Hockey League (NFHL). We are a co-ed team and accept all levels of skill! Follow us on Instagram at @gw.fieldhockey to stay updated on our season!
summary: We, the GW Field Hockey Team, gather to play field hockey competitively within the National Field Hockey League (NFHL). We are a co-ed team and accept all levels of skill. Follow us on Instagram at @gw.fieldhockey to stay updated on our season!

name: District K Dance Team
description:
District K is a k-pop/hiphop dance team that aims to provide a community for dancers of all experience levels with interests in k-pop music and/or hiphop dance styles. 

Throughout the year, we film covers of k-pop songs and various studio choreographies (1million, Millennium Dance Complex, Just Jerk Dance, etc.) that we post on our Youtube channel GWU District K. You can also catch us performing at various events on campus, such as our annual Spring Showcase, and elsewhere across the DMV!

At auditions, which we hold at the beginning of every semester, we teach two short k-pop choreographies -- No prior dance experience or preparation is required to audition or be accepted into the org! We also have a myriad of non-dancer positions in the organization, such as publicity, videography, and editing. Please email us if interested: gwu.districtk@gmail.com

For more information on auditions, performances, covers, and events, check out our Instagram.
summary: District K is a k-pop/hip-hop dance team that aims to provide a community for dancers interested in k-pop music and/or hiphop dance styles. We regularly perform and film dance covers to upload on YouTube. No prior dance experience required to audition.

name: The Engineers' Council
description: The Engineers’ Council is a SEAS student umbrella organization that oversees and facilitates collaboration between SEAS student organizations, and among the general student body within SEAS.
summary: The Engineers’ Council is a SEAS student umbrella organization that oversees and facilitates collaboration between SEAS student organizations, and among the general student body within SEAS.

name: WRGW District Radio
description: Located in the heart of Washington DC, WRGW District Radio combines the benefits of professional broadcasting studios with those of a learning environment. Our mission is to provide The George Washington University with high-quality music, sports, news, and talk radio programming, while also producing live special events. WRGW is a community station dedicated to programming and supporting the local music community, new artists, and an eclectic variety of music, news, talk, and sports content. We do so by channeling our members' creativity and giving them the freedom to create their own unique and professional on-air content. Visit our website gwradio.com to learn more.
summary: WRGW District Radio is GW's official student-run radio station, broadcasting independent programming every day in our Music, Sports, News, and Talk departments.

name: The Muslim Students' Association
description: The purpose of the MSA is to serve the best interest of Muslims as members of the GW community. To this end, the MSA shall: promote unity and joint action among Muslim students; conduct social, cultural, religious, and other activities in accordance with Islamic values and teachings; arrange congregational prayers and Islamic religious festivals at appropriate times; create opportunities for Islamic education; and foster friendly and meaningful relationships with other student organizations to promote intersectional leadership, advocacy, and solidarity on campus. "Ours is not a caravan of despair, but a Caravan of Hope. Come-- even if you've broken your vows a thousand times. Come, yet again." (Rumi)
summary: The purpose of the MSA is to serve the best interest of Muslims as members of the GW community. To this end, the MSA shall: promote unity and joint action among Muslim students; conduct social, cultural, religious, and other activities.

name: GW Men's Club Volleyball
description: The Men's Club Volleyball team is composed of students from the George Washington University who like to play competitive volleyball. This club plays throughout the mid-Atlantic region in weekend tournaments. The club plays under the rules of the ECVA and attends the NCVF Collegiate Nationals every year.
summary: The Men's Club Volleyball team is a community for students who like to play competitive volleyball. As a member of the ECVA, we play in competitive tournaments across the region and frequent DC's beach courts.

name: The Undergraduate GW Moot Court Team
description: By utilizing real Supreme Court precedent, students learn how to critically analyze case law to develop argument presentations that mimic the oral arguments before the Supreme Court. The GW Moot Court team will develop their own argument that answers the distinct constitutional issues which are provided by the AMCA in the case packet each year.
summary: The GW Undergraduate Moot Court Team focuses on developing skills for Supreme Court style appellate advocacy and seeks to inspire interest in Constitutional law.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers - GWU
description: GW-SHPE empowers students, of all races and majors, to develop professionally and academically in addition to maintaining the social element of Latinx/Hispanic culture. We offer our members countless opportunities to develop as students, professionals, and leaders each day.
summary: GW-SHPE empowers students, of all races and majors, to develop professionally and academically in addition to maintaining the social element of Hispanic culture. We offer our members countless opportunities to develop as students and professionals.

name: The GroW Community
description: The GroW Community seeks to educate, unite and mobilize students of The George Washington University and community members of Washington, DC, to restore the environment, promote community, build relationships, and pursue justice through food for a local and sustainable food system. Through the GroW Community Garden we offer students and community members a place to learn about native, organic food systems by providing service opportunities in sustainable gardening, food distribution, and community outreach. We also raise awareness through education and advocacy around issues of local food production, environmental justice, and developing a strong local economy. To learn how to become involved with our organization and the GroW Garden that our GroW Community maintains, please email gwgardenmanager@gmail.com. Follow us: GroW Community Facebook page (https://www.facebook.com/TheGroWCommunity/?ref=bookmarks) GroW Community Garden page (https://www.facebook.com/GroW-Community-Garden-140439647547/) Instagram @thegrowgarden for other updates.
summary: The GroW Community seeks to educate, unite and mobilize students of The George Washington University and community members of Washington, DC, to restore the environment, promote community, build relationships, and pursue justice through food for a loca...

name: GW Club Golf
description:
This is a club that welcomes all students of George Washington University who want to casually or competitively play golf at the intercollegiate level. The team holds weekly practices at a local driving range and outings to play rounds at local courses. GW Club Golf also has a tournament team for golfers of all skill levels to try their hand at competition. Both A and B teams compete at one or two tournaments a semester, hosted by NCCGA.

https://groupme.com/join_group/107434700/5d3UR8c1
summary: This is a club that welcomes all students of George Washington University who want to casually play golf at the intercollegiate level. The team holds weekly practices at a local driving range and outings to play rounds at local courses

name: Kappa Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc. - Alpha Beta Chapter
description: We are the Alpha Beta Chapter of Kappa Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc. located at The George Washington University. Kappa Phi Lambda is an Asian-interest, but not Asian-exclusive sorority upholding our three pillars of Sisterhood, Service, and Cultural Diversity. With over 4,000 Sisters all over the world, the Kappa Sisterhood can be found anywhere you go.
summary: Kappa Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc. is an Asian-interest, not Asian-exclusive sorority. Our pillars are Sisterhood, Service, and Cultural Diversity.

name: The Black Men's Initiative
description: The Black Men's Initiative seeks to proactively mentor, support, and most importantly uplift all under-represented male students to ensure that the men you entered GWU with are the same men you exit GW with, degree in hand and head held high. Through our programming and collaborative efforts we are here to advocate for you and lift you up as we all climb. Our core values are brotherhood, academic excellence and servant leadership.
summary: The Black Men's Initiative seeks to proactively mentor, support, and most importantly uplift all under-represented male students to ensure that the men you entered GWU with are the same men you exit GW with, degree in hand and head held high. Through o...

name: The Graduate School of Education and Human Development Student Council
description:
The purpose of the GSEHD Student Council is to:

Promote collaboration among student associations within GSEHD

Foster coordination with the greater graduate student community at GW

Assist GSEHD student organizations with the GW registration and budget process to ensure compliance and adequate funding, and

Serve as a liaison between the GSEHD student body and the Dean's office to address concerns that may arise, including academic, extracurricular, and administrative.

Further, the GSEHD Student Council shall act as an umbrella organization for the school's student organizations providing student organization support.
summary: The purpose of the GSEHD Student Council is to: Promote collaboration among student associations within GSEHD-Foster coordination with the greater graduate student community at GW-Assist GSEHD student organizations with the GW registration and budg.

name: Xi Sigma Chapter of Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Incorporated
description: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Incorporated is a community conscious, action-oriented organization with a mission to foster the ideas of Service, Charity, Scholarship, Civic and Cultural endeavors, Sisterhood and Finer Womanhood.
summary: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Incorporated is a community conscious, action-oriented organization with a mission to foster the ideas of Service, Charity, Scholarship, Civic and Cultural endeavors, Sisterhood and Finer Womanhood.

name: Asian American Student Association
description:
Mission

Asian American Student Association is a student-led organization dedicated to educating our peers and community about the Asian American identity. This group was founded to create a safe and inclusive space for members and allies of our community. These spaces are intended for important conversations surrounding issues prevalent in the Asian American community. We aim to educate, advocate for, and empower our peers within GW.

AASA is constantly looking to interrogate what it means to be Asian American as we understand that this label encompasses many identities and experiences. AASA does not look to define what it means to be Asian American but looks to create a space where students may discuss this question themselves. 

 

AASA's Vision

  • Teach our community and peers about Asian American identities and interrogate what it means to be Asian American
    • Where do we fit into the greater picture of people of color?
    • Create a safe space to have uncomfortable discussions that encourage people to question their biases and grow
  • Create a safe and radical community for Asian Americans on campus; cultivate a sense of unity among different Asian American ethnicities
  • Learn from and stand in solidarity with other marginalized communities on campus in the fight against marginalization and towards liberation
    • This includes being staunchly against but not limited to, transphobia, queerphobia, sexism, racism, anti-Blackness, capitalism, imperialism, colonialism, ableism, Zionism, caste discrimination, religious discrimination, colorism, classism, xenophobia, and any other oppressive systems
    • Empower our own community and others
summary: Asian American Student Association is a student organization at the George Washington University that aims to cultivate an environment of understanding within GWU's Asian American student population through educational and cultural events.

name: Forensic Student Guild
description: The goals of the FSG are to expose students to agencies and individuals with professional experiences in the field, to inform students of the many opportunities available in the various disciplines of forensic science, to establish opportunities for students to network with professionals, academic authorities, other students, graduates, and the community. The FSG has the potential to aid students in researching and obtaining careers and to foster a general interest in forensic science. FSG events will include speaker presentations and social events.
summary: The goals of the FSG are to expose students to agencies and individuals in the field, to inform students of available opportunities in the various disciplines of forensic science, and to establish a place for students in all disciplines to interact.

name: GWU Red Cross
description: The George Washington University Red Cross Chapter (GWU Red Cross) will practice and exemplify humanitarian values supported by the American Red Cross. The humanitarian mission of the American Red Cross connects us to people and communities across the nation and around the world. The common bonds of humanity and compassion unite us together, not just in the face of emergencies and disasters, but in helping our neighbors every day. The main mission of the organization is: providing assistance for those in need; empowering local communities to prevent, prepare for and respond to emergencies; and collecting and distributing critical supply for hospitals throughout the country. By creating a community of students interested in volunteer opportunities, our organization hopes to help fulfill the missions of the American Red Cross. No matter how small our contributions are.
summary: The George Washington University Red Cross Club will practice and exemplify humanitarian values supported by the American Red Cross. The humanitarian mission of the American Red Cross connects us to people and communities across the nation.

name: Indian Students' Association
description: The Indian Students' Association is an organization dedicated to preserving and promoting Indian culture in the GW community and neighboring areas. As one of the largest student organizations on campus, we have maintained a tradition of excellence for many years through our various events that bring about awareness to Indian and non-Indian students alike. As always, ISA has made every effort to support the ideas of our members to provide them with a forum to express their ideas and opinions. Through our activities, community service and educational programs, we have been able to share with so many people the diversity of Indian culture.
summary: The Indian Students' Association is an organization dedicated to preserving and promoting Indian culture in the GW community and neighboring areas. As one of the largest student organizations on campus, we have maintained a tradition of excellence for ...

name: GW Women's Rugby Football Club
description:
Founded in 1996, GWWRFC is GWU's premiere D2 women's+ rugby club. We are a vibrant and inclusive community for interested athletes of all levels! 

Our team typically practices Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays with competitive matches on Saturdays. 

If you are interested in joining our team, please contact gwwrfcrookies@gmail.com or @gwwrfc on instagram!

To donate click the link here!
summary: GWWRFC is an inclusive women's club rugby team on campus

name: Undergraduate Commuters Student Association
description: The CSA aims to work with The George Washington University to create a manageable and comfortable environment for undergraduate commuting students. It seeks to create a community where commuting students can interact with one another, while supporting each other through their commuter experience. It aims to work alongside the university to provide students with transportation, storage, and safety resources. It seeks to cooperate with the university to provide commuter students with a place in The George Washington University community.
summary: The CSA works with The George Washington University to create a comfortable environment and close-knit community for fellow undergraduate commuters to connect with and support each other.

name: Global Brigades GW
description:
GLOBAL BRIGADES MISSION: To empower volunteers and under-resourced communities to resolve global health and economic disparities and inspire all involved to collaboratively work towards an equal world.

 

GLOBAL BRIGADES VISION: To improve equality of life, by igniting the largest student-led social responsibility movement on the planet.

 

YOUR BENEFITS: Contribute to sustainable change - Provide a community access to healthcare - Education application - Intake & triage of patients - Shadow doctors - Lead preventative healthcare workshops - Participate in clinic pharmacy - Spanish language immersion - International development experience

 

COMMUNITY BENEFITS: Empowerment - Students empower communities - Reinforce communities’ commitments to their health- Access to healthcare - Access to public health projects- Programs are offered in Honduras, Panama, Guatemala, Greece, and Ghana
summary: Global Brigades is the largest student-led movement for global health and holistic development. We volunteer and work alongside local communities in Central America to implement sustainable health systems.

name: GW Club Baseball
description: The GW Club Baseball team (River Horse Baseball) participates in the National Club Baseball Association Division III, District II South. We provide the opportunity for baseball players who have chosen not to participate in the Varsity Baseball program to continue to participate in an organized baseball program. We have been ranked #1 in the country twice.
summary: The GW Club Baseball team participates in the NCBA Division 2 Chesapeake South Division. We provide the opportunity for baseball players who have chosen not to participate in the Varsity Baseball program to continue to participate in organized baseball.

name: JKA Karate Club
description: JKA (the Japan Karate Association) was founded in 1955 with Gichin Funakoshi as its chief instructor. Sensei Funakoshi is considered the father of modern karate, after having brought the art from Okinawa to Japan. JKA Karate is distinguished from other martial arts by the linearity and strength of its punches, blocks, and kicks. Precise techniques, accompanied by mastery and focus of energy flows and a deep knowledge of the body's vital points, make this karate style a comprehensive system for self-defense and combat. The JKA Karate Club of GWU was founded in 2002 and our chief instructor is Sensei Kenichi Haramoto, a 7th-degree black belt from Japan who began his training in 1962. In 1966, he participated in the All Japan Collegiate Karate championship Tournament and took first place together with his Aoyama Gakuin University Karate Club men's kumite team.
summary: JKA (the Japan Karate Association) was founded in 1955 with Gichin Funakoshi as its chief instructor. Sensei Funakoshi is considered the father of modern karate, after having brought the art from Okinawa to Japan. JKA Karate is distinguished from other..

name: GW Arab Student Association
description: The Arab Student Association (ASA) is an unaffiliated, non-profit, student-run organization that aims to promote an understanding of the Arab world, its culture, and issues that affect the region. Through actively planning a variety of social, cultural, and political events, the ASA strives for the atrophy of stereotypes and the growth of a sincere representation of the Arab world and its diversity on campus. The ASA aims to serve in the best interests of the Arab and Arab-American students on campus.
summary: The Arab Student Association (ASA) is an unaffiliated, non-profit, student-run organization that aims to promote an understanding of the Arab world, its culture, and issues that affect the region. Through actively planning a variety of social, cultural...

name: George Washington University Singers
description:
Welcome! We are the GW University Singers, a mixed choir of GW undergraduate and graduate students!

 

We're a non-audition-based choir catering to a variety of skill levels, aiming to foster community among the student body through music and art. We are a semester-based choir that meets once a week for a couple of hours, preparing for our concerts! 

 

University Singers meets Wednesday nights from 7:15 - 9:45 and performs a few times through the semester. It is conducted by professors Erin Freeman, Jeff Kempskie, and Julie Tucker and organized by a student leadership team.

 

In past years, we've gone on tour overseas, with destinations including the Baltic, South America, Romania, Croatia, Slovenia, Italy, and South Africa. In the DMV area, we've had the opportunity to sing at amazing venues like the Washington National Cathedral, Strathmore Concert Hall, Schlesinger Performing Arts Center, and The Kennedy Center Concert Hall.

 

To join us, register for the course on Banweb (University Singers) or email cmus@gwu.edu
summary: The GW University Singers seeks to foster a love of choral music in its members, sharing that gift with the wider GW and DMV Community.

name: GW Garmi
description: GW Garmi (formerly GW Naach) is a vibrant South Asian dance team. With a passion for blending traditional and modern dance forms, GW Garmi stands out for its unique approach to dance. We blend traditional and contemporary styles, combining the vibrancy of Bollywood with the grace of classical Indian forms like Bharatnatyam and Kathak, and the high energy of Hip Hop along with other styles. Our performances celebrate South Asian heritage while pushing creative boundaries to create innovative, captivating shows.
summary: GW Garmi (formerly GW Naach) is a co-ed Bollywood fusion dance team that brings together Bollywood, Indian classical dance, Hip Hop, contemporary, and more to create a captivating and diverse performance style.

name: Chi Omega Fraternity: The Phi Alpha Chapter
description:
We are one of the largest FSL organizations in the United States, which offers unique networking and alumnae opportunities.

Chi Omega is an intergenerational organization forever committed to our founding purposes of:

1. friendship

2. personal integrity

3. service to others

4. academic excellence & intellectual pursuits

5. community & campus involvement

6. personal & career development.

 

THE CHI OMEGA SYMPHONY

"To live constantly above snobbery of word or deed; to place scholarship before social obligations and character before appearances; to be in the best sense, democratic rather than “exclusive”, and lovable rather than “popular”; to work earnestly, to speak kindly, to act sincerely, to choose thoughtfully that course which occasion and conscience demand; to be womanly always; to be discouraged never; in a word, to be loyal under any and all circumstances to my Fraternity and her highest teachings and to have her welfare ever at heart that she may be a symphony of high purpose and helpfulness in which there is no discordant note." -Written by Ethel Switzer Howard, Xi Chapter, 1904
summary: GW's chapter of the Chi Omega Fraternity.

name: Education Policy Student Association
description: The Association's purpose is to be a forum for interaction among Education Policy students, alums, and faculty as well as the broader GW and DC policy community to foster social, academic, and professional development activities. The association provides an opportunity for professional growth, social interaction and networking among its members. The goal is to make EPSA a welcoming place for both current students and alums of both the Master’s and Doctorate in Education Policy programs where ideas are shared, lasting friendships are made, and professional advancements are cultivated. Joining the organization is free and open to all alums and GSEHD students with an interest in education policy.
summary: The Association's purpose is to be a forum for interaction among Education Policy students, alums, and faculty as well as the broader GW and DC policy community to foster social, academic, and professional development activities.

name: Delta Sigma Pi
description:
Delta Sigma Pi is one of GW's top co-ed Professional Business Fraternities. We strive to bolster professional development, utilize experiences to further expand our Fraternity network, and equip our Brothers with the necessary means to achieve their post-graduation goals. From internships on Wall Street to entrepreneurial start-ups, DSP is a great place to start your professional track. Beyond professional aspirations, we are a supportive community of like-driven individuals who can be seen helping each other perfect job applications, ace group projects, and find the best D.C. restaurants!

Our diverse Brotherhood features 70+ members from across the world who are dedicated Business, Economics, or International Economics students. We recruit new members at the start of every semester. Please check out our website, or reach out to dsprecruitmentgw@gmail.com to get involved!
summary:
Delta Sigma Pi is a Professional Business Fraternity that blends top-notch professional development with a supportive social community. We help Brothers achieve post-grad goals while making lifelong friendships.

Get involved! dsprecruitmentgw@gmail.com

name: Zeta Beta Tau
description: The mission of Zeta Beta Tau Fraternity is to foster and develop in its membership the tenets of its Credo: Intellectual Awareness, Social Responsibility, Integrity and Brotherly Love, in order to prepare its members for positions of leadership and service within their communities.
summary: The mission of Zeta Beta Tau Fraternity is to foster and develop in its membership the tenets of its Credo: Intellectual Awareness, Social Responsibility, Integrity and Brotherly Love, in order to prepare its members for positions of leadership and ser...

name: Student Association for Graduate Economists
description:
The Student Association for Graduate Economists, abbreviated as SAGE, represents the interests of graduate students in the Economics Department.

SAGE (Student Association for Graduate Economists) is a student-run association that provides GWU graduate students in Economics with opportunities to socialize, network and grow, through meeting with each other and discussing economics. SAGE represents the interests of graduate students, and organizes educational and social events. The purpose of SAGE is to bring together aspiring economists to create a productive research, and social, community. SAGE aims to grow the graduate program in Economics by strengthening the GWU network of graduate economists and engaging together.
summary: The Student Association for Graduate Economists, abbreviated as SAGE, represents the interests of graduate students in the Economics Department and organizes educational and social events. SAGE also runs an informal seminar series for graduate students...

name: Beta Theta Pi
description: Founded in 1839, Beta Theta Pi is an international fraternity built on a foundation of brotherhood, excellence, tradition and academics. Our mission is to develop Men of Principle for a Principled Life.
summary: Founded in 1839, Beta Theta Pi is an international fraternity built on a foundation of brotherhood, excellence, tradition and academics. Our mission is to develop Men of Principle for a Principled Life.

name: GW Catholics
description:
The Mission of GW Catholics at the Newman Center is to form lifelong disciples of Jesus Christ by encouraging a commitment to prayer, the sacraments, service, stewardship, and evangelization. Anyone and everyone is welcome to join us to pray, adore, study, eat, or just hang out at the Newman Center! Come join the family, you are not alone!

 

Our townhouse at 2210 F Street is open everyday that classes are in session--Monday through Thursday from 12-9:30pm and Friday from 12-8pm. Stop by anytime and see us!

 

 

DAILY SACRAMENTS (Mon-Fri):

Adoration: 5-6pm

Confession: 5:30-6pm

Mass: 6:10pm

 

Visit our website (https://www.gwcatholics.com) to find out more about our community and all of our upcoming events!
summary: The Mission of GW Catholics at the Newman Center is to form lifelong disciples of Jesus Christ by encouraging a commitment to prayer, the sacraments, service, stewardship, and evangelization. Anyone and everyone is welcome to join us!

name: Hindu Students Association
description: The Hindu Students Association at The George Washington University is an open and welcoming community for Hindus and non-Hindus alike. We host various events on campus like Diwali (festival of light), Holi (festival of color), and Interfaith Panels. All of our events are open to everyone and most have free admission and free food.
summary: The Hindu Students Association at The George Washington University is an open and welcoming community for Hindus and non-Hindus alike. We host various events on campus like Diwali (festival of light), Holi (festival of color), and Shivratri.

name: Capital Funk
description: Established over 20 years ago, Capital Funk has become a cornerstone of the DMV and GW dance community. Founded in the Spring of 2003 at George Washington University, Capital Funk celebrates the diversity that defines our region and cultivates safe spaces for dancers to be their authentic selves.  When we aren’t performing or competing, we center our core values of learning and contribution. We recognize hip hop’s history and foundations. We are constantly seeking to educate ourselves and provide students and local community members with access to low-cost training opportunities. At least twice per year, we host showcases to bring together a variety of teams and dancers across DC. These events spotlight the talent and innovation found right here in our city and university.
summary: Capital Funk has been a fixture of the DMV dance scene within GW since 2003. CFunk is best known for promoting a safe and comfortable hip hop culture and dance through trainings and stage productions all over the East Coast and within the DMV community.

name: Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Incorporated
description: Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Incorporated was founded January 5th, 1911 on the campus of Indiana University at Bloomington, Indiana. The American University Chapter, the Kappa Chi of Kappa Alpha Psi was charted September 12th, 1981. Kappa Chi encompasses American University, Catholic University, Georgetown University, and The George Washington University. The fundamental purpose of Kappa Alpha Psi is achievement. Since it's inception, Kappa Alpha Psi, and Kappa Chi Specifically has been dedicated to service in the community, especially in D.C.
summary: Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Incorporated was founded January 5th, 1911 on the campus of Indiana University at Bloomington, Indiana. The American University Chapter, the Kappa Chi of Kappa Alpha Psi was charted September 12th, 1981. Kappa Chi encompasse..

name: GW Class Council
description:
The GW Class Council is dedicated to fostering a strong sense of class affinity and
identity among undergraduate students at The George Washington University. The
organization will guide members of each undergraduate class in order to forge
meaningful connections between students and create significant milestones throughout
students’ time during their college careers that can be reflected in the future. In pursuit
of this objective, GW’s Class Council will do the following: it assists in the cultivation of class-specific traditions and events that celebrate and honor milestones that create a shared affinity within students’ class years; it encourages student engagement with GW student life through fostering strong
class identities and culture that provides connections amongst students
throughout their time at GW; it develops and encourages personal relationships among students through class-specific programming or activities; it provides class-specific mentorship and services to create a smoother and collaborative college process; it supplies students with tailored support and resources to create a smoother undergraduate college process; and it maintains and cultivates positive environments for students to connect and collaborate with their peers, regardless of academic or professional background.
summary: It is the purpose of GW Class Council to provide leadership and guidance to members of each of the undergraduate classes in order to enrich the unique bond of GW students through a diverse variety of programming and events.

name: GW Women in Business
description:
GW Women in Business strives to bring together students from all majors, career interests, and aspirations to prepare them with the skills to achieve personal and career success. We seek to provide opportunities for mentorship, networking, leadership, and community engagement to the members of GWWIB.

In addition to our annual signature events, GWWIB hosts three General Body Meetings a semester, at various times and locations. Follow our Instagram (@gwwib) for regular updates.

The 2025-2026 membership form is officially open for the Fall 2025 semester! Invitations to join GWWIB on GW Engage will not be accepted until this form is submitted.

Link to the Membership Form (2025-2026)

If you have any questions regarding the form or the membership process, please contact gwwib.membership@gmail.com with any questions.
summary: GWWIB provides students who are committed to empowering themselves with the necessary resources to excel in the workforce, fostering a community where members can learn and grow through our focus areas: Careers, Connections, and Community.

name: Sigma Chi Fraternity
description: According to Time Magazine, "Sigma Chi is easily the most solid and well known fraternity in America". Sigma Chi recently held our 150th anniversary, and became the only Fraternity to be honored by the United States Congress. As one of the oldest and largest Greek organizations, with over 240 chapters, we have a long and proud tradition. Sigma Chi was founded on June 28, 1855 at Miami University in Ohio. The fundamental purpose of the Sigma Chi Fraternity is the cultivation, maintenance, and accomplishment of the ideals of friendship, justice, and learning within our membership. Our Fraternity does not haze and we strongly encourage our members to excel academically, civically, and socially outside our organization.
summary: According to Time Magazine, "Sigma Chi is easily the most solid and well known fraternity in America". Sigma Chi recently held our 150th anniversary, and became the only Fraternity to be honored by the United States Congress. As one of the oldest and l...

name: Organization of Latin American Students
description: The Organization Of Latin American Students celebrates the individuality and intersectionality of identities with ties to Latin America. We recognize the differences and nuances that exist within our community and are committed to encourage for all our members to be seen and heard within our organization, as well as within GW. We welcome all who join us in celebrating the uniqueness of our identities.
summary: The Organization of Latin American Students, founded in 1996 as Latinos For Progress, shall embody all members of The George Washington University interested in the Latinx culture and the general elevation of the Latinx community.

name: The George Washington University Club Fencing
description:
GW Club Fencing focuses on training and developing the skills of its fencers, and we have members with all levels of experience. We offer training in Foil, Sabre and Epee. All fencers are encouraged to take part in tournaments to give them practical experience in fencing, and we compete in regional and national tournaments.

 

For Fall 2024, we will practice every Wednesday from 7-9pm and Saturday from 5-7pm, with exceptions for holidays, breaks, tournaments, etc. All are welcome! 

For more updates or details of the club make sure to follow us on instagram @gw_fencing
summary: GW Club Fencing focuses on training and developing the skills of our fencers, and we have members with all levels of experience. We offer training in Foil, Sabre and Epee. All fencers are encouraged to participate in regional and national tournaments.

name: The American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics
description: AIAA is a national organization with a local branch at GW. It advocates for emerging technologies in aerospace, and serves to help teach, connect, and learn about the industry. We visit local engineering facilities, attend conferences, host events, and are engaged in GW's engineering community
summary: GW AIAA is a student branch of AIAA that advocates for emerging technologies in aerospace, and serves to help teach, connect, and learn about the industry. We visit local engineering research facilities, attend conferences, and host events.

name: George Washington University Men’s Rugby Football Club
description: GW Rugby exists to teach the sport of rugby to male students of the George Washington University, while providing a safe, fun, and competitive atmosphere in which to practice and play rugby
summary: GW Rugby exists to teach the sport of rugby to male students of the George Washington University, while providing a safe, fun, and competitive atmosphere in which to practice and play rugby

name: George Washington Fashion & Business Association
description:
GWFBA is a creative and professional hub on campus blending passions for fashion and business. Students involved get a chance to explore career paths within the fashion, beauty and entertainment industries through three realms: visual content, written content, and event planning. At FBA's core is GEORGE -our student-produced annual publication. GEORGE features local business highlights, current events, and trends in fashion, it amplifies student voices and is shared university-wide. Throughout the year, we host fashion-related events such as speakers series (Rachel Zoe, Ivan Bart, and Katie Welch,CMO of Rare Beauty), pop-up shops on campus (By Lil Bikinis), and local shopping events (Glossier).

 

Our Core Values: 

Creativity: to continuously understand when and where fashion can be woven into life regardless of intended major, we push ourselves and our work to question and examine fashion from the nation’s capital

Collaboration: we believe that the fusion of diverse perspectives enhances our collective potential and invite any student from any school in any major to join

Professional Development: through networking events, career workshops, and industry partnerships, we enable our members to leverage their passion for fashion into tangible opportunity

Education: we are nothing if we are not what we come to GW for, we are committed to providing high-quality educational opportunities that equip our members with a comprehensive understanding of both the fashion and business

Community: we cultivate a tight-knit and supportive community, FBA serves as a platform for building lifelong friendships, mentorship connections, and a shared appreciation form fashion
summary: FBA's mission is to foster a collaborative environment where creative innovation meets strategic acumen, empowering our members with professional opportunities and the network necessary to excel in the fashion and business spaces.

name: GW Women's and Gender Inclusive Club Ultimate Frisbee
description: We are the Women's and Gender Inclusive (WGI) Ultimate Frisbee team at GW! Our ~ultimate~ goal is to bring together women and gender expansive individuals who want to play competitive frisbee and participate in high-level college tournaments. Our mission is to bring the spirit of the game back to sports, starting with our own. Any and all levels of experience are welcome! Our practices are 4 - 6 pm Tuesday/Thursdays, and 10 - 1pm Saturdays down at the national mall polo fields!
summary: We are the Women's and Gender Inclusive (WGI) ultimate frisbee team at GW. We hold regular practices and strive to bring the spirit of the game back to sport, starting with our own.

name: GW Women's Club Lacrosse
description: The George Washington Women's Club Lacrosse team is a student organization that allows participants to continue playing lacrosse at the collegiate level. In addition to field practices during the week, we also have workouts, team dinners, fundraisers, social events, and of course, games and tournaments in the DMV. We play during both the Fall and Spring seasons, and hold tryouts at the beginning of both.
summary: The George Washington Women's Club Lacrosse team is a student organization which allows participants to continue playing lacrosse at the collegiate level.

name: GW Association for Women in Mathematics
description: We are the George Washington University student chapter of the Association for Women in Mathematics. We welcome all interested students, regardless of major, gender, or age. Our goals: 1) To encourage women and girls to pursue careers in the mathematical sciences 2) To provide support for mathematics faculty, graduate students, and undergraduate students 3) To expose all students, regardless of major or gender, to the world of professional mathematics and to the various career options in mathematics 4) To create opportunities for networking with professional mathematicians
summary: We are the GW chapter of the Association for Women in Mathematics. We welcome all interested students, regardless of major, gender, or age. Our goal is to encourage women to pursue careers in mathematical studies and provide a professional network.

name: George Washington University Men's Club Soccer
description: A group of dedicated and experienced soccer players that competes regularly against other universities in the surrounding area. We hold regular practices, and a try out is required to become a member of the team.
summary: A group of dedicated and experienced soccer players that competes regularly against other universities in the surrounding area. We hold regular practices, and a try out is required to become a member of the team.

name: George Washington Women's Club Soccer
description: The GW Women's Club Soccer Team (GWWCS), established in 2001, provides an opportunity for women to play soccer at a collegiate level while having fun and learning more about the game. The team is composed of competitive and dedicated players who are chosen through selective tryouts held in early September. We compete in Region I of the National Intramural and Recreational Sports Association's (NIRSA) soccer league, against other club teams from universities along the east coast. Practices are held 2-3 times a week and games are played on the weekend during both a fall and spring season.
summary: The GW Women's Club Soccer Team (GWWCS), established in 2001, provides an opportunity for women to play soccer at a collegiate level while having fun and learning more about the game. The team is composed of competitive and dedicated players who are ch..

name: GW Club Water Polo
description:
Welcome to GW Club Water Polo!
We’re a co-ed, skill-inclusive club team that practices three times a week in the Smith Center and competes in both home and away tournaments each semester. Whether you're brand new to the sport or a seasoned player, our team is all about learning, playing, and building a supportive, tight-knit community—while having a lot of fun along the way.

Practices are held in the Smith Center (600 22nd St NW, Washington, DC 20052)

Monday: 8:00-9:30 PM

Tuesday: 8:00-9:30 PM

Thursday: 8:00-9:30 PM

📧 Interested in joining? Email us at gwclubwaterpolo@gmail.com
📸 Follow us on Instagram: @gwclubwaterpolo
📝 Fill out our Spring 2026 Interest Form
summary: Welcome to GW Club Water Polo!! Our co-ed club sports team practices three times weekly in the Smith Center and competes in home and away tournaments each semester. Reach us at gwclubwaterpolo@gmail.com!

name: GW Casa Blanca
description: Casa Blanca is a student organization formed to create a safe space that caters to the needs of Latinx students and/or students interested in the LatinX culture. We hope to provide students with a home away from home.
summary: Casa Blanca is a student organization formed to create a safe space that caters to the needs of LatinX students and/or students interested in the LatinX culture. We hope to provide students with a home away from home.

name: American Marketing Association
description:
AMA is a national organization that connects you to marketers across all specialties to collaborate, network, gain practical experience, and empower your career. We have a continual program of events and training throughout the year that are tailored to make you a better marketer. Not only will you be learning from some of the sharpest minds in the industry, as an AMA member you’ll also enjoy student discounts and access to the DC professional chapter. All majors are welcome!

 

This year we are going to work on building up your experience, resources, and community through various activities including:

- Nationally-Recognized Case Competition

- Keynote Speakers

- Resume, Design, and Marketing Workshops

- Networking Opportunities 

To join us, please fill out our AMA 2025-26 Membership Application

Join our GroupMe: Here

Click the calendar below to subscribe to the AMA Events calendar!
summary: “Membership in a national organization like AMA connects you to marketers across all specialties to collaborate, network, gain practical experience, and empower your career."

name: Socialist Action Initiative
description:
GW Socialist Action Initiative hopes to create a space to develop socialist organizers dedicated to building mass power: dedicated to building the power of workers, of tenants, of students, of oppressed communities. SAI is a multi-tendency socialist organization that encourages internal debate and discussion. We believe that no one group on the left has all the answers to the enormous challenges we face in building a mass, fighting socialist movement in this country. Our unity, even as we focus on different fields of struggle—whether that is labor and tenant organizing, anti-imperialist solidarity, building student and worker power on campus, or political education—is rooted in the understanding of this all being the same fight: a fight that will ultimately only be successful through moving the broad masses of working people.

At SAI, we have an internal distinction between what we call "Friends of SAI" and full members. If you are just interested in hearing about what SAI is up to and attending our events, then just join on Engage. If you actually want to be an active part of the work SAI is doing, also fill out this google form and one of the members of our recruitment committee will get in contact with you in order to schedule an informal chat to see if you would be a good fit.
summary: GW Socialist Action Initiative is a multi-tendency socialist organization on campus, dedicated to struggling against colonialism, imperialism, and capitalism through both building mass power and developing militant socialists.

name: The Mexican Students Association at The George Washington University
description: The Mexican Students Association, or MXGWU for short, was established in 2013 as the first Mexican-centric organization. The purpose of MXGWU is to spread and promote Mexican culture while also raising awareness of relevant social concerns. We aim to provide a venue to learn, develop identity and be in contact with Mexican culture through education, debate, culture, networking, and value promotion by facilitating academic, cultural, and social events, resources, and opportunities to the GWU community.
summary: The Mexican Students Association, or MXGWU for short, was established in 2013 as the first Mexican-centric organization whose primary objectives are to share Mexican culture, cuisine, traditions, and create a home away from home on campus.

name: Cisneros Hispanic Leadership Institute
description: The Cisneros Hispanic Leadership Institute draws on the intellectual strength of the George Washington University and the resources of the nation’s capital to cultivate citizen scholars and innovative research focused on the Latino community. Research produced by the institute examines current issues facing our country to encourage diverse perspectives in national dialogue and decision-making, while the Institute’s robust curriculum in leadership and professional development prepares high-achieving students to make a difference and become the leaders of their generation.
summary: The Cisneros Hispanic Leadership Institute draws on the intellectual strength of the George Washington University and the resources of the nation’s capital to cultivate citizen scholars and innovative research focused on the Latino community. Research ...

name: Chabad GW
description: - A network of campus-based Jewish organizations providing social, educational, recreational, and spiritual programming for students and faculty. - A family-centered "home away from home" where all Jews are welcome. - A oasis where students can socialize in a comfortable, home-like setting, with friends, food and discussion. - A community for all students, with opportunities to enrich their experiences in all aspects of their college career. - A center for prayer and study, where students can gain a deeper understanding and appreciation of their Jewish heritage. - A resource for Jewish lifecycle events. - A beacon of tradition to the local community. - An institution where students can question your faith without fear of judgment. - A partnership between students and staff to help create student programming, plan social action projects, raise women's issues, promote health awareness activities and offer volunteer opportunities. - The student-centered campus component of the international Chabad-Lubavitch movement.
summary: - A network of campus-based Jewish organizations providing social, educational, recreational, and spiritual programming for students and faculty.- A family-centered "home away from home" where all Jews are welcome.- A oasis where students can socialize...

name: GW for Israel
description: GW for Israel (GWI) is a non-partisan, pro­-Israel student organization focused on promoting education on Israeli culture, history, politics, and encouraging deeper understanding of the nuanced Israeli-Palestinian conflict. GWI fosters a tight-knit student body community that brings a piece of Israel to campus.
summary: GW for Israel (GWI) is a non-partisan, pro­-Israel student organization focused on promoting education on Israeli culture, history, politics, and encouraging deeper understanding of the nuanced Israeli-Palestinian conflict. GWI fosters a tight-knit stu...

name: The George Washington University Triathlon Team
description: The Triathlon Team is a Club Sports Team at GWU. We compete in the Mid-Atlantic Triathlon Conference (MACTC). On campus we provide both undergraduate and graduate students the opportunity to train and compete with fellow triathletes, regardless of prior experience. We welcome all abilities, whether you've been racing triathlons your whole life or need a few bike and swim lessons to boost your confidence.
summary: The George Washington University Triathlon Team (GW Triathlon Team) was established in November, 2011. The GW Triathlon Team brings interested GW students together to learn, train, and compete in the sport of triathlon in a safe and inclusive environment

name: Elliott School Graduate Board
description: The Elliott School Graduate Board (ESGB) is the umbrella organization for graduate student organizations at the George Washington University's Elliott School of International Affairs. The mission of ESGB is to foster a strong sense of community among Elliott School graduate students, represent them at the university level, and enrich their professional and intellectual advancement. ESGB accomplishes this by providing Elliott School graduate students with activities to expand their professional and social networks and by encouraging active involvement in the greater Washington, D.C. community.
summary: ESGB is the umbrella organization for graduate student organizations at the Elliott School. We seek to foster a strong sense of community among Elliott graduate students and enrich their professional and intellectual advancement.

name: Multicultural Greek Council
description: The George Washington University, Multicultural Greek Council (MGC) is the governing council for historically cultural interest-based and identity-based organizations. We strive to serve and coordinate the activities of our member chapters, promote involvement in Greek Life, support the development of individual chapters, enhance cultural awareness, and encourage collaboration and communication throughout the Greek community.
summary: The George Washington University, Multicultural Greek Council (MGC) serves to coordinate the activities of its member chapters, promote involvement in Greek Life, support the development of individual chapters, enhance cultural awareness, and encourage...

name: GW Women's Club Volleyball
description:
This is a club volleyball team for both undergraduate and graduate female students. We host tryouts in the beginning of the fall semester, have two practices each week, and participate in three weekend tournaments each semester! In the spring, we send a team to compete at the NCVF National Championships!

Please fill out our interest form: https://forms.gle/vG8Nmz6BjD6BCYD3A
summary: Club volleyball team for undergraduate and graduate students. We host tryouts in the beginning of the semester, have two practices each week, and participate in three tournaments each semester! In the spring, we send a team to compete at NCVF Nationals!

name: GW Dance Marathon
description:
GW Dance Marathon is an organization aimed at raising awareness and money for the DC area Children's Miracle Network Hospital, through a year round campaign and fundraising, culminating in our 6 hour activity marathon.

Follow us on Instagram @gwdancemarathon for updates on GWDM and to find more ways to get involved! We meet every other week on Tuesday at 7:30 PM (DM or email us for more info!).

Want to donate or register for the marathon? Use this link to access our DonorDrive home page!
summary: GW Dance Marathon is an organization aimed at raising money for the DC area Children's Miracle Network Hospitals, through year round fundraising, ending in a 6 hour activity marathon. Follow us on Instagram @gwdancemarathon for updates on GWDM!

name: Epsilon Sigma Alpha
description: Epsilon Sigma Alpha is an Alternative Greek Organization and the only community service and leadership sorority at The George Washington University in Washington, DC. Our motto is "Sisterhood Through Service" and we strive to fulfill this ideal put forth by our founders. Since the establishment of the Delta Zeta chapter in 2003, ESA at GW has grown to be a group of more than 50 members, accomplishing over 3,000 hours of service each year and raising over $5,000 annually for St. Jude Children's Research Hospital, the most of any other ESA chapter in the country.
summary: Epsilon Sigma Alpha is an Alternative Greek community service and leadership sorority. Our motto is "Sisterhood Through Service," and we aim to serve the GW and DC community while forming a close-knit community.

name: Phi Sigma Pi: Beta Mu Chapter
description:
Phi Sigma Pi National Honor Fraternity takes the best attributes from an honor society, a service group and a social organization and blends them into one organization unique from all others. Phi Sigma Pi is the premier National Honor Fraternity in existence. We engage the brightest and best academic students on campuses and provide them with the opportunity to enhance their college experience and improve their abilities outside the classroom. We know what you’re thinking… these students must be the most active students on campus! Because Phi Sigma Pi is a blend of traditional organizations, it offers a major advantage to the most active students. We offer strong academic students the chance to experience the benefits of three types of organizations all consolidated into one premier organization:

• the personal rewards of an honor society

• the internal satisfaction of serving others through service

• memories and friendships that will last long after the college years are over
summary: Phi Sigma Pi National Honor Fraternity takes the best attributes from an honor society, a service group, and a social organization and blends them into a unique organization. We emphasize fellowship, leadership, and scholarship to impact our community.

name: GW-TV
description:
GW-TV is The George Washington University’s only student-run television station, catering to both the GW and local D.C. communities. GW-TV welcomes members from all backgrounds, areas of study and levels of experience.

GW-TV currently produces four shows in its newly-renovated, state-of-the-art studio: GWN (news), Capital Crossfire (debate), Unstoppable (sports), and District Debrief (sketch comedy). We make news packages, conduct studio shoots, edit, and film packages.
summary: GW-TV is a student-run TV station located in the School of Media and Public Affairs. We create programming ranging from campus news to sketch comedy to political debates. We are not limited to SMPA majors and encourage all students to participate!

name: Chess Club
description:
Our purpose is to provide a student-led organization that unleashes its member's creativity and problem-solving skills through the art of chess. We meet at the USC on Thursdays from 7-9pm! Players of any skill level are welcome! 

Follow us on Instagram!
summary: Our purpose is to provide a student led organization that unleashes its members creativity and problem solving skills through the art of chess.

name: Prospanica at GWU
description: The Association of Hispanic Professionals (Prospanica) at GWU, previously known as NSHMBA, is a professional, nationally-affiliated student organization. Founded by MBA students in 2017, our university chapter's mission is to empower Hispanic students and business professionals to achieve their full educational, economic, and social potential. In the spirit of promoting diversity and building partnerships, we welcome and encourage students of all races, ethnicities, and majors to become a part of the organization. Please note, National Prospanica membership at the discounted student rate of $30 is required for full benefits. Further details can be found through the link: https://prospanica.org/professionals/#member-benefits
summary: The Association of Hispanic Professionals (Prospanica) at GWU, previously known as NSHMBA, is a professional, nationally-affiliated student organization. Founded by MBA students in 2017, our university chapter's mission is to empower Hispanic students

name: GWU Esports
description:
George Washington Esports is an organization that brings students who share an interest in playing and spectating matches of computer and video games together. 

Come join us in our discord server here: https://discord.gg/GumkvnUnPx

Twitch Account: https://www.twitch.tv/gwuesports
summary: George Washington Esports is an organization that brings together students interested in playing and spectating computer and video game matches. We welcome players of all skill levels, as well as spectators, to all meetings and events.

name: Alpha Theta Delta–George Washington University Delta Chapter
description: Alpha Theta Delta (ATD) seeks to promote design and the versatility of design, to create a professional network between designers and students, to create a welcoming design community for Interior Architecture majors and those interested in the field, to host design-related academic and social events on campus, and to empower members of all backgrounds with a solid foundation of versatile design skills, encouraging them to incorporate these design foundations in real world applications.
summary: ATD is a design organization that fosters community, professional networking, and versatile design skills through academic and social events, supporting Interior Architecture majors and others interested in design.

name: Chronic Health Advocates
description:
Chronic Health Advocates is a health advocacy organization that aims to (1) educate our community on issues regarding rare and chronic illnesses and disabilities, (2) promote awareness across George Washington University on rare and chronic illnesses, (3) provide a space for both allies and patients to discuss various topics relevant to the rare and chronic illness community. 6 in 10 Americans are living with a rare or chronic illness and there are approximately 7,000 rare diseases with more being discovered every day. Patients diagnosed with rare or chronic illnesses may experience a number of issues like stigma, misdiagnoses, mental health, and many others. Many patients feel isolated and face unique obstacles when living day-to-day with a chronic illness. Thus, the mission of Chronic Health Advocates is to provide a space for both students and allies to discuss issues relevant to the rare disease and chronic illness community

 

We are an official Students for Rare Chapter of the National Organization for Rare Disorders and will frequently host events alongside NORD as well as other rare disease advocacy organizations.

 

If you don’t know much about the rare disease and chronic illness community, don’t worry! We’re completely open to membership from the most experienced student advocate to students who seek to learn more about our community.
summary: Chronic Health Advocates is a student-run advocacy organization at The George Washington University dedicated to serving students with rare and chronic conditions. We are open to both allies and students with rare diseases who are dedicated to advocacy.

name: Beta Alpha Psi
description:
Founded in 1919, Beta Alpha Psi is an Accounting Honors Society for all financial information students and professionals. There are over 300 chapters on college and university campuses with over 300,000 members initiated since Beta Alpha Psi's formation.

Why Beta Alpha Psi?
summary: Founded in 1919, Beta Alpha Psi is an Accounting Honors Society for all financial information students and professionals. There are over 300 chapters on college and university campuses with over 300,000 members initiated since Beta Alpha Psi's formation.

name: Association of Energy Engineers - GW Student Chapter
description: The GW Student Association of Energy Engineers is a student led-organization closely associated with both the National AEE Organization and the National Capital Chapter. The group holds regular meetings, facility tours and opportunities for student engagement. Members are primarily a mix of engineering disciplines but all with a shared passion for energy, sustainability, and technology explored through an engineering lens are welcome to join.
summary: The GW Student Association of Energy Engineers is a student-led organization closely associated with the National AEE Organization. Members are from various engineering disciplines with a shared passion for energy systems and innovation.

name: GW Friends of KofC
description: The GW Friends of KofC is the premier Catholic men's group on campus. Our fraternity brings Catholic men together in a commitment of faith, service and brotherhood.
summary: The GW Friends of KofC is the premier Catholic men's group on campus. Our fraternity brings Catholic men together in a commitment of faith, service and brotherhood.

name: GW Chamak
description: GW Chamak is George Washington University’s only femme-centric multicultural dance team. Since 2002, Chamak has been representing GW’s diverse community through a blend of various dance forms, including Bollywood, CPop, classical, reggaeton, hip hop, street, etc. We exist on the common understanding that dance is more than just movement- it is a way of life. This philosophy has allowed us to bring together the different cultures of our members  right to GW’s community. Chamak performs at various events and showcases throughout the year. We start out each season with fresh ideas from our team members to promote a celebration of diversity through dance. We dance for laughter, we dance for madness, we dance for ourselves, and we dance for you.
summary: GW Chamak is George Washington University’s only femme inclusive multicultural dance team. Since 2002, Chamak has been representing GW’s diverse community through a blend of various dance forms, from Bollywood, CPop, hip hop, reggaeton, street, etc.

name: Organizers for Revolutionary Climate Action
description: ORCA (Organizers for Revolutionary Climate Action) is a movement to stop climate change by bringing down the fossil fuel industry, starting with universities. We are currently running a campaign, No Fossil Fuel Money, to rid GW of the influence of the fossil fuel industry, and we need your help! Join our GroupMe HERE!
summary: Organizers for Revolutionary Climate Action is a movement to stop climate change by bringing down the fossil fuel industry, starting by ridding GW of its ties to the fossil fuel industry.

name: Master of Health Administration Student Association
description: Founded to provide a vehicle for constructive student involvement, recognition, and representation in the university and healthcare community, The GWU Master of Health Administration Student Association acts as a liaison between the department and students. This student organization is also responsible for increasing the interaction between the student body and leading healthcare players in Washington, D.C., and its surrounding areas.
summary: Founded to provide a vehicle for constructive student involvement, recognition, and representation in the university and healthcare community, The George Washington University Health Administration Student Association acts as a liaison between the depa..

name: GW South Asian Society
description: The South Asian Society is a non-profit, socio-cultural organization that has been created to promote unity and cultural awareness amongst the South Asian students of The George Washington University, including those from countries such as Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Maldives, Nepal, Pakistan, and Sri Lanka.
summary: GW SAS is a non-profit, socio-cultural organization that has been created to promote unity and cultural awareness amongst the South Asian students of The George Washington University.

name: GW Ice Hockey
description: The team currently plays in the Atlantic Coast Collegiate Hockey League (ACCHL) within the Southeastern Division II Conference of the American Collegiate Hockey Association (ACHA). Our schedule is made up of a collection of Division II and I ACHA teams from up and down the East Coast. Our team is comprised of graduate and undergraduate students who came from playing prep school, junior A and B, high school, and tier I hockey. The team is run with the help of managers, coaches, league representatives, school administrators, and supporters.
