summary: The team currently plays in the Atlantic Coast Collegiate Hockey League (ACCHL) within the Southeastern Division II Conference of the American Collegiate Hockey Association (ACHA). Our schedule is made up of a collection of Division II and III ACHA tea...

name: George Washington University Desis
description:
About GW Desis

The GW Desis is a passionate and dedicated team committed to celebrating the rich tapestry of Desi cultures. We're here to foster inclusivity, unity, and growth within the GW Desis community. Our mission encompasses the following key pillars:

Cultural Unity: We host vibrant events to showcase and celebrate the diverse cultures of our community, nurturing understanding and respect.

Inclusive Space: Every voice is heard and valued in our community. We create a welcoming and supportive environment for all.

Cross-Cultural Collaboration: We collaborate with other groups to create enriching exchanges and meaningful friendships that extend beyond our community.

Growth Opportunities: We offer skill-building workshops, mentorship programs, and initiatives to support academic and personal excellence.

Student-Driven Initiatives: Your ideas drive our growth. We provide a platform for your visions to become impactful actions.

Alumni Engagement: We connect with our esteemed alumni to bridge the gap between the past and the present, offering valuable guidance and networking opportunities.

Transparency: We uphold open communication and accountability, ensuring that you're informed and actively involved in our activities.

Sustainability: We lead by example in environmental responsibility, making eco-conscious choices in all our endeavours.

Join us in building an inclusive and vibrant future for the GW Desis community. Together, we're making a difference.
summary: The GW Desis is dedicated to celebrating diverse Desi cultures and fostering an inclusive community. We showcase our heritage through vibrant events, ensure every voice is valued, and promote cross-cultural collaboration.

name: Graduate Business Association (GBA)
description:
Mission Statement: To enhance all GWSB graduate students’ experience through various academic, social and professional opportunities while funding and supporting all subsidiary graduate student organizations.

The GBA serves as the official “umbrella” organization for all participating graduate student organizations within the The George Washington University School of Business (GWSB). Called “Subsidiary Organizations”, the GBA provides constant support and unity among them throughout the course of the academic year.
summary: Mission Statement: To enhance all GWSB graduate students’ experience through various academic, social and professional opportunities while funding and supporting all subsidiary graduate student organizations.

name: GW College Republicans
description: GW College Republicans is the largest conservative organization on campus and one of the most active CRs chapters in the nation. We host guest speakers, political events, social events, and more! We are a big tent organization welcoming all types of Republicans: moderates, libertarians, national conservatives, and those who are unsure and just interested in learning more. Past speakers include: Lee Zeldin, Dan Crenshaw, Pat Buchanan, Corey Lewandowski, Ron Paul, Karl Rove, Dana Perino, Pete Sessions, Jon Huntsman, Mia Love, Charles Krauthammer, and others.
summary: GW College Republicans is the largest conservative organization on campus and one of the most active CRs chapters in the nation. We host guest speakers, political events, social events, and more! We are a big tent organization welcoming all types of ...

name: GW Pakistani Students' Association
description: The GW Pakistani Students' Association is dedicated to bringing the larger South Asian and GW community together to raise awareness about Pakistan and to promote and celebrate its rich cultural heritage.
summary: The GW Pakistani Students' Association is dedicated to bringing the South Asian and larger GW community together to raise awareness about Pakistan and to promote and celebrate its rich cultural heritage.

name: GW Parliamentary Debate Society
description:
We are GW Parliamentary Debate Society: GW's ONLY student-run debate organization! We compete regularly against the nation's top universities through the American Parliamentary Debate Association. Our club is APDA's 1st overall club. In addition, we have multiple debaters historically ranked in the individual top 5 as well as multiple two-person teams historically ranked in the top 5 as well. 

 

We have no tryouts, no debate experience requirements and GWPDS is completely free to join. We meet Mondays and Wednesdays from 8-10pm in Funger 209 and provide opportunities to compete every weekend. We will teach you how to debate!

 

Fill out this form to join our mailing list and receive more information about joining the team: https://forms.gle/7ikrfCuXDjrdp7MH7

 

We'd love to meet you! If you have any questions you're always welcome to reach out to us via facebook, instagram, or at gwpds1@gmail.com.
summary: GW's ONLY entirely student-run debate organization. We compete against the nation's top universities through the American Parliamentary Debate Association and are currently ranked 1st overall nationally. No debate experience necessary. We will teach you!

name: GW School of Business Office of Undergrad Programs
The Association of Chemistry Graduate Students
description: The purpose of ACGS is to provide an active community for incoming and current graduate students, serve as a liaison between faculty and graduate students, host professional development events, and cultivate opportunities and resources for those pursuing a career in chemistry at GW.
summary: The Association of Chemistry Graduate Students (ACGS) is dedicated to providing an active community for current and incoming chemistry graduate students by generating opportunities for professional development and socialization internally and externally.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers
description: WELCOME TO GWU ASCE! The American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE) GWU Student Chapter strives to develop interest, among undergraduate students, in the various fields of civil engineering through hands-on applications of academic and professional nature. Through our activities, members take advantage of concepts they learn in the classroom and apply it to real-life situations.
summary: ASCE stands at the forefront of a profession that plans, designs, constructs, and operates society’s economic and social engine – the built environment – while protecting and restoring the natural environment.

name: GW Club Running
description:
GW’s finest running club since 2012

Take a look at our new website! gwuclubrunning.com

All are encouraged to join- no experience necessary! We provide our members and community with regular opportunities for recreational and competitive running by running different routes and distances all over DC and competing in meets in the area. All practices and meets are optional, so whether you run once a day or once a month, you are welcome. We also have lots of social events and fundraisers so that we have fun outside of running!

 

Purpose

GW Club Cross Country serves to provide GW graduate and undergraduate students running opportunities, including but not limited to:

  1. Regularly scheduled runs 
  2. Exploration of DC trails and running locations
  3. Improvement of running abilities
  4. Access to local and regional races
  5. Inclusion and camaraderie of a recognized team

 

Join us on Strava here!
summary: GW’s finest running club since 2012 - all are welcome, no experience necessary! We provide our members regular opportunities for recreational & competitive running, as well as social events, to promote community-building, self-improvement, & fitness.

name: GW Ballroom
description: Waltz, tango, cha-cha, jive: do any of these words sound familiar? Learn all about them in GW Ballroom, one of DC's finest collegiate ballroom teams! Our team’s goal is to bring together a community of people who are enthusiastic about learning and competing ballroom dance. Many of our current dancers, who joined the team with little to no experience, have been crowned finalists in prestigious competitions across the country! GW Ballroom also serves as a place where students can unwind from their studies, have fun, and learn something new!
summary: Want to learn how to dance? GW Ballroom offers lessons on all styles of ballroom so that our members can compete professionally or just have fun at socials and show off their new moves to friends! No music or dance experience needed!

name: GW Club Swimming
description:
GW Club Swimming was established in 2007 to provide an active recreational outlet for swimmers who want to keep up their passion for the pool without the pressure of varsity-level competition. Club Swimming competes in Collegiate Club Swimming sanctioned meets. This nationally affiliated league brings together clubs from across the country to compete with each other. Come to practice and meet the team if you enjoy a fun, competitive, and pressure-free swimming environment! Club Swimming practices during both the fall and spring semesters.

Please fill out the 2025-2026 Interest Form so we can add you to our email list :) Join our GroupMe here!
summary: GW Club Swimming provides an active recreational outlet for swimmers who want keep up their passion for the pool without the pressure of varsity-level competition. Come to practice and meet the team if you enjoy a fun swimming environment!

name: Black Girl Mentorship Program
description: BGM is the organization devoted to the mentorship, friendship, unity, and guidance amongst the Black women and girls at GW. The org's main goal is to allow for an easy transition for Black girls who are in need of assistance in assimilating into a PWI. We accomplish this by our Mentor/Mentee program that pairs an underclassman with an upperclassman to be as your guiding light and friend as they you navigate the college life. We also provide fun and engaging events to make way for opportunity for the Black girl community to meet and form lasting bonds.
summary: BGM is the organization devoted to the mentorship, friendship, unity, and guidance amongst the Black women and girls at GW. The org's main goal is to allow for an easy transition for Black girls who are in need of assistance in assimilating into a PWI.

name: Women's MBA Association
description: We aim to build an inclusive GW community that supports women with future careers in Business. To achieve this, WMBAA strives to form a network of career-oriented guidance for its members through dialogue and educational forums. We intend to work towards a goal of women leading across all levels and fields during and after their time at GW.
summary: We aim to engage the entire MBA community through career enhancement and personal and community empowerment.

name: GWU National Society of Black Engineers
description: The purpose of NSBE is to increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally and positively impact the community.
summary: The purpose of NSBE is to increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally and positively impact the community.

name: Biomedical Engineering Society
description: The Mission of The George Washington University Biomedical Engineering Society Student Chapter shall be to build and support the biomedical engineering community at GWU and in the local area by promoting social, professional, and educational development. The Biomedical Engineering Society strives to promote camaraderie and academic excellence, among not only GW’s Biomedical Engineers, but SEAS as a whole. Through professional development, community outreach, philanthropic efforts, and social engagement, The Biomedical Engineering Society seeks to provide opportunities for students to enhance both their personal and professional lives.
summary: The Mission of The George Washington University Biomedical Engineering Society Student Chapter shall be to build and support the biomedical engineering community at GWU and in the local area by promoting social, professional, and educational development.

name: Interfraternity Council
description: The Interfraternity Council (IFC) is the umbrella organization that oversees all 9 men's social fraternities at The George Washington University. While these chapters have different histories, distinguishing characteristics, and members, they all share the central tenets of Fraternity and Sorority Life at GW: scholarship, service, active leadership, accountability, and community. The IFC is governed by an Executive Council and a board consisting of each of the 9 IFC chapter presidents.
summary: The Interfraternity Council (IFC) is the umbrella organization that oversees all 9 men's social fraternities at The George Washington University. These chapters have different histories and characteristics, but share similar core and guiding values.

name: Milken por la Salud
description: Milken por la Salud is a student-led organization of Latine students and allies who are committed to serving the Latine community through education, advocacy, and solidarity. As public health needs are not restricted to English-speaking communities, members will have the opportunity to learn, practice, and engage in Spanish with a focus on health and wellness. By developing our Spanish skills, we will be able to better serve members of the community whose social determinants include having a language barrier. Our aim is to create more conscientious and culturally aware public health professionals while promoting health among Spanish-speaking and Latine communities.
summary: Milken por la Salud is a student-led organization focusing on serving the Latine community through education, advocacy, and solidarity. It aims to create culturally aware public health professionals and promote health among Spanish-speaking communities.

name: Vietnamese Student Association
description: The Vietnamese Student Association (VSA) of the George Washington University seeks to promote the awareness of Vietnamese culture and to provide a forum to serve and learn about the Vietnamese community. We exist to promote cultural understanding at GWU and to provide valuable events and services to the university community. VSA welcomes all students who are interested in learning about Vietnamese culture, regardless of race, creed, religion, or political affiliation. Feel free to come out to any of our events to learn more about us!
summary: The Vietnamese Student Association (VSA) of the George Washington University seeks to promote the awareness of Vietnamese culture and to provide a forum to serve and learn about the Vietnamese community. We exist to promote cultural understanding at GW...

name: Delta Epsilon Mu
description:
The brothers and sisters of Delta Epsilon Mu are committed to serving the community by advocating health education and care, and through active participation in service activities, fundraising, and outreach programs.

Our Mission

Delta Epsilon Mu (ΔEM, or DEM) was founded on the grounds of Binghamton University in New York during the Spring semester of 1996. The founding fathers united their interests in building a professional co-educational, pre-health fraternity, which could serve Binghamton University students by exposing them to professionals from various health fields. They wanted to inform students about current health issues with the intent of helping students choose a field of interest following graduation and beyond. A large part of the fraternity's interests were focused on serving the community by uniting people of different age groups, ethnicities, religions, and ideologies, who were all joined by a common interest in the health fields.

Our History

In 2010, Delta Epsilon Mu expanded to Washington D.C., where the Iota chapter of the fraternity was established under the same goals as those originally set forth by the founding fathers in 1996. In an effort spearheaded by Tanvi Avasthi, who pledged the Eta Chapter of ΔEM at Virginia Commonwealth University, a pre-health, community service organization was established at GW.  It would later evolve into the foundation of ΔEM history at GW. Members of the Gamma Chapter of the University of Maryland, traveled to The George Washington University to induct the founding class members of the Iota Chapter in the Fall of 2010. The Iota Chapter welcomes all students who are interested in pursuing the health fields: medicine, psychology, dentistry, pharmacy, veterinary medicine, public health, and all other related vocations. As a brotherhood, we have pledged not only to support each other in our various health-field pursuits, but as well as all other aspects of life. Loyalty, dedication, and friendship form the foundation of our fraternity.
summary: The brothers and sisters of Delta Epsilon Mu are committed to serving the community by advocating health education and care, and through active participation in service activities, fundraising, and outreach programs.

name: GW International Students Association
description:
GW International Students Association (GWISA) is a student-led organization with a focus on the holistic international students’ experience for adjustment and engagement. With close collaborations with school departments, other on-campus as well as off-campus organizations, GWISA acts as a bridge to shorten the distance between university services, student organizations, and students with international background through events ranging from professional to social activities. 

 

Want to join us? You can either be a general member supporting us at our many events, or become an E-board member and take an active role in planning events! If you would like to be a general member, all you have to do is join our GW Engage and to stay up to date on our events, follow our Instagram.
summary: GW International Students Association (GWISA) is a student-led organization with a focus on the holistic international students’ experience through events ranging from professional to social activities.

name: Forbidden Planet Productions
description: FPP is a student-run theatre organization that was created in 1994. The company is known for its production of The Rocky Horror Picture Show every Halloween and its Annual Musical Cabaret every spring, along with two full-length musicals each semester. We are dedicated to producing quality shows and having fun, and we are always looking for interested performers, technicians, pit musicians, and audience members!
summary: FPP is a student-run theatre organization that was created in 1994. The company is known for its production of The Rocky Horror Picture Show every Halloween and its Annual Musical Cabaret every spring. FPP puts on a number of musicals and straight plays!

name: International Education Association of the George Washington University
description: The George Washington University International Education Association (IEA) is a student organization whose mission is to advocate for increased awareness of international education through: fostering professional development, providing networking opportunities, and facilitating cultural and service events for students and alumni of GW’s International Education Program (IEP). The IEA also seeks to promote communication between other student organizations and GW as a whole. GOALS: -Increase involvement of IEA and other interested parties -Increase communication with GHSED and IEP -Create organized and efficient communication strategy
summary: The George Washington University International Education Association (IEA) is a student organization whose mission is to advocate for increased awareness of international education through: fostering professional development, providing networking oppor..

name: Circle K International
description:
Circle K International is a student-led service organization that works with local and global partners to create meaningful change. Circle K offers a diverse range of service opportunities, making it easy for students with any schedule to get involved. With no minimum service hour requirement, members are free to serve on their own terms and according to their availability.

Sponsored by the Kiwanis Club of Washington, DC, GW Circle K is committed to cultivating service and leadership while engaging directly with the DC community. Above all, we are an inclusive organization that welcomes students from all backgrounds and ensures every student has a place in the broader GW community.
summary: Circle K International is a student-led service organization that works with local and global partners to create meaningful change. Circle K aims to emphasize growth in service, leadership, and fellowship.

name: GW Turkish Student Association
description: Providing a common environment to establish communication and cooperation between the Turkish Community of GWU and the rest of GWU Community, in addition to promoting Turkish culture to the GWU Community at large. We welcome all members of the GWU Community to join, whether they have ties to Turkey or just want to learn more about the country and culture!
summary: Providing a common environment to establish communication and cooperation between the Turkish Community of GW and the rest of GW Community, in addition to promoting Turkish culture to the GW Community at large.

name: Women In Computer Science
description: The GW student chapter of Women in Computer Science (WiCS) exists to enrich the academic and extracurricular lives of women studying or interested in Computer Science. Through educational and career-focused opportunities, WiCS works to support female-identifying people in the CS field.
summary: The GW student chapter of Women in Computer Science (WiCS) exists to enrich the academic and extracurricular lives of women studying or interested in Computer Science.

name: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers
description:
Links to our social media pages and registrations to our upcoming events is available on our Linktree! 

https://linktr.ee/ieeegwu

The George Washington University IEEE Student Branch was founded in the early 1970's. A diversified group of engineering students have continuously worked towards the promotion of unity between all students, the development of leadership, and the furthering of career building events by networking with the IEEE local section, university faculty, and professionals in the industry.
summary: A diversified group of engineering students who continuously work towards the promotion of unity between all students, the development of leadership, and the furthering of career-building events by networking with the IEEE local section.

name: J Street U at GW
description: A pro-peace movement fighting for human rights, an end to the occupation, and a shared future for Israelis and Palestinians. We are the grassroots student organizing arm of J-Street, an advocacy organization.
summary: We are the grassroots student organizing arm for J Street, an advocacy organization that promotes a two-state solution to the Israeli-Palestinian conflict.

name: Student National Medical Association
description: The Student National Medical Association (SNMA) is a student run, not-for-profit organization dedicated to supporting current and future underrepresented minority medical students, addressing the needs of underserved communities, and increasing the number of clinically excellent, culturally competent and socially conscious physicians.
summary: The Student National Medical Association (SNMA) is a student run, not-for-profit organization dedicated to supporting the development of current and future underrepresented minority medical students, and  addressing the needs of underserved communities.

name: Students for One Health
description: GW Students for One Health supports the global One Health movement, which promotes collaboration among professionals in the health, animal, and environmental science fields. One Health recognizes the inextricable link between human health, animal health, and environmental health. By encouraging interdisciplinary collaboration and communication, we can improve the well-being of our planet and all its species. Students for One Health aims to bring students from different programs together to discuss, learn, and promote the principles and issues pertaining to One Health by fostering interdisciplinary collaboration. We do this through informative GBMs, guest speaker events, volunteer events, and celebrations like Earth Month and One Health Day.
summary: Students for One Health supports the One Health movement, which promotes interdisciplinary collaboration to address the human, animal and environmental health link. We do this with GBMs, guest speakers, volunteering, and celebrations like One Health Day.

name: Alexander Hamilton Society at The George Washington University
description:
The Alexander Hamilton Society at GW is a student-led, nonpartisan organization that fosters intellectual curiosity, professional development, and meaningful community.

 

We help our members build the skills and connections to pursue internships, graduate programs, and careers in academia, business, journalism, public service, and beyond.

 

AHS is a free, open-membership organization—not a fraternity or advocacy group—grounded in a national network of exceptional students, faculty, and professionals.

 

Our chapter welcomes all GW students to weekly conversations on American foreign, economic, and national security policy.

 

Whether you’re looking to deepen your understanding of global affairs, connect with new friends, or explore career paths, there’s a place for you here.

 

We host a podcast, distinguished speakers, reading series, professional outings, mentorship programs, roundtable discussions, and social gatherings.

 

Our academic programming reflects Alexander Hamilton’s belief that “No government could give us tranquility and happiness at home, which did not possess sufficient stability and strength to make us respectable abroad.”

 

Stay up to date with our events, opportunities, and Spotify podcast, The Diplomat’s Desk, through this link! —> https://linktr.ee/ahsatgw
summary: The Alexander Hamilton Society promotes leadership and community grounded in strong, principled U.S. engagement abroad. GW’s chapter, the 2024–25 National Chapter of the Year, offers invaluable career opportunities in the heart of our nation’s capital.

name: Phi Alpha Delta Pre-Law Fraternity
description: Phi Alpha Delta is a professional pre-law fraternity designed in order to form a strong bond uniting students and teachers of the law with members of the Bench and Bar in a fraternal fellowship designed to advance the ideals of liberty and equal justice under law; to stimulate excellence in scholarship; to inspire the virtues of compassion and courage; to foster integrity and professional competence; to promote the welfare of its members; and to encourage their moral, intellectual, and cultural advancement; so that each member may enjoy a lifetime of honorable professional and public service. We are a registered student organization at The George Washington University whose goal is to provide service to the student, the school, the profession and the community. We are an organization that brings together all students interested in pursuing a career in the legal field and provides numerous opportunities for members to acquaint themselves with professionals in the legal field as well as news and events to assist members in their preparation for law school. We are always looking for dedicated, passionate GW students who are interested in pursuing a career in law or attending law school.
summary: Phi Alpha Delta is a professional pre-law fraternity designed in order to form a strong bond uniting students and teachers of the law with members of the Bench and Bar in a fraternal fellowship designed to advance the ideals of liberty and equal justice.

name: Generic Theatre Company
description: Generic Theatre Company, founded in 1985, is The George Washington University’s largest and oldest student theatre company. Each year Generic produces a variety of shows from straight plays to musicals to the annual Freshman Showcase. If you are interested in getting involved we would love to hear from you! No experience is necessary.
summary: The award-winning Generic Theatre Company, founded in 1985, is The George Washington University’s largest and oldest student theatre company. Each year Generic produces a variety of shows from straight plays to musicals to the annual Freshman Showcase....

name: InterVarsity Christian Fellowship
description: InterVarsity Christian Fellowship is a multi-ethnic, multi-cultural, interdenominational Christian fellowship at the George Washington University. We are a community seeking to learn more about Jesus and show His love to campus.  We do this by living out purposeful community with God and one another and by acting as faithful witnesses of Christ through care for our campus and our city. All are welcome, regardless of religious affiliation or background.
summary: InterVarsity Christian Fellowship is a multi-cultural, multi-ethnic, interdenominational Christian fellowship at GW. We are a community seeking to learn more about Jesus and show His love to campus. All are welcome, regardless of religious affiliation!

name: DC Through A Lens
description:
Our mission is to break out of the “Foggy Bottom bubble.” We are fortunate enough to live in such a vibrant city; however, we often don’t get to see all that DC has to offer. DC Through A Lens aims to change that! We explore DC through photography and take members to some of DC’s staples, as well as its hidden treasures. We select each biweekly trip to focus on a different photography technique and a unique location. Whether you only take photos with your phone or have a DSLR, we welcome photographers of all skill levels. We aim to foster a community that shares a passion for travel and photography.

If you're interested in joining, all you have to do is click the blue JOIN button and we'll get you added to our mailing list!
summary: Our mission is to break out of the "Foggy Bottom bubble". We explore DC through judgment-free photography and take members to popular sites and hidden treasures. We also host special events and fundraisers. We welcome photographers of all skill level!

name: Balance: The GW Dance Group
description: Balance: The GW Dance group strives to form a welcoming and supportive community for dancers and the GW community. We seek to provide passionate dancers with opportunities to take and teach classes, and to perform, stage manage, and direct shows. We promote our collective love of dance by helping students find the perfect balance between college academics and the healthy lifestyle that dance supports. We are an all inclusive community composed of beginners through pre-professional level dancers.
summary: Balance: The GW Dance group strives to form a welcoming and supportive community for dancers and the GW community. We seek to provide passionate dancers with opportunities to take and teach classes, and to perform, stage manage, and direct shows.

name: Fourteenth Grade Players
description: For the past twenty four years, Fourteenth Grade Players has prided itself on bringing the talent, inclusivity, and professionalism of the GW student theatre community to the wider student body. Our wide range of hard-hitting productions, both dramatic and comedic, allow any member of the GW community to pursue their interest in the arts through a warm, accepting, enthusiastic, and professional environment. From being a member of the student team involved in directing a production, acting on the stage in one of our four shows each year, designing and executing the myriad technical elements of each production, to simply being an audience member watching the performance unfold, we hope to unite the larger GW and DC community in a shared passion and appreciation for theatre and the arts.
summary: For the past twenty four years, Fourteenth Grade Players has prided itself on bringing the talent, inclusivity, and professionalism of the GW student theatre community to the wider student body. Join one of our acting, tech, or directing teams today!

Join the NAACP, an organization working to ensure the political, educational, social and economic equality of rights of all persons. Only at GW!

Through its strategic initiatives, MBSA opens doors to valuable opportunities, including mentorship, networking, and career development, thereby equipping students with the tools they need to succeed in the business world while simultaneously enriching the academic community with cross-cultural awareness and understanding.

name: Alpha Chi Sigma, Alpha Pi Chapter
description:
Alpha Chi Sigma is a professional, co-ed chemistry fraternity that welcomes students of all majors and seeks to bring together students who enjoy the sciences. 

Our mission is to:

1) To bind its members with a tie of true and lasting friendship.

2) To strive for the advancement of chemistry both as a science and as a profession.

3) To aid its members by every honorable means in the attainment of their ambitions as chemists throughout their mortal lives.

description: Since 1908, The Cherry Tree Yearbook has been preserving the legacy of the GW Community through the publication of its annual yearbook. The yearbook captures, through stories and photographs, the diversity of the GW student body and its activities. As the longest-running publication on campus, The Cherry Tree continues to record and preserve the GW experience for generations to come.
summary: Since 1908, The Cherry Tree Yearbook has been preserving the legacy of the GW Community through the publication of its annual yearbook. The yearbook captures, through stories and photographs, the diversity of the GW student body and its activities.

name: American Foundation for Suicide Prevention - George Washington University Chapter
description: The American Foundation for Suicide Prevention (AFSP) is a non-profit aiming to save lives and bring hope to those affected by suicide. AFSP GW is a student group within the AFSP National Capital Area Chapter. AFSP has four main areas of focus: education, advocacy, research, and support for survivors of suicide loss. AFSP GW is a group of students dedicated to AFSP's mission. We work both on the GW campus and in the greater DC area to save lives and spread hope. By joining our organization, you too can play a crucial role in reducing a major public health issue. As a member of our organization you can partake in advocacy for suicide prevention policy, fundraising events, educational campaigns, and community bonding. Remember, you are not alone and GW is better with you here.
summary: The American Foundation for Suicide Prevention (AFSP) is a non-profit aiming to save lives and bring hope to those affected by suicide. AFSP GW is a student group within the AFSP National Capital Area Chapter.

From participating in not only our own philanthropy, but also other chapters' philanthropies, to fun sisterhood events, we have created a loving, and inclusive bond throughout our sorority. Alone, each Kappa brings unique qualities to campus, but together we make a difference.

name: George's Army
description:
Raising High the Buff and Blue, George's Army is the official student fan section for all of George Washington University Athletics. We are responsible for organizing the student section at all GW Athletics games, and for student engagement.

FAQs:

 

Are all GW Athletics games free to attend?

Yes! All GW Athletics home games are free for students. Just make sure to bring your GWorld card for entry at the Smith Center.

 

Where are GW men's and women's basketball games played?

All home basketball games are played at the Charles E. Smith Center, located in the heart of the Foggy Bottom campus (right across from the School of Business).

 

When does the GW Basketball season start?

Non-conference play typically tips off in early November (with occasional exhibition games in mid-to-late October), followed by the start of Atlantic 10 conference games in late December.

 

What is the bag policy at the Smith Center?

The Charles E. Smith Center implemented a no bags policy ahead of the 2025-26 season, so be sure to drop your bags off at your dorm before the games! Learn more: https://gwsports.com/sports/2025/5/30/no-bag-policy

 

Do I need to reserve a ticket in advance as a student?

Nope! Seating is first-come-first serve within the dedicated student section.

 

Is there a specific entrance for students?

Yes! For men's basketball games the student section entrance is located at Gate 5 (on F and 23rd Street); for women's games the entrance is Gate 2 (on G and 22nd Street).

Does George’s Army go to games besides Basketball?

Yes! We support all GW Athletics.
summary: Raising High the Buff and Blue, we are the official student fan section for all of George Washington University Athletics.

summary: The Graduate Finance Society is student organization dedicated to educating graduate students about various sectors of the financial services industry as well as assisting them in their pursuit to obtain employment in the financial services industry.

name: LATAM@GW
description: LATAM@GW is a professional, research and social forum dedicated to bringing together students who share a broadly defined interest in Latin America and the Caribbean and promoting awareness and knowledge of the region’s trials, contributions and diversity. We advocate for solidarity among all Latin American-focused student organizations by strengthening student connections to the Latin American and Caribbean Studies Initiative academic program and providing an intellectual forum for open dialogue about Latin America-related topics at The George Washington University.
summary: LATAM@GW is a professional, research, and social forum dedicated to bringing together students who share an interest in Latin America & the Caribbean and promoting awareness and knowledge of the region’s trials, contributions, and diversity.

name: Onero GW
description:
The Onero Institute is a youth-driven, nonprofit research organization that seeks to develop the next generation of leaders and foreign affairs experts by bridging the divide between young people and the academic community and by better engaging youth and the world community on international issues. We offer support and a platform for aspiring young professionals to improve upon and share their work. We place special emphasis on creating intergenerational dialogues and discussing underrepresented and youth issues. This chapter of the Onero Institute seeks to engage the GW and Washington, D.C. communities with the aforementioned mission and strives to empower the voices of students and young people.

Link to Application

The Onero Community
summary: The Onero Institute is a youth-driven, nonprofit research organization that seeks to develop the next generation of leaders by bridging the divide between young people and the academic community and by better engaging youth on international issues.

name: receSs
description:
receSs is the only sketch and improv comedy group at The George Washington University and is considered one of the oldest comedy groups in Washington, DC. Their monthly shows include video sketches, live sketches and long form improv. Their annual comedy festival, SLATE: The World College Comedy Festival host other collegiate groups as well as a professional headliner. Recent performers for SLATE have included Troy Bond, Pete Holmes, Michelle Wolf, Kate Berlant, Joe Mande, and Upright Citizens Brigade Tour Co. 

receSs takes new members through auditions in September, and they will hold auditions in the fall of 2023. To get to know more about the group, follow them on Instagram @gwurecess and like their Facebook page @gwrecess. Their shows are announced on their Facebook Page as well as their Instagram. DMs are always open to questions/comments/introductions!
summary: receSs is the only student sketch and improv comedy group at GW and is considered one of the oldest comedy groups in Washington, DC. They hold monthly shows with long form improv and sketch comedy.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi
description: Alpha Kappa Psi is the world’s oldest and largest professional business fraternity. The Beta Mu chapter of Alpha Kappa Psi was installed at the George Washington University on May 6, 1933. In the 90 years, since, AKPsi has been an active part of the university and local community, hosting innovative and large-scale professional development, philanthropic, and social impact programs. Each year, AKPsi co-hosts the Ready, Set, Grow! career conference, the largest student-run conference in the Northeast, and organizes a career trek to New York City. AKPsi members are actively involved in student leadership, standing for the values of Brotherhood, Unity, Integrity, Knowledge, and Service. The chapter is always welcome to new members for two new member classes each year. New members undertake a rigorous professional development program that prepares them to be principled business leaders. With extraordinarily active alumni, Alpha Kappa Psi prides itself on excellence career and higher education placement.
summary: Alpha Kappa Psi is GW's premier professional fraternity. Open to all majors, AKPsi promotes professional development and philanthropy here on campus and across the world.

name: Delta Phi Epsilon Professional Foreign Service Society

Today, we provide academic and professional resources to our members who hail from all around the world, brought together by our common interests in international affairs and service, while inculcating a strong sense of community within our organization. While we have been a historically all-male organization, we now consider anyone regardless of gender identity for membership.
summary: We're GW's oldest professional student organization dedicated to providing resources to students interested in International Affairs, the Foreign Service, and any adjacent fields. We are a co-ed organization.

name: English Graduate Student Association
description: The English Graduate Student Association (EGSA) is both a professional and a social organization of graduate students in or affiliated with The George Washington University Department of English. Abiding by university policy, EGSA represents graduate interests on department faculty committees and, more generally, provides graduate students with a collective voice in departmental affairs. Additionally, EGSA works with graduate students from other departments in sponsoring and coordinating interdisciplinary activities for the professional development of its members. EGSA also sponsors various social activities that foster a sense of community for its members, while also providing newer students with access to students further along in the program who can serve as mentors. To this end, EGSA pursues three directives: 1) To expand participation of English graduate students in campus governance and to lobby effectively for graduate student concerns with the Department of English and the University; 2) To encourage and facilitate graduate students' academic and professional efforts; 3) To foster collegiality and fellowship among English graduate students with each other as well as with other departments in the Columbian College of Arts and Sciences (CCAS), including but not limited to graduate students, faculty, and staff.
summary: The English Graduate Student Association (EGSA) is both a professional and a social organization of graduate students in or affiliated with The George Washington University Department of English.

description:
The GW Pre-Law Student Association is an umbrella organization that helps members prepare themselves for a career in the legal field. Through PLSA, members have access to speaker events, mock Law School Admission Test (LSAT) sessions, LSAT test preparation discounts, and the ability to improve their legal writing and research through two flagship publications, the Undergraduate Law Review and the Justice Journal.

The mission of the organization is to provide undergraduate students with the opportunity to network, inspire professional ethics, promote scholastic excellence, understand and excel in the law school application process, and explore different aspects of the legal profession. Being involved in the Pre-Law Student Association will open many doors and help members be fully prepared to apply for and succeed in law school. Learn more here at gwprelaw.com.
summary: The GW Pre-Law Student Association is an umbrella organization that helps members prepare themselves for a career in the legal field by providing them with the tools to succeed and opportunities to up their legal writing and research skills.

The JEC is governed by the JEC charter, which can be located under files. If you have a question, please complete the contact form on the contact page, or email the JEC directly at jec@gwu.edu. If you are filing a complaint or need to get in contact with the investigative staff, please email jec@gwu.edu.
summary: The JEC is responsible for organizing and administering the student body elections at the George Washington University. The JEC is governed by the JEC charter, which can be located under files. If you have a question, please complete the contact form o...

name: George Washington Socially Responsible Investment Fund
description:
At GWSRIF, members will evaluate publicly traded companies based on a set of parameters detailing social responsibility and analyze their financial status in order to select and maintain a socially and ethically responsible investment portfolio. SRIF provides a platform of engagement for students interested in learning the basics of portfolio management, investing in the US stock market and teaching a hands on approach to the skills that future investment bankers and asset managers and will need. Further, the members of the Portfolio will adhere to investing the capital of the fund in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the Investment Parameters. In the long term, the group is to donate their yearly profits back to the GWSB in the form of a scholarship under the name of the donators in the endowment. Overall, the group looks to maintain a balance between profit and social responsibility, as well as a balance between faculty/advisor support and student autonomy as well as provide students with resources to help them achieve and excel in financial careers including investment banking, management consulting and ESG investing. 

Please fill out the interest form on our website (gwsrif.org) if you are interested in joining us and we will reach out to you during our recruitment cycle. Additionally, please feel free to reach out to gwsrif@gwu.edu.
summary: The objective of GWSRIF is for members to evaluate the policies of publicly traded companies based on a set of social responsibility parameters and financial metrics in order to maintain a sustainable equity portfolio.

The GW Tabletop Gaming Society strives to provide a non-judgemental, cohesive community for individuals with interests in the varied forms of tabletop gaming, whilst promoting an environment of healthy competition, and providing support for individuals seeking to compete in official tournaments and events. The GW TGS also aims to work in conjunction with local businesses in order to raise funds for educational tabletop games in support of neighborhood schools and the community.

Join us here on Engage to be added to our mailing list, where you can learn about upcoming events!

We recommend that you join our Discord to keep in touch. Discord link: https://discord.gg/6Uy76XhFqV
summary: The GW Tabletop Gaming Society strives to provide a nonjudgmental, cohesive community for individuals with interests in the varied forms of tabletop, board, and card gaming while promoting an environment of healthy competition.

name: Black Women's Forum
