summary: The Black Women's Forum (BWF) is a safe space that fosters sisterhood, support, friendship and communication for Black women at GW. Through BWF, black women on campus have the opportunity to grow and express themselves through their shared experiences.

name: The George Washington University Bands
description:
GW Bands is an inclusive instrumental music organization at the George Washington University dedicated to producing quality musical performances, promoting school spirit, fostering strong friendships, and serving the community. 

 

GW Bands consists of three instrumental ensembles: Wind Ensemble, Symphonic Band, and the Foghorns Pep Band. All three ensembles rehearse twice a week on Tuesdays and Thursdays during the school year. The two concert bands, Wind Ensemble and Symphonic Band, make up the University Band class and perform at least one concert a semester. The Foghorns Pep Band performs at every home women's and men's basketball game as well at various events around campus throughout the year. In addition to playing music, we love to socialize and have fun together! 

 

University Band:

GW Bands is the home of the Wind Ensemble and Symphonic Band, auditioned concert band ensembles in the George Washington University’s Corcoran School of the Arts & Design. Both ensembles rehearse on Tuesdays and Thursdays. The ensembles perform classic, contemporary and newly composed wind music with on-campus performances in the Fall and Spring. Membership in both groups is open to students from all schools and departments at GW and requires instructor approval via audition. Anyone who auditions for University Band is automatically welcomed into Symphonic Band; auditions are for seat placement and acceptance into Wind Ensemble. If accepted into Wind Ensemble, a student can choose to play in one or both ensembles. Auditions are held at the beginning of the Fall semester as well as at the beginning of the Spring semester and and on a case by case basis for anyone who joins throughout the year. 

 

Wind Ensemble meets Tuesdays and Thursdays from 3:45 - 5:00 PM ET. Symphonic Band meets Tuesdays and Thursdays from 5:10 - 6:25 PM ET.

 

Foghorns Pep Band:

The Foghorns Pep Band is a student organization that works with the Music Department and Athletics to put on a variety of performances around campus. We are an enthusiastic, passionate group of musicians who perform pop hits, classic rock favorites, and other modern songs to pump up our teams and energize GW fans. As the university's pep band, the Foghorns is the soundtrack to all men's and women's home basketball games. We can also be seen performing at various events and spots on campus, from performing in Kogan Plaza to get students excited for a big game to playing a fight song by the hippo to welcome newly accepted students to campus. The Foghorns also perform at the A-10 conference and other national tournaments during March Madness! The Foghorns are open to all GW students, but members must also participate in at least one of the University Band ensembles. As an added bonus, Foghorn members receive an award added to their financial aid for their participation. This award increases with each year you participate in The Foghorns.

 

Foghorns meets every Tuesday and Thursday night from 7:10-8:10 PM ET. 

 

Being a part of our two concert bands, Symphonic Band and Wind Ensemble, and/or the Foghorns Pep Band is an incredible way to make friends, memories, and music. You DO NOT need to own an instrument to participate in or audition for Band. No one will be excluded from participating in band due to financial or any other barriers. We would absolutely love to have you!!

If you are interested in joining GW Bands, please reach out to band@gwu.edu or request to join here.
summary: GW Bands is an inclusive instrumental music organization at the George Washington University dedicated to producing quality musical performances, promoting school spirit, fostering strong friendships, and serving the community.

name: Commercial Real Estate Network
description: Our mission is to empower students at the George Washington University to pursue a career in real estate. Through networking events, informational panels, site visits and peer-to-peer discussion, we hope that students will be able to navigate through the various sectors of commercial real estate and find a profession, and even a company, that interests them.
summary: We attempt to encourage and spread real estate knowledge here at GW. We connect students and alumni through networking events, panel discussions, and career treks to help foster an excitement for real estate among GW's undergraduate population.

name: Excellence in Student Life Awards
description: The Excellence in Student Life Awards are an annual tradition at the George Washington University. The Awards allow the campus community to come together to celebrate the amazing achievements accomplished during each academic year. The Excellence Awards specifically recognize the many faculty, staff, students, and student organizations on campus that have made a meaningful impact on campus in the past year. Nominations for Awards open on February 8 and will close on March 12. All Awards nominations are accepted through this Engage portal. Award winners will be announced in April.
summary: The Excellence in Student Life Awards are an annual tradition at the George Washington University. The Awards allow the campus community to come together to celebrate the amazing achievements accomplished during each academic year. The Excellence Award...

name: Undergraduate Business Association
description:
The Undergraduate Business Association (UBA) is the overarching umbrella organization that coordinates the leadership of all GW School of Business (GWSB)-Affiliated Undergraduate Student Organizations. We serve as a liaison between the GWSB undergraduate student body and GWSB faculty and staff to elevate student voices and perspectives, provide opportunities for leadership development, and create meaningful community. 

 

Guided by our values of integrity, community, and development, the UBA is composed of an executive board of student leaders dedicated to enhancing the undergraduate experience. The UBA’s general body membership is made up of the UBA Leadership Council, representing each of the GWSB-Affiliated Organizations, members of UBA-led initiatives, such as the First-Year Cohort and UBA Ambassadors, and the general GWSB undergraduate population. The UBA works with and serves all GWSB undergraduate students, regardless of student organization membership or involvement.

 

To learn more about us, visit our UBA Website.

 

To engage with the GWSB community, connect with our GWSB-Affiliated Undergraduate Student Organizations.
summary: The Undergraduate Business Association is an umbrella body that unites and advocates for all constituents of the GWSB community by supporting it's GWSB Affiliated Student Organizations and helping them reach their full potential.

name: International Affairs Review
description: IAR is a graduate student-run publication of The George Washington University's Elliott School of International Affairs in Washington, D.C., devoted to providing a forum for graduate student publication in international affairs and related fields, and graduate student training in academic writing and editing, via the twin outlets of a twice-yearly academic journal and a constantly updated news and op-ed website.
summary: IAR is a graduate student-run publication of The George Washington University's Elliott School of International Affairs in Washington, D.C., devoted to providing a forum for graduate student publication in international affairs and related fields.

name: African Students Association
description:
George Washington University’s African Student Association is a student led organization focused on fostering a safe space for the celebration and recognition of African cultures.

We host a variety of academic, social and service events year round that allow for students from all over the continent to connect and build long lasting relationships.
summary: George Washington University’s African Student Association is a student led organization focused on fostering a safe space for the celebration and recognition of African cultures.

name: GW Mock Trial
description:
Ever wish you could channel your inner Elle Woods, giving iconic courtroom speeches and bringing the jury to your side? Or maybe you dream of being the next Saul Goodman, using your quick wits to sway the case. As GW Mock Trial, we channel our favorite legal drama characters by taking on the roles of attorneys and witnesses and arguing fictionalized cases against other universities.

 

But we are more than just a team: we are a family united by our love for the law and drive to succeed. We create lasting friendships and build lifelong memories that extend far beyond our time at GW. We are relentless in our pursuit of excellence. We are GW Mock Trial.

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/t0jul5-PyoA?si=ir2ru1ClqGsNJqwf" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>
summary: Mock Trial is a public speaking activity focused on trial advocacy. Through intercollegiate mock trial competitions, we advance legal education among undergraduate students.

name: Sports Business Association
description: The George Washington University's Sports Business Association is made up of both undergraduate and graduate students at GW interested in the Sports Industry. We work to assist students, regardless of their field of study, to get engaged and informed about one of the world's most exciting and largest industries.  The sports industry involves the use of analytics, marketing, financial and legal practices, and more. There are opportunities to work with sport teams, leagues, athletes, organizations (e.g. youth, collegiate, Olympic), Esports, sport media, venues, and sporting good companies (Nike, Adidas, Under Armour). The SBA also assists in the execution of the annual Sports Industry Networking and Career (SINC) conference which attracts over 100 industry executives to campus.
summary: SBA is made up of both undergraduate and graduate students at GW interested in the field of sports management. The organization works to promote the Sport Management program in the School of Business and to get students engaged and informed about sports.

name: Women and Youth Supporting Each Other
description:
Women and Youth Supporting Each Other (WYSE) is a national, curriculum-based mentorship program that provides women, females, and non-binary 6th-8th graders with the resources, information, and support necessary to make informed and empowered decisions about their relationships, sexuality, and futures. Once a week, we hold sessions at two local middle schools, Jefferson Middle School and Eliot Hine Middle School, which include various discussions, activities, games, and occasional field trips relating to each weekly topic. We use an up-to-date and program-specific curriculum to plan and build weekly sessions, tailored to the unique needs of our mentees. Our WYSE mission is to empower young women by providing the resources and support necessary to make positive life choices and create community change. WYSE envisions a world in which all women are empowered to determine their future and effect change.

Time Commitment: 

Eliot Hine meets Wednesdays and Jefferson meets on Tuesdays for sessions. We ask mentors to block out 2:30 PM - 5:30 PM on one of these days to spend with mentees (this time frame includes travel time between schools). We also meet once a week (on Sundays) for reflections on our session.
summary: Women and Youth Supporting Each Other (WYSE) is a curriculum-based mentorship program that provides young women with the resources, information and support necessary to make positive life choices and create community change.

name: Forté Organization at GW
description: Forté Organization at GW is a student organization dedicated to the Forté Foundation’s mission to launch women into fulfilling significant careers by providing access to business education, opportunities and a community of successful women. We strive to raise awareness of women's impact on the business, societal, cultural and economic environments.
summary: The George Washington Forté Organization is a student organization dedicated to the Forté Foundation’s mission to launch women into fulfilling significant careers through access to business education and a community of successful women.

name: George Washington Student Organization of Biomedical Scientists
description:
GW Student Organization of Biomedical Scientists (GW-SOBS) serves as a student government to provide a forum for the advancement of and advocacy of PhD students in the Institute of Biomedical Sciences (IBS) program intellectually, professionally, academically, and socially. 

Do you have concerns or suggestions for GW-SOBS? Provide them anonymously through our Suggestion Box!
summary: GW Student Organization of Biomedical Scientists (GW-SOBS) serves as a student government forum for the advancement of and advocacy for Institute of Biomedical Sciences (IBS) PhD students intellectually, professionally, academically, and socially.

name: Medical School Class of 2022 Student Council
description: The Medical School Class of 2022 Student Council fundraising holding account.
summary: The Medical School Class of 2022 Student Council fundraising holding account.

name: GW French Club
description: We bring together students passionate about French language and francophone cultures, creating an inclusive environment to enhance multicultural competencies. Join us for French cuisine, café outings, museum tours, study abroad advising, volunteer opportunities, and more! All levels of French speakers are welcome to join!
summary: Our goal is to bring together students who share a common interest in the French language and the cultures of French-speaking countries in order to gain a better understanding of the similarities and intricacies that unite the francophone world.

name: GW Raas
description: GW Raas is our nationally competitive Garba-Raas dance team. They perform a popular folk dance native to the state of Gujarat, located in Western India called, Garba-Raas. They’ve performed in cities such as Austin, Texas, St. Louis, Missouri, Atlanta, Georgia, and Richmond, Virginia. As they prepare for another competition season, they strive to promote and spread awareness of South Asian culture.
summary: Raas/Garba is a popular folk dance native to the state of Gujarat, located in Western India. As a competitive Indian performing arts group, we strive to promote South Asian culture through performances on campus and at competitions across the country.

name: Black Student Union
description: Founded in 1968, The George Washington University Black Student Union’s goal, above all else, is to create a Black experience where all Black students feel supported, welcomed, advocated for, and cared for. The Black Student Union achieves this through programming, both professional and social, service, public forums for discussion, and collaboration across organizations and identities. It is our duty, as the organization overseeing Black life at GW, to create a space where every Black student can be successful, feel heard, and most importantly be proud to be a Black student at GW. The Black Student Union does not waiver in this responsibility, and will do whatever is necessary to protect the interests of Black students at this institution.
summary: Founded in 1968, The George Washington University Black Student Union’s goal, above all else, is to create a Black experience where all Black students feel supported, welcomed, advocated for, and cared for.

name: Delta Lambda Phi
description:
Delta Lambda Phi is the first ever fraternity for queer men and trans people. It was founded here, in Washington, DC, in 1986, and quickly spread across the United States and Canada. Today, the organization has 11 active chapters, from CSU-Long Beach to NYU, and, of course, here at the George Washington University!

The Alpha Chapter, originally a community-based organization in DC, rechartered in 2018 in its new home at the George Washington University.

DLP is not your typical fraternity - we are a family! We build a tight-knit, diverse, and loving brotherhood through recreational, social, and service events. Examples of some of our most-loved events are our picnics all across the beautiful city of Washington, our brotherhood bonding retreats, and of course, our charity drag show! Come join us! DLP is a friend of all, and a brother for many.

Join us for Rush in the Fall or Spring!

Contact us on social media or at alpha.chapter@dlp.org for more information.

Instagram: @dlpgw

Facebook: facebook.com/gwdeltalambdaphi/
summary: Welcome to Delta Lambda Phi Social Fraternity! Since 1986, Delta Lambda Phi (DLP) has offered queer men and trans people the opportunity to grow in the true spirit of brotherhood — one that embraces diversity and respects the value of all.

name: Hawaiʻi Club
description:
E komo mai! Welcome to the Hawaiʻi Club, a support network for students from Hawaiʻi and anyone interested in learning about the unique culture of the islands. Our mission is to foster a sense belonging at The George Washington University by sharing and celebrating the rich heritage, traditions, and values of Hawaiʻi and the broader Pacific Islands. Through our events, we hope to educate the GW community about the vibrant history and customs while also providing a home away from home for students far from the islands.

Throughout the year, we host a variety of events and activities that showcase the spirit of aloha and immerse our members in the cultural traditions of Hawaiʻi. Some of our signature events include:

  • Annual Lūʻau: Our biggest event of the year, featuring Hawaiian cuisine, music, and performances that bring the warmth and hospitality of the islands to the GW campus.
  • Hula Workshops: An opportunity to learn and experience the art of hula, a storytelling dance that reflects the history and heart of Hawaiʻi.
  • Cultural Exchanges: We collaborate with other AAPI student organizations to celebrate the diverse cultures of the Pacific and foster cross-cultural understanding.
  • Spam Musubi & Ramen Nights: A taste of home with classic comfort foods from Hawaiʻi, offering students a chance to connect over familiar flavors and good company.

Whether you’re from Hawaiʻi, have ties to the islands, or simply want to learn more about our culture, we invite you to join us. The Hawaiʻi Club is a place where you can embrace the aloha spirit, make lifelong connections, and experience the traditions that make our islands so special.

Here is our 2025-26 Interest Form!

Mahalo nui loa, and we look forward to welcoming you into our ʻohana!
summary: E komo mai! Welcome to the Hawaiʻi Club, a support network for college students originating from Hawaiʻi. Our goal is to educate The George Washington University community about the culture and heritage of Hawaiʻi, the Pacific Islands, and its people.

name: The Ethiopian-Eritrean Students Association
description: The Ethiopian-Eritrean Students Association at The George Washington University (GW) is an organization dedicated to the enhancement of the Ethiopian and Eritrean community at GW and throughout the DMV. Our events are intended to showcase the rich and diverse cultures of Eritrea and Ethiopia, increase awareness of Eritrean and Ethiopian regional affairs, and contribute to the social, academic, and professional development of our members. Surrounded by one of the largest Ethiopian and Eritrean diaspora populations in the world, we also seek to serve the local diaspora community and highlight their unique contributions to the greater Washington metropolitan area.
summary: The Ethiopian-Eritrean Students Association at The George Washington University (GW) is an organization dedicated to the enhancement of the Ethiopian and Eritrean community at GW and throughout the DMV.

name: Association of Curriculum and Pedagogy Students
description: Our mission is to cultivate within our group a caring, intellectually curious, and engaged group of scholars equal to the challenges within our field. We engage our membership, our faculty and others who are part of the lifelong learning community at GSEHD, and those within the broader education community, by encouraging new dialogue, thinking, writing and research that speaks to needs of students and teachers.
summary: Our mission is to cultivate within our group a caring, intellectually curious, and engaged group of scholars equal to the challenges within our field. We engage our membership, our faculty and others who are part of the lifelong learning community.

name: Merriweather Hall
description:
Merriweather Hall is a first-year, co-ed residence hall on the Mount Vernon Campus, offering a small, close-knit living experience. Most rooms are double bedrooms with adjoined bathrooms, creating a shared space that fosters strong friendships.

Each fully furnished Merriweather room includes desks, desk chairs, dressers, and twin beds. The hall features a basement lounge with a TV, laundry facilities, and a community kitchen, where residents can cook together and share favorite recipes from home.

 

When the weather is nice, students enjoy hanging out outside Merriweather, cheering on lacrosse and soccer games on Mount Vernon’s athletic fields, or enjoying the campus’ green spaces.
summary: Merriweather Hall, an all-female first-year and upperclass student living option on Mount Vernon campus, has a maximum occupancy of 43 students in mainly double bedrooms with adjoined bathrooms. Fully furnished, each Merriweather room offers students d...

name: Somers Hall
description: Somers Hall, a first-year student coed living option on the Mount Vernon campus, has a maximum occupancy of 246 students in single rooms, double suites with shared bathrooms and double rooms. A limited number of single rooms offer private bathrooms while double rooms share a bathroom with another double room. Somers features a study room on each floor, a community kitchen and laundry facilities. The lower level has a television, DVD player, and a foosball table, making it a great place to relax and catch up with friends. Somers ideal location in the center of campus makes it easy to check out the annual Octoberfest celebration and to watch a movie screening on the Quad. Somers is is also home to students in the Women's Leadership Program academic residential community.
summary: Somers Hall, a first-year student coed living option on the Mount Vernon campus, has a maximum occupancy of 246 students in single rooms, double suites with shared bathrooms and double rooms. Somers is the home to students in the Women's Leadership Pro...

name: GW Shakespeare Company
description: GW Shakespeare Company is a theater troupe dedicated to excellence in its performance of the works of Shakespeare and to the entertainment of its audience. GW Shakespeare hopes to provide an outlet for creative expression as well as create a community of dedicated and enthusiastic members who desire to use their talents to put on high quality productions.
summary: GW Shakespeare Company is a theater troupe dedicated to excellence in its performance of the works of Shakespeare and to the entertainment of its audience. GW Shakespeare hopes to provide an outlet for creative expression as well as create a community...

name: GW Undergraduate Consulting Group
description: The GW Undergraduate Consulting Group equips students with deep industry insights while offering an array of pro-bono consulting services to clients.
summary: GW Undergraduate Consulting Group (GW UCG) serves to provide distinguished undergraduates at The George Washington University with the tools necessary for entering and succeeding in the consulting industry.

name: Center for Career Services
description:
The George Washington University Center for Career Services offers comprehensive career development, experiential education and student employment services and resources for GW degree-seeking students, alumni and international exchange students. For employers, we offer a variety of services, programs and resources to help them meet their hiring and recruitment needs.

 

Through personalized services such as career coaching, self-assessment, online resume and cover letter development and assistance with networking and interviewing, the Center offers a variety of ways to help GW students discover their talents and interests, decide on an academic major, explore industries and career options and create an effective job search strategy that will prepare them for their transition into the world of work or graduate school.

 

To supplement our individualized services, the Center also features a variety of events and programs such as career and internship fairs, specialized industry career expos, skills workshops and industry-expert panels, employer information sessions, company site visits, and in-house employer consultations.
summary: The George Washington University Center for Career Services offers comprehensive career development, experiential education and student employment services and resources for GW degree-seeking students, alumni and international exchange students.

name: Student Org Staff Advisors
summary: Student Organization Staff Advisors

name: Organization for International Development
description: Since its founding in 1992, The Organization of International Development (OID) has been the leading student group examining and promoting the theory and practice of international development at The George Washington University Elliott School of International Affairs. Each year, OID hosts a variety of events on campus and around Washington, including: roundtable discussions with academic leaders, development practitioners, and students; presentations and lectures by scholars, ambassadors, journalists, and field workers; small development talks guided by students’ and professors’ professional experiences; capstone mixer to identify groups for the IDS Program Capstone Project; career and networking workshops; film screenings; and social events.
summary: The OID was founded for students at the ESIA, who share an interest in international development. It is a common platform to share their thoughts and ideas and to participate in and learn from informative events on topics of relevance in the field.

name: Organization of Asian Studies
description:
The Organization of Asian Studies (OAS) is a graduate student organization and think tank at The George Washington University dedicated to exploring and analyzing Indo-Pacific security, politics, economics, history, culture, and various other aspects of international affairs.

Our mission is to foster a deeper understanding of the Indo-Pacific region among the GW community
and with those whose studies or work focuses on the Indo-Pacific in the greater Washington area.
OAS looks to increase awareness of the issues affecting the various countries of Asia, highlight
interesting and important subject matter not often covered in classes, and promote an
understanding of these issues from a global perspective.
summary: OAS is a graduate student organization and think tank dedicated to fostering a deeper understanding about the Indo-Pacific and bridging the gap between classrooms and policy.

name: Transgender and Non-Binary Students of GWU
summary: As GW's only trans-centered student organization, TNBS strives to create a safe space for transgender, non-binary, gender non-conforming, and questioning students through social, educational, and advocacy programming.

name: RHA Hall Councils
description:
The Page for all RHA Hall Council Members. 

Mission Statement: The George Washington University Residence Hall Association supports and improves residence life through advocacy and community building.

Vision Statement: A true home for every resident.
summary: The Page for all RHA Hall Council Members. Mission Statement: The George Washington University Residence Hall Association supports and improves residence life through advocacy and community building.Vision Statement: A true home for every resident.

name: GW TRAiLS
description: GW TRAiLS is George Washington University’s outdoor adventure and leadership program that is committed to connecting the GW community to the outdoors. Join GW TRAiLS at our bi-weekly general body meetings, on one of our weekend day trips, or on an extended break for a longer immersive outdoor experience! Visit us at https://gwu.dserec.com/online/eventsr to sign-up for upcoming trips!
summary: GW TRAiLS is The George Washington University’s outdoor adventure and leadership program, and we are committed to connecting the GW community to the outdoors!

name: FSK Hall
description: Francis Scott Key (FSK) Hall houses approximately 160 first year students in single, 2nd -person units. All apartments include a full kitchen, private bathroom, beds, desks, dressers, high-speed Internet per occupant, and air conditioning
summary: Francis Scott Key (FSK) Hall houses approximately 160 first-year students in single, and 2-person units. All apartments include a full kitchen, private bathroom, beds, desks, dressers.

name: West Hall
description: West Hall opened in the Fall of 2010 as George Washington University’s first LEED residence hall and campus life center on the Mount Vernon campus. West Hall houses 287 first-year and upperclass students, in single and double bedrooms in 4-person units. Living at West Hall provides students access to an in-house fitness center, state of the art recording studios, individual rehearsal space, as well as laundry facilities located on each floor, and more than 10 student study rooms. For those students interested in the fine arts, West Hall also provides students with a black-box theater and an art studio. Immersed in beautiful greenery, West Hall encompasses a courtyard that provides students with ample outside areas where they can study or socialize while enjoying great food from the dining hall. Overlooking the beautiful tennis courts and athletic fields, West Hall offers residents quick access to the Mount Vernon Shuttle. It's also a short walk to the soccer/lacrosse field where you can watch GW's varsity teams or you can enjoy a pickup game of your own.
summary: West Hall opened in the Fall of 2010 as George Washington University’s first LEED residence hall and campus life center on the Mount Vernon campus. West Hall houses 287 first-year and upperclass students, in single and double bedrooms in 4-person units...

name: Fulbright Hall
description:
Home to approximately 240 second-year residents, Fulbright Hall, was built in 1950 and renovated in 2016. Fulbright Hall has: 

  • 8 Residential Floors
  • 3- Person and 4- Person Studio style rooms with kitchen and private bathroom within the unit
  • Two lounges and laundry facilities are located in the basement level of the building
  • Outdoor bike racks located by the entrance of the building
  • All rooms come furnished with a twin XL bed/bed frame, desk/desk chair, fridge, and 2 dressers for each resident
  • Fulbright is also conveniently located one block from Gelman Library, the Academic Center, Smith Center athletic facilities, the School of Engineering and Applied Science, and the Lerner Health and Wellness Center.
summary: Fulbright Hall is home to approximately 240 second-year students and has 3 or 4 person studio style rooms.

name: Center for Undergraduate Fellowships & Research
description:
Housed in Gelman Library, a unit within Libraries and Academic Innovation, the Center for Undergraduate Fellowships and Research provides individualized and group mentorship to all GW students and alumni pursuing national awards, fellowships, and undergraduate research opportunities.

Mission

Empowering scholars through culturally responsive mentoring, the Center for Undergraduate Fellowships and Research will serve all George Washington University undergraduates and alumni pursuing intellectual development through scholarship and fellowship competitions and undergraduate and postgraduate research participation.

Vision

To become a premier academic unit at GW and in the District of Columbia that nurtures diverse thought leaders to become global citizens who positively change the world through their scholarship.

Learning Outcomes

After working with staff in the Center for Undergraduate Fellowships and Research on a national award application or research opportunity, you will:

  • develop a well-constructed working knowledge of undergraduate and postgraduate nationally competitive awards and research experiences that serve as funding sources for academic and professional opportunities;
  • effectively identify opportunities that inform and enhance scholarly productivity such as undergraduate research, internships, community leadership, language study programs, study abroad, and civic engagement;
  • write a personal statement that demonstrates consideration of academic and career goals to a specific audience;
  • develop verbal communication skills that demonstrate consideration of academic and career goals to a specific audience;
  • enhance career self-efficacy to promote deliberate decisions; and
  • develop or further develop a growth mindset.
summary: The Center for Undergraduate Fellowships and Research provides individualized and group mentorship to all GW undergraduate students and alumni pursuing national awards, fellowships, and undergraduate research opportunities.

name: No Lost Generation George Washington University
description:
No Lost Generation at The George Washington University (NLG GWU) is a student organization dedicated to advocating for refugees. We work to support those affected by global refugee crises and are particularly concerned with increasing access to education for displaced and refugee youth. Our organization is the first collegiate chapter to support the global No Lost Generation Initiative, which began in September of 2015 as a partnership between the State Department, UNICEF, and the Elliott School. The four goals of our initiative are:


1) Fundraising to support aid organizations and NGOs,
2) Volunteering with refugee communities and providing support for volunteer programs,
3) Working to educate ourselves and our campus on refugee issues,
4) Involving GW students in advocating—particularly lobbying—for refugee issues.

We hold general body meetings twice per month, host regular events, connect students to volunteer and professional opportunities, and more. Visit our website, follow along on our Instagram, and sign up for our newsletter for updates!

 

Office Hours:

Monday: 6:00pm-7:30pm

Tuesday: 9:00am-11:00am, 10:30am-12:00pm, 3:45pm-4:30pm

Wednesday: 2:00pm-3:30pm, 4:00pm-6:00pm

Thursday: 9:00am-11:00am, 3:45pm-4:30pm
summary: No Lost Generation GW is a student organization dedicated to advocating for refugees. We work to support those affected by global refugee crises and are particularly concerned with increasing access to education for refugees.

name: Honey W Nashman Center for Civic Engagement and Public Service
description: The Honey W. Nashman Center for Civic Engagement and Public Service aims to integrate civic engagement into George Washington University’s educational work. We focus GW’s resources to meet community needs beyond the campus, promote active citizenship in a diverse democracy, and enhance teaching, learning and scholarship at GW.
summary: The Honey W. Nashman Center for Civic Engagement and Public Service aims to integrate civic engagement into George Washington University’s educational work. We focus GW’s resources to meet community needs beyond the campus, promote active citizenship i...

name: Leading Women of Tomorrow GW
description: GW Leading Women of Tomorrow is a student run, nonpartisan initiative that seeks to bridge the gender gap in leadership, whether that be in the public or private sector. We work to equip young and aspiring professionals with the tools they need to succeed in their goals and a supportive network. Here is our Group me link: https://groupme.com/join_group/110675966/zqzk9jrI
summary: GW Leading Women of Tomorrow is a student run, nonpartisan initiative that seeks to bridge the gender gap in leadership.

name: The George Washington University Student Nurses' Association
description: To contribute to nursing education in order to provide for the highest quality of health care and create leaders in nursing. To provide programs representative of fundamental interests and concerns to nursing students. To aid in the development of the whole person, his/her professional role, and his/her responsibility for health care of people in all walks of life. To foster fellowship, unity, and camaraderie amongst nursing students at The George Washington University.
summary: To contribute to nursing education in order to provide for the highest quality of health care and create leaders in nursing.To provide programs representative of fundamental interests and concerns to nursing students.

name: The GW Hatchet
description: The GW Hatchet is an editorially and financially independent student newspaper serving The George Washington University community in downtown Washington, D.C. First published Oct. 5, 1904, it is the second-oldest continuously published newspaper in the District, after the Washington Post. The GW Hatchet publishes once each week during the academic year on Mondays. The newspaper is produced by Hatchet Publications, Inc., an independent, 501(c)(3) nonprofit corporation. It is headquartered in a townhouse at 609 21st St. NW in the heart of Foggy Bottom.
summary: The GW Hatchet is an editorially and financially independent student newspaper serving The George Washington University community in downtown Washington, D.C. First published Oct. 5, 1904, it is the second-oldest continuously published newspaper in the...

name: Sigma Kappa
description: Sigma Kappa Sorority was founded in 1874 by five women at Colby College, the first college in New England to admit women on an equal basis with men, in Waterville, Maine. Our sorority was founded with the purpose of providing women lifelong opportunities and support for social, intellectual and spiritual development by bringing women together to positively impact our communities. Our sisterhood is built upon our four core values: personal growth, friendship, service and loyalty; bound by a promise.
summary: Sigma Kappa Sorority was founded in 1874 by five women attending Colby College, the first college in New England to admit women on an equal basis with men, in Waterville, Maine. Our sorority was founded with the purpose of providing women lifelong oppo...

name: JBKO Hall
description: JBKO Hall is home to approximately 265 second- and third-year students in single, 2-person, and 3-person units. During the 2016 summer, JBKO received new flooring in all rooms and the interior was painted. Named for GW alumna Jacqueline Bouvier Kennedy Onassis, JBKO Hall has a convenient, central location across the street from the Foggy Bottom/GWU Metro station and the GW Hospital. JBKO is very close to the K Street corridor, offering many business opportunities to GW students. It’s also down the block from Whole Foods grocery and has restaurants across the street. With Munson and Fulbright located on the same block and the Health and Wellness Center and Funger Hall only a few minutes away, the location is one of the many qualities that make JBKO a great place to live.
summary: JBKO Hall is home to approximately 265 second- and third-year students in single, 2-person, and 4-person units. During the 2016 summer, JBKO will received new flooring in all rooms and the interior was painted. Named for GW alumna Jacqueline Bouvier Ke...

name: Tau Beta Pi
description: The Tau Beta Pi Association is the oldest engineering honor society and the second oldest collegiate honor society in the United States. It honors engineering students in American universities who have shown a history of academic achievement as well as a commitment to personal and professional integrity. We are the DC Gamma Chapter.
summary: Tau Beta Pi is an honors society for outstanding students in engineering.

name: Munson Hall
description: Munson is home to approximately 200 second-year students in 2, 3, and 4-person units. Munson was most recently renovated in 2018 with upgrades to the bathrooms, kitchens, flooring, and hallway carpeting. Munson is located next to Panda Express and the Science and Engineering Hall, down the street from the Foggy Bottom/GWU Metro station, and across from Whole Foods Market. It is also near the Gelman Library and the University Student Center.
summary: Munson is home to approximately 200 second-year students in 2, 3, and 4-person units. Munson was most recently renovated in 2018 with upgrades to the bathrooms, kitchens, flooring, and hallway carpeting. Munson is located next to Panda Express and the...

name: Nu Rho Psi Honors Society in Neuroscience
description: Nu Rho Psi membership recognizes verified academic excellence in the interdisciplinary field of Neuroscience. Students who become members of Nu Rho Psi are selected based on their superior scholarly accomplishments as well as their excellent work in the laboratory. Nu Rho Psi membership is for life and it is often a springboard for the networking and collaboration of like-minded colleagues interested in the study of the brain. Our members are provided opportunities to form professional relationships that carry them through their careers in Neuroscience.
summary: Nu Rho Psi membership provides recognition of verified academic excellence in the interdisciplinary field of Neuroscience. Members of Nu Rho Psi are selected based on superior scholarly accomplishments as well as their excellent work in the laboratory.

name: Capitol Advertising
description:
Applicants from all schools and class standings are welcomed.

Contact capadgwu@gmail.com to explore further!

 

Capitol Advertising is a team that is hand-selected to compete on behalf of GW in the National Student Advertising Competition (NSAC), a national competition where 150+ schools compete and develop an advertising campaign from the ground up for a real-world client. Some examples of our past clients are AT&T, Tide, Meta, Tinder, Adobe, Bank of America, Nestle, Toyota, American Airlines, Coca-Cola, State Farm, and Pizza Hut. In 2022, we won 1st place in NSAC District II!

Capitol Advertising (CapAd) is a part of Advanced Advertising MKTG 4149 in the Marketing Division in the School of Business. The class is not exclusive to the business school and can be requested to substitute for many other credits. Applicants from all schools and class standings are welcomed. Our purpose is to promote a better understanding of the functions of advertising while stimulating and encouraging advertising professionalism by enhancing advertising education through interaction and teamwork. 

We are a sub-organization of the American Advertising Federation (AAF). AAF provides numerous programs to guide its college students through advertising curriculum and job placement. AAF's programs include the following: Internship opportunities, scholarships, career guides, industry mentors, and networking with top agency and corporate recruiters.
summary: Our purpose: to inspire and encourage advertising professionalism by exposing students to real-world marketing experiences. We're a sub-organization of the American Advertising Federation participating in the National Student Advertising Competition.

name: GW Robotics Team
description: The mission of GW Robotics is to spark an interest for robotics and provide opportunities for students to apply their knowledge from their course work through building robots. The members in GW robotics work each year to build a robot that competes in the NASA Lunabotics Competition. Beyond building an mars rover for competition, the team strives to promote robotics within the GW community through hosting mini robotics competitions for the students and other various educational events. Students on the team also reach out into the local community by mentoring FIRST robotics teams at the local high schools.
summary: The mission of GW Robotics is to spark an interest for robotics and provide opportunities for students to apply their knowledge from their course work through building robots.

name: GW First Gen United
description:
First Gen United is a student organization for first-generation college students. We consider you a first-generation student—or “first-gen” for short—if one or both of your parents did not graduate from a four-year college or the equivalent in the United States. Being a first-gen student is something to celebrate and we seek to help first-generation college students soar to success. By assisting with acclimation, academics, and advocacy, we encourage students to gain confidence and grow professionally while seeking career-enriching opportunities. For anyone who is a first-generation student, we'd like you to know this organization is a community that supports you and welcomes all!

For more information, please follow @gwu_fgu on Instagram and join our GroupMe linked below!

GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/49223343/4X084F1b
summary: First Gen United (FGU) is the only student organization for first-generation (first-gen) college students at GW. FGU offers a welcoming community for first-gen students and offers mentorship, academic resources, and social events.

name: Guthridge Hall
description:
Guthridge Hall is home to approximately 300 first-year students in 2-person, 3-person, and 4-person units. Located near the Smith Center athletic facilities, Guthridge is an eight-story building. There are 2 kitchens located on the 2nd and 6th floors, as well as a student lounge on the basement level. Each fully furnished apartment has a private bathroom, fridge, and microwave.

Across the street from Anniversary Park and in front of Square 80, Guthridge residents can put together an impromptu picnic or study session on a beautiful fall or spring day. Additionally, Guthridge is home to the first-gen LLC, Blaze the Tra1l.
summary: Guthridge Hall is home to approximately 300 first-year students in 2-person, 3-person, and 4-person units. Located near the Smith Center athletic facilities, Guthridge is an eight-story residence hall with two kitchens, a lounge, and private bathrooms.

name: GW Argentine Tango
description:
The GW Argentine Tango Club strives to create a relaxing but educational atmosphere. Our mission is to make Argentine Tango accessible to members of the GW community. GW Argentine Tango offers dance classes and social dances which provide an excellent opportunity for interaction between all members of the community and a means of escape from the hustle and bustle of everyday life. No experience or partners required. We offer tango classes, workshop of musicality and the cultural lessons about tango to promote diversity and cultural exchange.

 

Please request membership if you're interesting in attending a class to stay up to date, class locations may move week to week depending on room availability.
summary: The GW Argentine Tango Club strives to create a relaxing but educational atmosphere. Our mission is to make Argentine Tango accessible to members of the GW community. GW Argentine Tango offers dance classes and social dances which provide an excellent ...

name: Alpha Phi Fraternity
description: The sisterhood of Alpha Phi stretches from coast to coast through 170 collegiate campuses and more than 200,000 members. Sisters share a commitment to excellence and a strong desire to help one another and their communities. Alpha Phis are leaders, scholars, and lifetime members of a sisterhood that values these traits. Alpha Phi assists college women in making informed choices to reach their highest potential. Alpha Phi enhances and promotes each member's development and learning by focusing on sisterhood, service, scholarship, leadership, loyalty and character development. The innovative leadership and organizational practices of Alpha Phi's Founders in 1872 set the spirit for the Alpha Phi of today...a sisterhood that values the past but looks forward to the progress offered by the future.
summary: The sisterhood of Alpha Phi stretches from coast to coast through 160 collegiate campuses and more than 200,000 members.  Sisters share a commitment to excellence and a strong desire to help one another and their communities.  Alpha Phis are leaders, s...

name: Health eLeaders Association
description:
The Health eLeaders Association aims to unite a diverse group of experienced healthcare professionals from the MHA@GW program for interaction, collaboration, and skill development, both in and out of the classroom. This student association leverages the professional backgrounds and networks of MHA@GW students to enrich the educational experiences offered throughout the program. To achieve these goals, the Health eLeaders Association will:

- Represent MHA@GW students to voice their collective interests and concerns regarding School and University matters.
- Serve as a liaison between the student body and the MHA@GW program.
- Create unique educational opportunities to support the professional growth of emerging healthcare administrators nationwide.
- Foster collaboration and unity among other GWU graduate programs.
summary: The MHA@GW program allows us to learn effectively from the relationships that we build with our professors and cohort members. The Health eLeaders Association provides a collaborative forum for which students can expand those relationships across all c..

name: International House
description: International House, located at 2201 Virginia Avenue, has a maximum occupancy of 176 second- and third-year students in one- and two-person units. Each of the nine floors houses residents, with every apartment having its own private bathroom. Each room comes equipped with a dining table, bed, desk, and desk chair, and 24 of the apartments have balconies overlooking 23rd street down to the Watergate. International House was most recently renovated in 2014 with updates to the kitchens and bathroom vanities. In 2015 International House received new furniture. International House is just steps away from a CVS pharmacy, the State Department, World Health Organization, and even the Lincoln Memorial.
summary: International House, located at 2201 Virginia Avenue, has a maximum occupancy of 176 second- and third-year students in one- and two-person units. Each of the nine floors houses residents, with every apartment having its own private bathroom. Each room...

name: Student Bar Association
description: The Student Bar Association is the student-run governing body of the GW Law School. The organization oversees the student groups within the law school and serves as a resource for law students, faculty, administration, and the broader GW community.
summary: The Student Bar Association is the student-run governing body of the GW Law School. The organization oversees the student groups within the law school and serves as a resource for law students, faculty, administration, and the broader GW community.

name: Fraternity & Sorority Life
description: With over 155 years of tradition, 40 active Greek-letter men's and women's organizations, and just over 2,400 members, the Fraternity & Sorority Community at GW is one of the strongest, healthiest, and largest (for an urban institution) communities in the country! The self-governing, self-supporting bodies of the fraternities and sororities are the Interfraternity Council, the National Pan-Hellenic Association, the Multicultural Greek Council, and the Panhellenic Association. These groups coordinate events, provide educational programs, establish community wide regulations and implement policies for their member organizations. Fraternity & Sorority life is founded on five pillars: scholarship, service, active leadership, and community. Many members describe it as a one-stop shopping opportunity for student involvement that complements and adds to their curricular life on campus. By joining a fraternity/sorority, members can be involved with activities that fulfill their diverse interests and help them develop into productive members of society. The GW Fraternity & Sorority Community is unlike any other community in the country. Visit our FSL website and find out about our various councils, how to meet our members, learn about the groups, and see if this is the type of ‘Only at GW’ experience you want to be involved in!
summary: GW's Fraternity and Sorority Life Community

name: Students for Indigenous and Native American Rights
description: Students for Indigenous and Native American Rights is committed to providing a safe space for Indigenous and Native students on campus, amplifying issues about Indian Country and the global Indigenous population, educating GW students on the historical and contemporary challenges Native and Indigenous people face, and advocating for Indigenous and Native American rights.
summary: The purpose of Students for Indigenous and Native American Rights is to promote the education, awareness, and activism related to the rights and history of Native Americans and Indigenous peoples around the world.

name: GW Partners in Health Engage
description: "PIH Engage aims to build the right to health movement by growing and amplifying PIH's work to advance the right to health globally. PIH Engage brings resources and builds power to stabilize the financing of these systems globally. We recruit and train teams of volunteer organizers to raise funds and engage with policy makers, focusing all of our actions on the three pillars of PIH Engage: advocacy, fundraising, and education."

description: Mark Shenkman Hall is conveniently situated on GW's Foggy Bottom campus and is home to 730 upperclass students in the comfort of one- or two-bedroom apartments, accommodating two to four people each. Mark Shenkman Hall is located across the street from the Lerner Health and Wellness Center, and its apartments include a living room, full kitchen, dining area and private bathrooms.
summary: Mark Shenkman Hall is conveniently situated on GW's Foggy Bottom campus and is home to 730 upperclass students in the comfort of one- or two-bedroom apartments, accommodating two to four people each. Mark Shenkman Hall is located across the street from...

description: Established in 2016, the GW Undergraduate Review is the premier publication of undergraduate research from students at The George Washington University in Washington, D.C.
summary: Established in 2016, the GW Undergraduate Review is the premier publication of undergraduate research from students at The George Washington University in Washington, D.C.

name: GW Club Gymnastics
description: This is a co-ed gymnastics team! You can join to do gymnastics recreationally or work toward competing against other local club gymnastics teams, culminating in Nationals each spring. We train off-campus 3 times a week in Virginia!
summary: This is a co-ed gymnastics team! You can join to do gymnastics recreationally or work toward competing against other local club gymnastics teams. We train off-campus in Virginia!

name: CHAARG (Changing Health, Attitudes + Actions to Recreate Girls)
description:
CHAARG is a health + wellness student org that empowers college women to find a workout they love while building strong, supportive friendships along the way.

We’ve got tons of fun events planned for the year — we meet every Tuesday night and try out different fitness studios around the DMV! Think boxing at RUMBLE, climbing at Movement Gym, yoga at ALO, and so much more. 💪✨
summary: CHAARG (Changing Health, Attitudes, + Actions to Recreate Girls) at The George Washington University is an organization to serve students in pursuing their health and wellness goals while also creating a strong community of peers. CHAARG at GWU will pr...

name: The George Washington University Art Therapy Student Association
description: The George Washington University Art Therapy Student Association (GWATSA) is established for the purpose of providing art therapy students with a means of involvement in the community, leadership development, and fundraising.
summary: The George Washington University Art Therapy Student Association (GWATSA) isestablished for the purpose of providing art therapy students with a means of involvement in the community, leadership development, and fundraising.

name: Delta Iota Gamma
description:
Delta Iota Gamma is a fraternal organization for students studying in the field of anthropology, classics, and humans in general. Our gender inclusive fraternity dedicates itself to developing the academic skills, professional networks, and technical expertise of its members. 

DIG is a rush-only fraternity. We host rush in both the fall and spring semesters every year. If you are interested in receiving emails regarding the rush process, please fill out this form: https://forms.gle/M9YRyPmQWDn38qjz5
summary: Delta Iota Gamma is a fraternal organization dedicated to developing the academic skills, professional networks, and technical expertise of future archaeologists, classicists, and those dedicated to the study of the ancient world.

name: Kappa Alpha Theta
description: Kappa Alpha Theta was founded in 1870 as the first Greek-letter fraternity for women. We value our history and our long tradition of leading the way for women's groups and women in higher education.
summary: Kappa Alpha Theta was founded in 1870 as the first Greek-letter fraternity for women. We value our history and our long tradition of leading the way for women's groups and women in higher education.

name: Afghan Student Union
description: "At The George Washington University, our mission for the Afghan Student Association is to serve as a diverse organization to help spread awareness on Afghan culture and issues through social events and fundraising as well as provide a welcoming and inclusive community for all students with similar interests."
summary: The Afghan Student Association [ASA] is a student-led organization open to registered GW undergraduate and graduate students. We cater to all, Afghan or non-Afghan students are welcome to learn more about Afghanistan's culture, history and issues.

name: GW A Cappella Council
description: The GW A Cappella Council (GWAC) is the advisory body for the a cappella groups at GW. GWAC strives to create a more cohesive and unified a cappella community, rooted in a foundation of great music, strong bonds, and a friendly and collaborative environment for all musical artists at GW.
summary: The GW A Cappella Council (GWAC) is a body consisting of the GW A Cappella groups on campus. Our mission is to provide a space for collaboration amongst the A Cappella community and a platform for ourselves to achieve clearer communication of our needs.

name: Student Health Center
description: The Student Health Center (SHC) at the George Washington University (GW) provides multidisciplinary, integrated, student-centered services in a safe, accessible, culturally inclusive, and supportive environment. The SHC seeks to promote healthy lifestyle choices and to holistically support the emotional and physical well-being of students in order to achieve academic success.
summary: The Student Health Center (SHC) at the George Washington University (GW) provides multidisciplinary, integrated, student-centered services in a safe, accessible, culturally inclusive, and supportive environment.

name: The George Washington Figure Skating Club
description: The mission of the George Washington Figure Skating Club is to provide students with a supportive and friendly community to continue their passion for figure skating or to find a passion for figure skating. Our goal is to increase access to figure skating and the level of skating at the collegiate level for students of all backgrounds. We invest in our student skaters so they can continue growing in something they love while not feeling alone in seeking this opportunity. The club was founded in 2016 and became a club sport at GW in January 2018.
summary: The mission of the George Washington Figure Skating Club is to provide students with a supportive and friendly community to continue their passion for figure skating or to find a passion for figure skating. Our goal is to increase access to the sport.

name: George Hacks
description: George Hacks is a student-led innovation organization at the George Washington University, dedicated to empowering the next generation of problem-solvers in healthcare. Since our founding in 2018, we’ve provided a platform for students from diverse backgrounds to collaborate, think outside the box, and address real-world problem-statements presented by experts in the healthcare industry. Our flagship event, the annual George Hacks Innovation Hackathon, brings together creative minds from across the D.C. metropolitan area to develop impactful solutions at the intersection of medicine, technology, and entrepreneurship. Through hands-on experiences, mentorships, problem solving, and interdisciplinary teamwork, George Hacks provides students a platform to harness their skills for the greater good.
summary: George Hacks catalyzes innovation in healthcare by empowering the next generation of change-makers.

name: China Development Student Think Tank(GWU Chapter)
description: China Development Student Think Tank (CDSTT GWU Chapter) is a non-partisan student think tank. CDSTT provides constructive academic reports and philosophical ideas regarding economic development, political reform, cultural diversity, and diplomatic strategy to students interested in global issues, public affairs, and international relations. Our goal is to provide a platform for students to share opinions publicly and spread intellectual ideas to society.
summary: China Development Student Think Tank (GWU Chapter) is a non-partisan student think tank focusing on global economic development, political reform, cultural diversity, and diplomatic strategy.

name: INCOSE GWU
description:
The GW Chapter of the International Council on Systems Engineering, stands as a beacon for excellence in the realm of systems engineering. Established with the mission to cultivate and propagate transdisciplinary principles and practices.
INCOSE GW is a professional organization that offers a platform to connect with like-minded professionals, fostering growth, collaboration, and innovation. INCOSE GW invites the EMSE community to tap into a rich network of EMSE alumni, but also open doors to a plethora of invaluable opportunities. From exclusive events to insightful discussions. 

● Link-tree: INCOSE GW
● Parent Chapter: INCOSE - WMA
summary: INCOSE GWU plans, organizes, and promotes events including seminars, industry showcases, alumni and enterprise networking opportunities, professional preparation events, and presentations of academic work by its members and affiliates.

name: Every Nation Campus at George Washington University
description:
#ENC2026 North America Recap

 

The purpose of Every Nation Campus at GWU is to be a diverse group of Christian and non-Christian students, staff, and associate members who are committed to serving the GWU campus by:

-Helping people in their relationship with Jesus Christ and providing them with training for victorious Christian living, leadership, and excellence.

-Sharing the knowledge of life in Jesus Christ with the GWU community.

-Making a positive impact on the GWU campus by developing students, equipped with biblical solutions to contemporary problems, to serve every facet of the GWU community, Washington, D.C. area, and ultimately the world.

-Serving the university and helping the students have a positive experience at GWU.

-Introducing its members to a local church family and other missions or ministry opportunities.
summary: The purpose of Every Nation Campus at GWU is to be a diverse group of Christian and non-Christian students, staff, and associate members who are committed to serving the GWU campus by helping people in their relationship with Jesus Christ.

name: The Disabled Students Collective
description:
The Disabled Students Collective is an organization founded by and for disabled students* at GWU! We welcome both disabled individuals and non-disabled allies to join us in weekly meetings and additional events focused on advocacy, creating community, providing support, and fostering pride in disability culture and identity! No disclosure of disability is required for membership. The privacy of our members is of utmost importance, and we promise to never share anyone's personal information without permission.

*We welcome people with all types of disabilities, including (but definitely not limited to!) chronic illnesses, mental illnesses, learning disabilities, Autism / Neurodivergency, d/Deaf & Hard of Hearing, individuals who are blind/low vision and all others. We never want anyone to feel as if they are "not disabled enough" to join. You are welcome and supported!
summary: The Disabled Students Collective is an organization founded by and for disabled students* at GWU! We welcome both disabled individuals and non-disabled allies to join us in weekly meetings and additional events focused on advocacy, community, and pride.

name: Phi Beta Sigma
description:
The Gamma Alpha Phi Chapter of Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Incorporated.

Charter: March 2, 2012.

Principles: Brotherhood, Scholarship, Service.

Motto: Culture for Service, Service for Humanity.

 

The Gamma Alpha Phi Chapter of Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Incorporated is the only National Pan-Hellenic Council (or Divine Nine) fraternity chartered at The George Washington University. We seek to engage our GW and DMV community through service-oriented events with our principles (brotherhood, scholarship, and service) in mind.
summary:
Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Incorporated, GrueΣome Gamma Alpha Phi Chapter. Chartered March 2, 2012. 

Principles: Brotherhood, Scholarship, Service. Motto: Culture for Service, Service for Humanity.

name: Alpha Phi Alpha
description: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc. is the first Black intercollegiate fraternity. Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc. develops leaders, promotes brotherhood and academic excellence, while providing service and advocacy for our communities.
summary: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc. develops leaders, promotes brotherhood and academic excellence, while providing service and advocacy for our communities.

description:
The School of Engineering and Applied Science (SEAS)- Office of Graduate Admissions & Student Services fosters academic, professional and personal growth for all graduate students. We collaborate with faculty and administrators to support and encourage learning and thriving at GW. 
http://graduate.seas.gwu.edu

name: Girl Up George Washington University
description: Girl Up: GW is committed to the education, health, and safety of girls around the globe, especially in developing countries, with the overarching goal of positioning girls to be the next generation of leaders in their communities. Through education, fundraising, and advocacy we hope to raise awareness of gender inequality and provide resources to empower the young women of today.
summary: Girl Up GW is committed to the education, health, and safety of girls around the globe, especially in developing countries, with the overarching goal of positioning girls to be the next generation of leaders in their communities.

name: Leadership Development Programs
description:
The Leadership Development Programs, hosted by the Office of Student Involvement, allow GW students to refine their leadership and organizational management skills. Students can participate in sessions designed to prepare them for future leadership positions - on or off campus. Student Organization Officers must participate in Leadership Development sessions to fulfill their annual requirements and be eligible for re-registration in the Spring.
 
What will I get out of this? These skills will make you more:
  • Marketable to employers
  • Prepared for leadership positions
  • Effective when working in groups, teams, and organizations
  • Able to create positive change for the causes and communities you care about

Leadership Development Programs are a great opportunity for any individual to further their leadership abilities and knowledge.
summary: The Office of Student Involvement offers an ongoing workshop series for students to build their leadership and organizational management skills.

name: Women in Finance Alliance
description:
The mission of the Women in Finance Alliance at GW is to provide female students interested in finance with networking opportunities, education and workshops, and targeted support and resources, with the purpose of helping our members achieve their professional aspirations and increasing gender diversity in financial fields. 

WIFA fosters collaboration between members and helps to generate a greater interest and knowledge of finance among students supporting female empowerment in historically underrepresented environments.

We have a two-step application process to be considered. First, fill out the application. Once received, you will be contacted by an executive board member to schedule a coffee chat, the second step in the application process.  

Apply here: Fall 2025 Application

Meetings are held every Monday from 6:00-7:00pm in Duques 362.
summary: The mission of the Women in Finance Alliance is to provide female students interested in finance with networking opportunities, education, and targeted support and resources, with the purpose of helping our members achieve their professional aspirations.

name: Potomac House
description: Potomac House is conveniently situated on GW's Foggy Bottom campus and houses 379 students in the comfort of double rooms. Potomac House is located right in the center of the F Street Corridor, near Thurston, one block from the Elliott School, and only minutes away from the Marvin Center. Potomac House also has a dining venue on the exterior of the building.
summary: Potomac House is conveniently situated on GW's Foggy Bottom campus and houses 379 students in the comfort of double rooms. Potomac House is located right in the center of the F Street Corridor, near Thurston, one block from the Elliott School, and only...

name: The Store
description: The Store is GW's campus food pantry. Opened in September 2016, The Store helps GW community members facing food insecurity by providing nutritious food, personal hygiene products, and other needed living supplies.
summary: The Store is GW's campus food pantry. Opened in September 2016, The Store helps GW community members facing food insecurity by providing nutritious food, personal hygiene products, and other needed living supplies.

name: National Pan-Hellenic Council
description: National Pan-hellenic Council serves to unite all black Greek letter organizations through events and community service.
summary: National Pan-hellenic Council serves to unite all black Greek letter organizations through events and community service.

name: Iranian Student Association
description: The GW Iranian Students Association (IRSA) is a cultural, social, and educational organization. IRSA's mission is to provide members a space to meet other students interested in the Persian language and culture and to build a stronger presence within the GW community and the greater DMV Iranian community. IRSA aims to promote Persian culture by hosting social gatherings, movie nights, and cultural events such as Persian New Year (Norooz); attending cultural events in the DMV; and collaborating with other Student Organizations on campus as well as Iranian Student Organizations at universities in the DMV area. Be sure to regularly check this page as well as our Facebook and Instagram for updates regarding events.
summary: The GW Iranian Students Association (IRSA) is a cultural, social, and educational organization. IRSA's mission is to to provide members a space to meet other students interested in the Persian language and culture and to build a stronger presence withi...

name: Rotaract Club of George Washington University
description: The George Washington University Rotaract Club is a university-level extension and Rotary International-sponsored youth program that fosters leadership, promotes peace through philanthropy, and unites individuals through service. Its primary goal is to teach emerging young professionals to take action and gain global perspectives through collective action and networking opportunities. We hope to foster world peace and cultural understanding as partners in service within the GW and DC communities.
summary: The Rotaract Club of George Washington University is Rotary-sponsored international youth program that fosters leadership, professional work and community service for young men and women between the ages 18–30.

name: Democracy Matters
description: Democracy Matters is a non-partisan national student organization that works to protect and strengthen our democracy. We organize actions and projects connecting pro-democracy reforms to issues of voting rights, civil rights, the rule of law, education, and more.
summary: We are the GW chapter of Democracy Matters, a non-partisan student organization that works to promote voting rights and defend our democracy.

name: National Society of Leadership and Success
description: The Society is an organization that helps people discover and achieve their goals. The Society offers life-changing lectures from the nation's leading presenters and a community where like-minded, success-oriented individuals come together and help one another succeed. The Society also serves as a powerful force of good in the greater community by encouraging and organizing action to better the world.
summary: The Society is an organization that helps people discover and achieve their goals. The Society offers life-changing lectures from the nation's leading presenters and a community where like-minded, success-oriented individuals come together.

name: Forensic Psychology Student Association
description: The purpose of the FPSA is to enrich the overall experience of graduate students enrolled in the GWU Forensic Psychology master’s program. The FPSA strives to improve students’ graduate experience through professional development, personal growth, and a sense of community with their peers.
summary: The purpose of the Forensic Psychology Student Association (FPSA) is to enrich the overall experience of graduate students enrolled in the M.A. Forensic Psychology program.

name: The Undergraduate Chapter of the Black Law Student Association
description: We are The Undergraduate Chapter of the Black Law Student Association here at GW! Our goal is to provide Black students of the GW community with a platform to exchange ideas with other students interested in law. Our organization introduces members to practitioners in the field of law, provides networking opportunities within their area of interest, and supports the application process for law school. GW BLSA advocates social justice, ethical legal practices, and community involvement. The organization will foster social interaction for students by sponsoring gatherings and recreational activities and will coordinate events/lectures with various organizations and law schools.
summary: The Undergraduate Pre Law Chapter of the Black Law Student Association strives to articulate and promote the needs and goals of Black GWU students interested in law school. We also hope to bring outside knowledge on problems affecting the Black community

name: 1959 E Street
description: 1959 E Street is connected to the Elliott School of International Affairs building and offers apartment-style living for upperclass students in single, 3-person, 4-person, or 5-person apartments. 1959 E Street features a study lounge and rooms with a breathtaking view of the Washington skyline. Spacious and modern, each apartment has a washer and dryer in the unit, a living room, kitchen, and private bathrooms. Each kitchen is furnished with a dishwasher, microwave, and refrigerator. The rooftop terrace of 1959 E Street is the perfect place to hang out with friends and watch the hustle and bustle of the city from one of the best vantage points in DC.
summary: 1959 E Street is connected to the Elliott School of International Affairs building and offers apartment-style living for upperclass students in single, 3-person, 4-person, or 5-person apartments. 1959 E Street features a study lounge and rooms with a b...

name: Clark Hall
description:
Clark Hall is a first-year, co-ed residence hall located on the Mount Vernon Campus. It offers students traditional-style double suites with shared bathrooms on the first and second floors and access to communal bathrooms on the third floor, fostering a close-knit community. 

 

Clark Hall is home to the Civic House Living and Learning Community, a highly selective first-year program designed for students passionate about civic engagement and community service. Civic House is built on three core values: Live, Learn, and Serve. Through this program, students gain a deeper understanding of Washington, D.C.'s unique culture, strengths, and needs while building meaningful relationships with faculty, peers, and community-based organizations to drive positive change.

 

Each fully furnished room in Clark Hall provides residents with desks, desk chairs, dressers, and twin beds. The hall features laundry facilities, a lounge with a TV, and a community kitchen.
summary: Clark Hall, a predominately first-year student co-ed living option on the Mount Vernon campus, offers students traditional style double suites with shared bathrooms. Clark Hall is directly across the street from Post Hall, one of the academic buildings...

name: 2109 F Street
description: 2109 F Street offers apartment-style living for upperclass students in doubles. A former apartment building, 2109 F Street is conveniently located in the middle of campus, near other residence halls like the Dakota and Guthridge Hall.
summary: 2109 F Street offers apartment-style living for upperclass students in doubles. A former apartment building, 2109 F Street is conveniently located in the middle of campus, near other residence halls like the Dakota and Guthridge Hall.

name: District House
description: District House, which opened for the 2016 Fall semester, is located between 21st, and 22nd, H and I Streets, N.W.. It is conveniently located near the University Student Center and Gelman Library. It is home to 850 upperclass students, residing in 2-person or 4-person units or Affinity Housing units. District House is home to numerous student meeting and study areas, a dance studio, and multiple dining areas.
