summary: District House, which opened for the 2016 Fall semester, is located between 21st, and 22nd, H and I Streets, N.W.. It is conveniently located near the University Student Center and Gelman Library. It is home to over 800 upperclass students.

name: American Medical Physiology Club
description:
AMPC is the largest pre-health organization dedicated to fostering an interdisciplinary community at GW where students can grow in their academic, personal, and professional lives. AMPC partners with healthcare professionals to advise & guide members, organizes hands-on opportunities to develop essential pre-professional skills, and organizes community-building events to help students connect with their pre-health peers.

As a pre-health community that aims to uplift one another, we offer a wide range of career opportunities to our members, including:

- Resume/CV Workshops through our Academic Director

- Linkage to Shadowing

- Research Positions through our Research Symposium Program

- Personalized Guidance from the Executive Board

- Mentorship with our Mentorship Program

- Volunteering with our Community Service & Bonding Program

- Panels and events with healthcare professionals through our Pre-Professional Program

- Opportunities to connect and make friends with pre-health peers!

Interested in any of these opportunities? Want to meet other pre-health students at GW? Join us now! There is no cost associated with being a member of AMPC. We strive to make our opportunities available to everyone:)
summary: The mission of the AMPC is to provide a platform for pre-health students to enrich their passion for various health  professions through hands-on learning, student-led experiments, and exposure to experts specialized in different fields.

name: Cole Hall
description:
Cole Hall is a first-year, co-ed residence hall located on the Mount Vernon Campus. Cole residents enjoy traditional double rooms and double suites with shared bathrooms.

 

Each fully furnished Cole Hall room includes desks, desk chairs, dressers, and twin beds. Students can relax in the community lounge, complete with a TV, or cook meals with friends in the shared kitchen. Laundry facilities are available in the basement. The small size of Cole Hall fosters a tight-knit community, allowing students to build strong connections.
summary: Cole Hall houses first-year students in the center of all the action that the Mount Vernon campus offers. Cole residents enjoy traditional double rooms and traditional double suites with shared bathrooms. Mount Vernon's beautiful sports complex is an e...

name: Lafayette Hall
description: Renovated and reopened Fall 2011, Lafayette Hall is home to approximately 120 second-year students. Room types include singles and 2-person spaces. Lafayette is located adjacent to the University Student Center and across the street from Western Market, which features restaurants and retail stores. Additionally, this residence hall is two blocks away from the Foggy Bottom-GWU Metro Station and is a short walk from Pennsylvania Avenue.
summary: Renovated and reopened Fall 2011, Lafayette Hall is home to approximately 120 second-year students. Lafayette is located adjacent to the University Student Center and across the street from Western Market...

name: Club Women's Ice Hockey
description:
We are a collegiate club ice hockey team that competes in the ACCHL and ACHA Division II. In our first-ever full season, we won the inaugural ACCHL Women's Division Championship.

Our team provides women of all skill levels the opportunity to play ice hockey at GWU. We require skating experience but no hockey experience. We practice and play out of The St James, an ice rink not too far from campus. We participate in an inter-collegiate league and represent GW to universities in the mid-Atlantic area.
summary: Our team provides women of all skill levels the opportunity to play ice hockey at GW. We play in the ACCHL, a league within the ACHA, against other Mid-Atlantic Area schools.

name: Hensley Hall
description:
Hensley Hall is a first-year, co-ed residence hall on the Mount Vernon Campus. It offers a mix of single rooms, double rooms with a community bathroom, and double suites with shared bathrooms. Hensley boasts a close-knit community.

 

Hensley Hall is home to the Politics & Values Living and Learning Community (LLC), where a select group of students live and study together while engaging in an intensive year-long course exploring societal politics. This immersive academic experience fosters meaningful discussions and strong connections among students with a shared interest in political thought and governance.

 

Each fully furnished Hensley room includes desks, desk chairs, dressers, and twin beds. The hall also features laundry facilities in the basement, a lounge with a billiards table and television, and a community kitchen where students can prepare meals.
summary: Hensley Hall, a predominately first-year co-ed living option on the Mount Vernon campus, has a maximum occupancy of 39 students in a combination of single rooms with a community bathroom, double rooms with a community bathroom, double suites with share...

name: Psychology Graduate Association
description: The Psychology Graduate Association (PGA) is a collective of all GW psychology graduate programs which aims to encourage cross-disciplinary collaboration, advocate for students’ interests, provide opportunities for professional development, host social gatherings, organize community outreach, and promote the knowledge and practice of psychology both within and outside of the GW community.
summary: The Psychology Graduate Association (PGA) is a collective of all GW psychology graduate programs which aims to encourage cross-disciplinary collaboration, advocate for students’ interests, and provide opportunities for professional development.

name: Policy Perspectives
description: Policy Perspectives is a Trachtenberg School organization that manages its public policy and public administration journal (Policy Perspectives) and blog (Brief Policy Perspectives). Each publishes work by current and former graduate students at the Trachtenberg School on a wide range of policy topics and aims to be accessible to interested readers both within and outside of Trachtenberg.
summary: Policy Perspectives is a Trachtenberg School organization that manages its public policy and public administration journal (Policy Perspectives) and blog (Brief Policy Perspectives).

name: Mitchell Hall
description: Mitchell Hall is home to approximately 350 first-year and upperclass in single rooms. Located on the southeast corner of campus, Mitchell is next to Thurston Hall, 1959 E Street, and is only three blocks from the White House. Mitchell Hall was most recently renovated in 2015, with updates to the plumbing, new vanities, and new furniture in all rooms. In rooms with private bathrooms, the bathrooms were updated as well. All rooms have vanities, microwaves and fridges, and medicine cabinets. Two large bathroom facilities are located on each floor; and all floors but the first floor have kitchen facilities.
summary: Mitchell Hall is home to approximately 350 first-year and upperclass in single rooms. Located on the southeast corner of campus, Mitchell is next to Thurston Hall, 1959 E Street, and is only three blocks from the White House. Mitchell Hall was most rec...

name: Madison Hall
description: Madison Hall has a maximum occupancy of 222 students in two-person, four-person, and six-person units. Located in the center of campus and next door to Duquès Hall, Madison residents also have the Smith Center athletic facilities and the Gelman Library close by. Madison is also located a short walk away from the Academic Center and Funger Hall, two other academic buildings on the Foggy Bottom campus. Each of Madison's eight floors houses residents with a private bathroom in each room or suite. Madison Hall was recently renovated in the summer of 2018.
summary: Madison Hall has a maximum occupancy of 222 students in two-person, four-person, and six-person units. Located in the center of campus and next door to Duquès Hall, Madison residents also have the Smith Center athletic facilities and the Gelman Library...

name: Amsterdam Hall
description: Philip Amsterdam Hall upperclass residents enjoy apartment-style living in the comfort of spacious one- or two-bedroom and one- or two-bathroom suites, which accommodate two to four people each. Philip Amsterdam Hall is the home our Second-Year residents. Residents of Philip Amsterdam Hall also enjoy a living room, and a fully equipped kitchen and dining area perfect for cooking up delicious meals for your roommates and friends. Philip Amsterdam Hall was most recently renovated in 2012 with updates to the flooring, grout, and some of the kitchen countertops and cabinets.
summary: Philip Amsterdam Hall upperclass residents enjoy apartment-style living in the comfort of spacious one- or two-bedroom and one- or two-bathroom suites, which accommodate two to four people each. Philip Amsterdam Hall is the home of the Global Connectio...

name: South Hall
description: In Fall 2009, South Hall became GW's first LEED residence hall, housing over 450 third and fourth-year students in single and double bedrooms in four and five-person units. Spacious and modern, each suite has a washer and dryer in the unit, a common living room, dining area, an eat-in kitchen, and 2 private bathrooms. Each kitchen is furnished with a dishwasher, microwave, and refrigerator. South Hall is located near the Smith Center athletic facilities, and is across the street from Anniversary Park.
summary: In Fall 2009, South Hall became GW's first LEED residence hall, housing over 450 fourth-year students in single and double bedrooms in four and five person units. Spacious and modern, each suite has a washer and dryer in the unit, a common living room,...

name: Thurston Hall
description: Thurston Hall has a maximum occupancy of 820 first-year students in a limited number of two- or one-person units. Thurston Hall, the largest first-year residence hall on the Foggy Bottom campus, offers students numerous amenities under one roof. Students living here enjoy television and 20+ lounges, as well as academic study space.
summary: Thurston Hall has a maximum occupancy of 820 first-year students in a limited number of two- or one-person units.

name: Women's Leadership Fellows
description:
The Women’s Leadership Fellows program is a selective cohort of women currently enrolled at the Trachtenberg School who meet monthly to build leadership and community through skills-based workshops, a mentoring program, and networking events. Monthly sessions are led by vetted trainers who volunteer their time and expertise to focus on negotiation, conflict resolution, verbal and nonverbal communication, and management skills.

Trachtenberg School alumnae, including previous Women’s Leadership Fellows, serve as mentors to current participants. Mentors provide fellows with coaching and professional development support. Women’s Leadership Fellows also have the opportunity to network with current and past fellows, George Washington University alumni, and other professionals.

The women are chosen by a panel of Trachtenberg School faculty, staff, and students after a competitive application process. Two student co-chairs coordinate the planning and logistics of the program in partnership with the Trachtenberg School Administration.
summary: The Women’s Leadership Fellows (WLF) program is a selective cohort of individuals who identify as female currently enrolled at the Trachtenberg School who meet monthly to build leadership and community through skills-based workshops and mentorship.

name: The Dakota
description: The Dakota is a modern yet classic apartment building with approximately 200 beds for second- and third-year students. The building has 3-person, 4-person, and a few single occupancy units. The Dakota was most recently renovated in 2014 with updates to the bathrooms, kitchens, flooring, and hallways of the building, as well as receiving new furniture.
summary: The Dakota is a modern yet classic apartment building with approximately 200 beds for second- and third-year students. The building has 3-person, 4-person, and a few single occupancy units. The Dakota was most recently renovated in 2014 with updates to...

name: Tau Kappa Epsilon
description: Founded on January 10, 1899, Tau Kappa Epsilon is one of the largest collegiate men’s social fraternities in North America with over 272,000 initiated members and 245 active chapters and colonies. TKE’s mission is to aid men in their mental, moral and social development for life. With nearly 12,000 collegiate members, Tau Kappa Epsilon contributes to the advancement of society through the personal growth of our members, and service to others. TKE builds Better Men for a Better World. Mission: To aid men in their mental, moral, and social development for life.
summary: Founded on January 10, 1899, Tau Kappa Epsilon is one of the largest collegiate men’s social fraternities in North America with over 272,000 initiated members and 245 active chapters and colonies. TKE’s mission is to aid men in their mental, moral...

name: Multicultural Student Services Center
description: Through strategic partnerships and innovative programs, the Multicultural Student Services Center (MSSC) fosters a university experience that provides GW students opportunities to engage across differences in an increasingly diverse and global society. The MSSC is a university hub for programming, community building, celebrating cultures and traditions, and creating a welcoming space for all students at GW. Our work is guided by a strong belief in our shared humanity.
summary: MSSC is grounded in three major pillars: Cultural Education & Celebration, Skill Development, and Cultivating a Sense of Community and Belonging. Central to our approach are two core identity areas: race, ethnicity, and culture, and gender and sexuality.

name: Title IX Office
description:
WHO ARE WE?

The George Washington University is committed to maintaining a positive climate for study and work, in which individuals are judged solely on relevant factors, such as skill and performance, and can pursue their activities in an atmosphere that is free from discrimination, harassment, and violence. The Title IX Office supports the university’s commitment by fairly and equitably responding to reports of sexual harassment, sexual assault, domestic and dating violence, and stalking. The Title IX Office also provides prevention and response educational programming for the GW community aiming to educate the community on how to create safe, respectful, and inclusive education and workplace environments.

 

WHAT IS TITLE IX?

Title IX is a federal law that prohibits sex discrimination in any educational institution or program that receives federal funding.  Sex discrimination includes sexual harassment, sexual assault, dating and domestic violence, stalking and other forms of discrimination on the basis of sex.  Title IX applies to faculty, staff, students, volunteers, and other participants in an institution's programs and activities.

 

WHAT HAPPENS WHEN A TITLE IX REPORT IS MADE?

After filing a report, you will receive an outreach email from the Title IX office.  You do not need to respond.  However, if desired, you can schedule an intake meeting with us to discuss your options, including supportive measures, which are non-disciplinary, non-punitive individualized services, accommodations, and other assistance.  These are available to you regardless of whether or not you choose to file a formal complaint and include but are not limited to:

  • Counseling and Medical Services
  • Academic Support
  • Change in Campus Housing
  • Safety Planning

If you choose to file a formal complaint, you can engage in one of two types of resolutions: alternative resolution, which is voluntary and remedies-based, or formal resolution, which involves a formal investigation and hearing process.

To learn more about all of your options, please visit the Title IX website.

 

HOW DO I REPORT?

You have several reporting options:

  • Complete an online report form at our website
  • Call (202) 994-7434
  • Email titleix@gwu.edu
  • Visit the Title IX Office at Building YY, 812 20th St, NW
  • Call the Sexual Assault & Intimate Violence Helpline (24/7) at (202) 994-7222

 

HOW DO I LEARN MORE ABOUT TITLE IX?

A great way to learn more about Title IX and how you can help us better our GW community is through our free trainings.  Click here to see our most up-to-date training calendar and register for one of our upcoming events.  You can also request a Title IX training for your student organization, residence hall, or staff/faculty group.  Just fill out this form to get connected.
summary: GW’s Title IX Office provides resources, training, and reporting options to students, faculty, and staff to address concerns related to sexual harassment and sexual violence.  We also provide supportive measures for pregnant and parenting students.

name: Engage Test Org
summary: This is a test org

name: Org Help
description:
Org Help is the hub for GW student organization resources, including key documents, Engage How-Tos, and more. For any questions and concerns, please contact orghelp@gwu.edu 

Student Org Durable Goods Rental Program
summary: Org Help is the hub for GW student organization resources, including key documents, Engage How-Tos, and more.

name: GW Folklorico
description: Founded in 2017, GW Folklorico began with a mission of carving out a space for traditional Latin-American dance at our university. Since then the purpose has expanded to create an inclusive environment where students of all backgrounds can be immersed in Latin culture through dance. Step by step, the organization continues the work to both preserve decades-old traditions while broadening the stage to new generations and audiences across the DMV.
summary: GW Folklorico’s purpose is to create an inclusive, diverse environment where people of all backgrounds can be immersed in Latinx culture and learn the traditional dances of different countries all over Latin America.

name: GW Women in Economics
description: GW Women in Economics seeks to increase women’s representation and support women’s participation in economics, at GWU and in the broader profession. The organization seeks to address the demonstrated lack of representation of women in the field of economics, beginning at the pipeline by fostering interest among students, increase visibility of women pursuing economic degrees, providing professional networking opportunities that promote the advancement of women in the professions, and to create a forum in which issues of common interest can be explored.
summary: GW Women in Economics seeks to increase women’s representation and support women’s participation in economics, at GWU and in the broader profession.

name: Compass: Impact Consulting at GW
description:
Compass: Impact Consulting at GW consists of students who serve as advisors to social enterprises and non-profits seeking funding from Impact Investors. Impact Investing is a rapidly growing field in which investors deploy capital investments to secure financial and social returns. The gap between investment capital and investment-ready projects is a common challenge facing the Impact Investing space. Compass’s mission is to advance and maximize social impact through the development of partnerships, collaboration, and accountability and measurement frameworks. The organization will function as an advisory service for projects that were unable to receive government funding and will undergo a capacity and proposal-building process in order to attain capital from an impact investor. 

 

Spring 2026 Interest Form: https://forms.gle/tanwTC3rwf44ZZ5h8
summary: Strategic advisors who work with investors, enterprises and start-ups to accelerate growth whilst driving social change. Compass members perform analyses, make recommendations, and create quality deliverables for clients.

name: GW ALPFA
description: Currently serving close to 100,000 student members nationwide, ALPFA helps with leadership and professional development, as well as networking, as a way to help foster the economic impact of the Latino demographic in the United States. Since its, inception in 1972, it has grown vastly. ALPFA members are exposed to over 50,000 internships from over 1,000 corporate partners, so it is a worthwhile investment for any Latino student.
summary: The Association of Latino Professionals for America (ALPFA) is a Latino-serving national organization promoting professional development among young professional, Latino college students.

name: Her Campus GW
description: Her Campus GWU is an online magazine publication where students can edit and write content that is both a resource and a source of entertainment, highlighting campus news, events, interesting students, faculty, alumni, campus photos, and fashion and lifestyle content specifically tailored for women at GW. Members and officers will gain journalism, marketing, publicity, and digital media experience, and all students at GW will gain a new and fun online resource through this online publication.
summary: Her Campus GWU is a branch of the Her Campus website dedicated entirely to the women of the George Washington University. The online lifestyle magazine offers content on topics including style, beauty, school, DC, health, etc., all with a GW spin.

name: 100% Triumph Campus Ministry
description: 100% Triumph Campus ministry is a student organization that exists to witness to the communities of non-believing students and those who follow Jesus Christ to grow in the love and power of God by an applicable working revelation knowledge of God’s word through the help of the Holy Spirit, and to proclaim Jesus Christ not only as savior, but also as Lord and King.
summary: A faith based student organization that envisions glorious trailblazers, pacesetters, pathfinders, high-flyers, world changers, solution-bearers, salt and light students and faculty developed on campus and sent to impact the world.

name: Ukrainian-American Student Association
description: UASA's mission is to unite Ukrainians, Ukrainian-Americans, and all who support a free and independent Ukraine. We raise awareness about Ukrainian culture, traditions, and current affairs through meetings, conferences, cultural events, discussions, meetings with influential Ukrainian officials, and embassy visits. Through our organization, members will be able to build connections with fellow Ukrainians and people who are truly passionate about Ukraine. We strive to contribute to the cultural diversity at The George Washington University by providing a platform for Ukrainian culture and raising awareness of the current hardships faced by Ukraine and its people. Anyone who sees the Ukrainian mission as aligned with their personal values is welcome to join at any time!
summary: Our organization is open to Ukrainians, Ukrainian-Americans, and all dedicated to a free and independent Ukraine. The goal of UASA is to raise awareness about Ukrainian culture, traditions and current affairs.

name: Student-Alumni Programs
description:
The support of GW's global network of alumni enhances the student experience from their first day at orientation through graduation on the National Mall. By connecting with alumni, current GW students can:

  • learn more about prospective industries
  • reflect on shared experiences 
  • network through the world
  • receive excellent career or professional advice
summary: Student-alumni programs gives students an opportunities to make connections over 330,000 GW alumni through out the world.

name: The GW Local
description: The GW Local is a one-stop-shop for everything and anything GW. Written by students with a casual writing style, it will provide prospective students, freshman, and upperclassmen with an authentic view of the GW student experience. The Local is a compilation of student experiences, stories, and opinions. It is a publication that embraces the individual voices of its writers, who are not confined to the rules of traditional news-style reporting.
summary: A student life blog that encompasses all aspects of being a student at GW. A website that is a one-stop-shop for information about campus-wide news, events, and academics.

name: The Humanity First Student Organization at the George Washington University
description: Humanity First is a non-political, non-religious, and impartial global nonprofit established in 1995. HF-USA, founded in 2004, operates as a national nonprofit. Our mission is to aid socially disadvantaged individuals and families in impoverished and disaster-stricken areas worldwide. We strive to deliver sustainable development solutions while empowering local communities with the resources to become self-reliant.
summary: The Humanity First Student Organization at GWU is a chapter of Humanity First USA, an international not-for-profit humanitarian relief organization established to promote safeguard & dignity in the world’s most vulnerable and poor communities.

name: Queer and Trans People Of Color Association
description: QTPOCA will address the social, cultural, and political problems that LGBTQ+ POC face both on and off campus. We hope this community will allow POC who feel unaccepted or uncomfortable with their truth to be encouraged to live their lives as their most authentic selves. Our organization will host many opportunities for both service-oriented programs and social events that aim to support the LGBTQ+ POC community. While the organization will have focus on unique queer POC issues, we also want to build an inclusive environment and welcome the participation of anyone who is willing to learn and become an ally.
summary: QTPOCA will address the social, cultural, and political problems that LGBTQ+ POC face both on and off campus. Our organization will host many opportunities for both service-oriented programs and social events that aim to support the LGBTQ+ POC community.

name: George Washington University American Medical Women's Association
description:
The American Medical Women’s Association (AMWA) is an organization of women physicians, medical students and other persons dedicated to serving as the unique voice for women’s health and the advancement of women in medicine. The organization was founded by Dr. Bertha VanHoosen in 1915 in Chicago, at a time when women physicians were an under-represented minority. As women in medicine increase in numbers, new problems and issues arise that were not anticipated.

For a century, the American Medical Women’s Association (AMWA) has been committed to the advancement of women in medicine. Although the number of women choosing careers in medicine has grown substantially, there has not been a commensurate increase in the percentage of women in senior leadership positions. To redress this situation, AMWA has carried out ambitious advocacy efforts including research, constituency building, mentoring, leadership development, and policy reform to enable environmental and institutional transformation.

AMWA functions at the local, national, & international level to advance women in medicine & improve women’s health by providing & developing leadership, advocacy, education, expertise, mentoring, & strategic alliances.
summary: GWU AMWA is an organization for pre-health women who are looking for a community of individuals with a passion for enhancing women in medicine and women's health rights to end the stigma that exists for women in the medical field.

name: ALIANZA
description: Alianza is an organization that promotes the unity and togetherness of the Afro-Latinx community, who are often marginalized, to be seen. Through programming, we aim to share our stories and experiences with one another to create lifelong bonds and also to educate the larger community to make our presence felt on campus. Lastly, we are an organization that promotes the advancement and professional development of the Afro-Latinx community by connecting them to potential resources and allies.
summary: Alianza is an organization at GW dedicated to creating a safe space for the Afro-Latinx community. Ensuring they see themselves reflected in on-campus programming and the professional world.

name: Adopted and Fostered Students Association
description: The Adopted and Fostered Students Association supports GW’s adopted and fostered student population. We seek to promote the understanding and visibility of all GW’s adopted and fostered students while also providing them a community to celebrate their experiences. We also seek to educate about adoption and fostering to encourage these practices. Whether you are adopted, fostered, have relatives who are, or are interested in doing so in the future, all are welcome to join our community.
summary: The Adopted and Fostered Students Association is an organization that seeks to promote adoption and fostering while also providing a community for the adopted and fostered students of GW.

name: International Services Office
description: The International Services Office at the George Washington University facilitates international educational exchange through its services and programs.  It serves as the primary administrative, programming, and advising office for the GW international community.
summary: The International Services Office (ISO) facilitates international educational exchange through its services and programs.  It serves as the primary administrative, programming, and advising office for the GW international community.

name: National Press Photographers Association
description:
The GW Chapter of National Press Photographer Association is a space where students interested in photography can grow into the worlds and industries of visual journalism, fine art photography, documentary work, and more.   

We hope to foster an environment where photographs, video production, and zines can be shared, embraced, and created. In this upcoming 2025-2026 academic year, we hope to host several museum trips, print sales, partner with creative orgs on campus and at nearby universities, and curate our own exhibitions on and off campus to promote student art across the city. Additionally, we frequently bring in guest speakers with visual journalism experience and other genres of photography and videography that students can learn from and network with. 

The overall purpose of this organization is to foster a creative environment for those wanting an outlet - so come create with us!
summary: The National Press Photographers Association is dedicated to the advancement of visual journalism – its creation, practice, training, editing and distribution – in all news media and works to promote its role as a vital public service.

name: Undergraduate Economics Society
description:
The Undergraduate Economics Society (UES) is a non-partisan student organization that engages students in economics, finance, and development fields. The UES has a student-run research program (the Undergraduate Economics Review) and competes in the College Fed Challenge, a national competition between top schools hosted by the Federal Reserve. In addition, the organization hosts an array of professional development and networking events, including resume workshops, Bloomberg Terminal teach-ins, and guest speaker series. 

If you are a new member joining through Engage, please fill out this form: https://forms.gle/UDAc5EsMDVE77iuT6
summary: UES is a student organization focused on providing GW students in economics and adjacent fields with professional resources, research and publication opportunities, and a like-minded community.

name: Puerto Rican Student Association at GWU
description: The goal of the PRSA at GW is to form a community in which Puerto Rican and non-Puerto Rican students alike can come together to cultivate understanding about the island's history and traditions through educational and cultural events. We are an all emcompasing organization focused on providing career, social, and academic opportunities. Proud to provide a home away from home! Please feel free to reach out!
summary: The goal of the PRSA at GW is to form a community in which Puerto Rican and non-Puerto Rican students alike can come together to cultivate understanding about the island's history and traditions through educational and cultural events.

name: China Affairs Forum at GW
description: Previously the US-China Strategic Studies Organization, the China Affairs Forum at GW is an organization driven by George Washington University students, dedicated to studying China from an objective and nonpartisan perspective. By establishing intellectual forums to discuss, analyze, learn about, and report on various international strategic developments regarding the Eastern nation, the necessity to understand and critically dive into Chinese domestic and foreign policy issues as young Americans is heavily emphasized. Follow and join us to become part of our movement to bring a fresh perspective to China studies and issues, while uplifting student voices in public speaking environments and providing a platform to build valuable professional skills. With the link below, join our mailing list to receive our weekly newsletter or express your interest in becoming a part of the team as a Contributor. All are welcome to follow and join us! —> linktr.ee/cafatgw
summary: The China Affairs Forum at GW is a non-biased, non-partisan intellectual forum where emerging student thinkers and leaders can develop, exchange, and delve into knowledge about the rising issues concerning China and the world.

name: Class Year Programs
description:
The Class Year Programs office is dedicated to fostering a sense of community and belonging among students throughout their undergraduate journey. 

Our office designs unique experiences tailored to each class year, from the excitement of first-year orientation to the celebration of Senior Week. We believe in creating opportunities for students to connect with their peers, build lasting memories, and embrace GW traditions. By offering meaningful connections and community-building activities, Class Year Programs support students as they grow, learn, and make the most of their time at GW.
summary: Our office designs unique experiences tailored to each class year,We believe in creating opportunities for students to connect with their peers, build lasting memories, and embracing GW traditions.

name: Black Defiance
description: Black Defiance focuses on eradicating white supremacy and injustices against Black people within our own university (GW), in addition to DC as a whole, through a feminist lens. We are committed to raising the consciousness of Black students and allies in an effort to fight for our collective liberation.
summary: Black Defiance focuses on eradicating white supremacy and injustices against Black people within our own university (GW), in addition, to DC as a whole through a feminist lens. All in all, fighting racism and the patriarchy our university and a movement.

name: Criminal Justice Student Association
description:
The Criminal Justice Student Association (CJSA) is a student organization on campus that allows students to come together who are seeking a better understanding of the criminal justice issues that our country is currently struggling with. CJSA strives to provide a platform to those who want to do more, but are not informed on how to do so, and will foster a community for students with similar interests. CJSA welcomes anyone to join the club! This includes criminal justice and sociology, majors, minors, and concentrations, as well as people who are simply interested in criminal justice-related issues!    

If you are interested in being notified about upcoming events and programing please join our Group Me!
summary: The Criminal Justice Student Association (CJSA) is a student organization on campus that allows students to come together who are seeking a better understanding of the criminal justice issues that our country is currently struggling with.

name: Black Professionals in International Affairs
description: Founded in 2020, The George Washington University Black Professionals in International Affairs (BPIA) is dedicated to enhancing, promoting, and increasing African and African diaspora (African-American, Afro-Latinx, Afro-Caribbean) participation, conversation, and perspective in both the Elliott School of International Affairs and the field of international affairs overall. BPIA has a goal of affirming, valuing, and amplifying the voices and concerns of Black students in the Elliott School of International Affairs on and off campus, as well as, strengthening and encouraging one another in what can be an alienating field. This organization aims to provide a supportive, enriching environment for fostering intellectual community and facilitating the development of both African and African Diaspora studies and thought.
summary: Black Professionals in International Affairs has a purpose of increasing and enhancing the participation of African & African Diaspora students in both the Elliott School of International Affairs and the field of international relations overall.

name: Wellbeing at GW
description: The college experience is portrayed as the "best years of your life." However, many students find that college can bring about stress, anxiety, burnout, and overwhelm. At GW, we give you access to comprehensive and holistic wellbeing resources and programming so you can thrive during your time here. Follow @wellbeingatGW for more sexy and smart content!
summary: Sexy. Smart. Supported.

name: Data Science Association
description: With a growth mindset and collaborative energy, the Graduate students and young alumni of the Graduate Data Science program founded the GW Data Science Association (DSA) in January 2020. This organization represents all graduate and undergraduate students studying Data Science at GWU. The mission of the DSA is to act as a bridge between all GW Data Science students and alumni, and the larger Data Science community across the DMV (DC, Maryland, and Virginia) area.
summary: The GW Data Science Association (GW DSA) is committed to elevating the experience of all Graduate and Undergraduate students studying Data Science at GWU through educational, social, and networking events.

name: Swing Left GWU
description: Our mission is to mobilize students to volunteer, using Swing Left’s tools and strategies, to elect Democrats up and down the ballot in 2024 and beyond. We provide an opportunity for students to take action and volunteer in the most effective and impactful way. We hope to build long-term organizing power and transform our government.
summary: Our mission is to mobilize students to volunteer, using Swing Left’s tools and strategy, to elect Democrats up and down the ballot. We provide an opportunity for students to take action in the most effective way and transform our government.

name: Office of Campus Recreation
description: Campus Recreation strives to create a space for students to move joyfully, explore different types of physical activity, enjoy constructive competition opportunities for individuals of all abilities, and connect with others in meaningful ways. Our home base for programming is the Lerner Health and Wellness Center. Our department provides a variety of programming opportunities, such as intramural sports, club sports, group fitness, personal training, fitness clinics and workshops, outdoor education, and certification opportunities. We also have one of the largest groups of student staff at GW, which allows us to cultivate many leadership, customer service, and continuing education opportunities for them.
summary: Campus Recreation strives to create a space for students to move joyfully, explore different types of physical activity, enjoy constructive competition opportunities for individuals of all abilities, and connect with others.

name: GW Eco-Reps Program
description: Eco-Reps are sustainability leaders who promote a culture of sustainability within the GW residence halls and community. We inform and engage students in activities that encourage a sustainable lifestyle. As an Eco-Rep, you will educate students on sustainability topics and build connections with the sustainability community at GW.
summary: Eco-Reps are sustainability leaders who promote a culture of sustainability within the GW residence halls and community.

name: GW Spirit: Cheer & Dance  (campus partner/affiliate)
description:
GW Spirit is a campus partner and not a student organization, but students can join Spirit by trying out.
GW Spirit is proudly committed to supporting GW athletics and aims to foster school spirit and excitement on and off the court. Video tryouts for both teams will be accepted through September 15. Please email gwcheer@gwu.edu or gwdance@gwu.edu for tryout requirements and more information. Here are our social links:
 
DANCE:
Facebook
Instagram
Twitter
YouTube
 
CHEER:
Facebook
Instagram
Twitter
Tiktok
summary: The GW Cheer Team and First Ladies Dance Team perform at all home men's and women's basketball games, travel to tournaments, and appear at GW and community events. GW Spirit is proudly committed to supporting GW athletics and school spirit campus wide.

name: University Events
description: The University Events Team ensures the continuation of annual traditions such as Welcome Day of Service and Convocation, Commencement, Presidential Distinguished Event Series, and other annual traditions.
summary: The University Events Team ensures the continuation of annual traditions such as Welcome Day of Service and Convocation, Commencement, Presidential Distinguished Event Series, and other annual traditions.

name: Office of Advocacy and Support
description: The Office of Advocacy and Support (OAS) provides trauma-informed and healing-centered confidential support to GW students impacted by sexual violence, domestic violence, intimate partner violence, and/or stalking. OAS plays a crucial role in fostering a community that values and promotes healthy relationships through support and prevention programming. OAS is committed to cultivating and strengthening a culture of care that empathetically supports and empowers GW community members. In service of this mission, OAS is proud to work with campus partners to create a safer, healthier, and more informed campus community.
summary: The Office of Advocacy and Support (OAS) provides trauma-informed and healing-centered confidential support to GW students impacted by sexual violence, relationship violence, and/or stalking.

name: Elliott School - Graduate Student Services
description: The Office of Graduate Student Services (GSS) combines graduate academic advising, international education and career development under one roof in order to help students plan their studies for their future career. It is dedicated to providing guidance throughout a graduate student's tenure at the Elliott School and worldwide career opportunities to current graduate students and graduate alumni.
summary: The Office of Graduate Student Services (GSS) combines graduate academic advising, international education and career development under one roof in order to help students plan their studies for their future career.

name: Environmental Justice Action Network
description: The Environmental Justice Action Network (EJAN) is a group of GW students dedicated to advancing and bringing awareness to the field of Environmental Justice. EJAN, much like the Environmental Justice (EJ) Movement, is multifaceted. EJAN’s first focal point is education, where its members discuss books, current events, and attend webinars related to EJ issues. EJAN strives to be inclusive and intersectional when educating others and researching these topics. EJAN’s second focus is action-centered. EJAN members directly volunteer within marginalized communities and collaborate with them to alleviate their environmental stresses. The last concentration of EJAN is advocacy. EJAN elevates awareness of EJ issues both on the GW campus and into the broader D.C. community. EJAN is a community of GW students that strive to uphold the ideals of EJ and is building a radical coalition of like-minded social justice-oriented students that want to make a change in their community. Please contact us at gwu.ejan@gmail.com if you would like to get involved!
summary: The Environmental Justice Action Network (EJAN) is a coalition of GW students that advance the field of Environmental Justice through an intersectional and inclusive framework. Our focal points are education, direct action and advocacy.

name: Black Student Athlete Alliance
summary: BSAA, created by George Washington's black students athletes, is a space to connect, raise awareness, and engage in dialogue about salient issues regarding race on campus and in the country.

name: The Women's Network: GW
description:
A women-led networking community that connects college students to professional leaders. Open to all women, femmes, and non-binary students, this organization is dedicated to redefining ambition, empowering our peers, and preparing future leaders. 

Thank you for joining our engage page! To be considered an official member please fill out our membership form here: bit.ly/jointwn
summary: A Network for Women

name: The Women's Pre-Law Student Association
description:
Welcome to the Women's Pre-Law Student Association, where EVERYONE has a voice and the potential for a fulfilling, empowering career.

Our organization is a dynamic and supportive community dedicated to nurturing the next generation of leading women in law. As a member, you'll find a network of like-minded individuals who share a passion for law, justice, and breaking barriers. In WPLSA, we provide a platform for you to thrive. Whether you're exploring the legal field or already committed to a legal career, our student organization will equip you with the knowledge, skills, and connections to succeed.

Join us in our mission to foster a diverse and inclusive legal community. From resume and career workshops, mentorship programs, local community service events, and engaging social events, we strive to empower every member to reach their full potential!
summary: Discover your voice, build lasting connections, find your passions, and prepare for a future in law. Join the Women's Pre-Law Student Association!

name: Conflict Education and Student Accountability
description: Conflict Education & Student Accountability (CESA) engages across GW to foster equitable and restorative accountability to community standards. We do this through promoting individual rights and communal responsibilities, supporting community members to identify and repair harm, and growing community capacity for conflict management.
summary: Conflict Education & Student Accountability engages across GW to foster equitable and restorative accountability to community standards.

name: George Washington Men's Rowing
description: Since 1956, GW Men’s Rowing has represented the university by competing in one of America’s oldest collegiate sports. As 2021 and 2022 National Champions, 2nd in the nation in 2023, 3rd at 2023 HOCR, and more, GW Men’s Rowing seeks to continue its track record of success as a high-performing club team.
summary: A continuation of the Varsity Men's Rowing Team who lost their status as a Varsity Team. GW Men's Rowing has existed for over half a century, and this team will continue to uphold the legacy of its oarsmen and develop competitive and upstanding humans.

name: George Washington Squash Club
summary: GW Squash Club is made up of a group of highly energetic and enthusiastic body of university students who are dedicated to the development and love of squash. We seek to encourage good sportsmanship, positive team play and the enjoyment of the game.

name: The GW Sailing Team
description: The GW sailing team's purpose is to form a community of competitive sailors focused on learning and growing as a team on the intercollegiate level. Please check out our website (gwsailing.com) to learn more about us and fill out our prospective sailors form if you're interested in sailing with us.
summary: The GW sailing team's purpose is to form a community of competitive sailors focused on learning and growing as a team on the intercollegiate level. Please check out our website (gwsailing.com) to learn more about us.

name: GWPD Community Outreach
description:
The mission of the Division of Safety and Security is to provide a safe and secure campus environment for all members of the university community. This mission is achieved through active participation of all university members and partnerships with local law enforcement and community partners.

The George Washington Police Department (GWPD) provides residential hall security and patrol services to the Foggy Bottom and Mount Vernon campuses and oversees security at the VSTC campus. The Department also coordinates safety & security for a variety of on-campus special events, including Commencement, Colonial Weekend and visits by dignitaries. In addition to those roles and responsibilities, GWPD provides crime prevention education to the GW community.
summary: The George Washington University Police Department provides a range of services to the GW community that play an important role in our community policing efforts.

name: Dear Asian Youth
description: We are the GW chapter of Dear Asian Youth, a global organization dedicated to uplifting and promoting the Asian community through intersectional activism. On ​campus, we have created a platform for Asians and non-Asian allies to amplify Asian voices and narratives through our bi-annual literary arts magazine and other digital platforms.
summary: Dear Asian Youth at GW is cultural organization for Asians and Non-Asian allies dedicated to amplifying Asian voices and narratives through our literary arts magazine and other digital platforms.

name: GW Maternal and Child Health Network
description: The purpose and goals of this organization are to (1) educate about and increase awareness of MCH issues and projects within the United States and abroad; (2) develop, foster, and sustain MCH competencies that extend beyond academic curricula and focus on research and real-world applications (3) connect students with service and learning opportunities in MCH; (4) create opportunities for mentorship and learning among alumni and students of George Washington University Milken Institute School of Public Health; (4) provide opportunities for interaction and community engagement among students interested in MCH, as well as MCH faculty and staff; (5) develop opportunities to enhance skills to increase preparedness to join the MCH workforce; and (6) support research interests that address priority needs of MCH populations, locally, nationally and globally and increase the evidence base for decision-makers and practitioners.
summary: This organization has been established to empower and equip future leaders in  Maternal and Child Health to make an impact in their communities, at the state and national levels, and globally.

name: The Black Girl Pre-Health Collective
description: The purpose of the Black Girl Pre-Health Collective is to create a space for mentorship, unity, and motivation for Black girls in Pre-Health concentrations at the George Washington University. The BGPC aims to overcome the challenges and obstacles faced by Black girls on the journey to becoming healthcare professionals and assist our members in becoming successful and as prepared as possible for their future applications.
summary: The purpose of the Black Girl Pre-Health Collective is to create a space for mentorship, unity, and motivation for the Black girls in Pre-Health concentrations at George Washington University.

name: Student Consortium on Women, Peace and Security George Washington University Chapter
description: The Student Consortium on Women, Peace, and Security (WPS) is a student-led organization that aims to — in collaboration with the US Civil Society Working Group on WPS (U.S. CSWG) — promote awareness of the implications of women and gender in security and foreign policy. We strive to ensure that policymakers around the globe are aware of the importance of gender issues in these disciplines and the need to institutionalize a feminist approach when solving critical issues surrounding peace and security.
summary: In collaboration with the US Civil Society Working Group on WPS (U.S. CSWG), our organization aims to promote awareness of the implications of women and gender in security and foreign policy.

name: Hairapeutic Beauty
summary: Everything hair! To educate and mentor young, college women and men about their kinks, coils, and waves while creating a safe space for them to connect with upcoming hairstylists on campus.

name: Timmy Global Health GWU
description: Timmy Global Health GWU essentially empowers GW students to provide more access to healthcare for the underserved DC communities and Montecristi in the Dominican Republic. Timmy GW is a chapter of the umbrella national organization, Timmy Global Health (TGH). TGH is an independent, non-partisan 501C3 organization aimed to empower students to make a meaningful difference in access to healthcare both locally and globally by advocating, fundraising, and serving. Timmy GW would translate these operations to provide for the DC community and our global partner, Banelino. Banelino is a public health/medically focused organization in Montecristi. Banelino consists of a group of banana-farm producers who have had the resources to build a medical center and provide their underserved Montecristi community with doctors, nurses, healthcare workers, and medical supplies. TGH financially supports these operations and completely covers patients in need of special medical care. Timmy GW would help provide TGH the money to support Banelino by fundraising at least $3,000 a year. Timmy GW would also support Banelino through advocacy pertaining to public health issues in Montecristi and traveling to Montecristi for a yearly medical service trip. The other half of Timmy GW's operations will include advocacy, fundraising, and service for the DC community. First, to advocate for DC's underserved communities, Timmy GW has been and continues to raise awareness about local health issues within DC and U.S. through our social media accounts. Second, we would raise money for local service organizations in DC that have already been serving minority and disadvantaged communities in the greater DC area. And lastly, we plan to focus immensely on volunteering in DC through our own service events and participating in local service through the DC Medical Reserve Corps, Bread for the City, Mary’s Center, and more prominent organizations in DC. The goal of all of our operations is to ultimately provide under-serviced and under-resourced communities including our own with more access to healthcare. Each and every one of our operations will strictly abide by this goal.
summary: Timmy Global Health GWU empowers students to advocate, fundraise, and serve to increase access to health care in the underserved communities of DC and Montecristi, Dominican Republic.

name: Global Surgery Student Alliance
description:
The Global Surgery Student Alliance (GSSA) is the national global surgery working group for the U.S. which launched in January 2017 with the purpose of bringing together students who are interested in global surgery and who have a desire to contribute to the field. Since starting, GSSA has helped 40+ schools around the country create their own local global surgery working groups. Through their affiliation with the International Student Surgical Network (InciSioN), GSSA is connected with similar national working groups around the world who are working towards the same goal of increasing access to safe and affordable surgical care for all people.

The GW Global Surgery Student Alliance (GSSA) Chapter is launched with a similar purpose of bringing together students who are interested in global surgery. The organization believes that students are a valuable resource for the advancement of global surgery. By exposing students to the field early on, the organization hopes to help them build their medical careers with global surgery in mind. Ultimately, many students are willing and able to make significant contributions to global surgery, and the organization hopes to be a resource that facilitates those efforts.
summary: The GW Global Surgery Student Alliance (GSSA) Chapter is launched with the purpose of bringing together students who are interested in global surgery and who have a desire to contribute to the field.

name: Club 18
description: Club 18 or Foggy Bottom Jewish Women is a Jewish women's organization here on campus. Here to provide a safe space for Jewish women at GW!
summary: Club 18 or Foggy Bottom Jewish Women, is a Jewish women's organization here on campus. Here to provide a safe space for Jewish women at GW!

name: Corcoran School of the Arts & Design
description: The Corcoran School of the Arts & Design at the George Washington University prepares its graduates to be the next generation of global leaders. Part of the Columbian College of Arts and Sciences, the school functions as an incubator for artists, designers and scholars, who learn from internationally renowned faculty at the intersection of creativity and social innovation. It is an academic experience like no other—inspiring students to research, create, and innovate with the potential to shape the world around them.
summary: Part of the Columbian College of Arts and Sciences, the Corcoran School of the Arts & Design at the George Washington University prepares its graduates to be the next generation of global leaders.

name: GW Azerbaijani Student Association
description:
The Azerbaijani Student Association is founded to bring together those who are passionate about Azerbaijani culture, customs, holidays, history, heritage, the country itself, and language.

 

The core missions of The Azerbaijani Student Association:

- The Azerbaijani Student Association will organize social and cultural events such as seminars, exhibitions, movie nights, culture days, festivities, and gatherings in order to promote Azerbaijan and its multicultural heritage.

 

- The Azerbaijani Student Association will work with other campus groups to support initiatives to promote intercultural understanding and diversity.

 

- The Azerbaijani Student Association will offer assistance to existing and prospective members, assisting them in overcoming obstacles that they may encounter while adjusting to a new cultural and educational environment.

 

- Promotion of George Washington University and American student culture in the Azerbaijani society and universities, facilitating the cross-connection between student communities.
summary: The Azerbaijani Student Association is founded to bring together those who are passionate about Azerbaijani culture, customs, holidays, history, heritage, the country itself, and language.

name: The Foreign Film Society
description:
The GW Foreign Film Society educates the GW community on global issues through the medium of cinema. The GW Foreign Film Society acts as a central organization on campus for students of various backgrounds and identities to converge and discuss films and their pivotal relevance to everyday life and history. We intend to create a safe space for discussions on historical events, international politics, and society alongside GW Elliott faculty members who provide context to thematic topics relevant to the field of International Relations. By doing so, the GW Foreign Film Society hopes to increase the appreciation of international films and provide a deeper understanding of their significance through robust conversations and added context while addressing various misconceptions and stereotypes of cultural communities and the perception of the international community.

Letterboxd: ffsgwu

Instagram: @ffs.gwu
summary: The GW Foreign Film Society aspires to create an inclusive and engaging community that aims to view and discuss the importance and relevance of classic and contemporary films from around the world at the intersection of International Affairs.

name: Pre-Dental Society
description: The purpose of this organization is to foster a community for pre-dental students at George Washington University. Our goal is to support and guide members through the dental school admissions process by providing relevant resources and mentorship. We are committed to helping our members gain exposure to the dental profession and also prepare for the dental school admissions process through shadowing, volunteering, and application preparation resources. We provide a great space to meet fellow students pursuing a career in dentistry to share information and experiences.
summary: The GWU Pre-Dental Society is dedicated to empower members with the knowledge, resources, and connections needed to thrive on the path to dental school (or oral health related fields), while promoting a passion for oral health and patient care.

name: OneWorld GW
description: OneWorld GW is a non-partisan environmental organization dedicated to combating climate change, building a sustainable future, and reconnecting students with the natural world. Through service and storytelling, OneWorld inspires students to Think Globally and Act Locally about our collective responsibility to care for the planet. Regarding service, OneWorld invites students into a relationship with their immediate environment through partnerships with local service organizations. Regarding storytelling, OneWorld invites the GW community to learn from rather than learn about our interconnectedness with nature from speakers at the front line of climate change. From service to storytelling, OneWorld cultivates an environment of inclusivity that encourages members of all backgrounds to work together to address the unifying issue of environmental conservation.
summary: OneWorld GW is a non-partisan environmental organization dedicated to combating climate change, building a sustainable future, and reconnecting students with the natural world.

name: GW Entrepreneurship Club
description:
GW Entrepreneurship Club is a place for students of any school, major, and experience to come together to learn more about entrepreneurship. GWEC's mission is to create an accessible and approachable organization for those at any step in the entrepreneurial process. Whether you are an established business owner or someone just looking to learn more, GWEC is ready to welcome you to our community! 

 

You can officially register to be a member through this Engage profile, but if you would like more information about the organization, the eboard, or our schedule for the semester, check out our Instagram page linked below. 

 

We also communicate via GroupMe: Join here!

 

We are looking forward to meeting you soon!
summary: GW Entrepreneurship Club is a place for you to create, learn, and network. GWEC was created by students, for students, and invites anyone who is curious about entrepreneurship to join.

name: Bridges International
description:
Bridges International is a caring community of Christ-followers committed to service. We engage in spiritual conversations with international students and promote social connections to help them become future leaders. These four areas are the cornerstones of what we hope to do alongside the GWU community:

  1. Service: Helping new students adjust to life in America and holding events to serve the community together.
  2. Socialization: Creating spaces for developing friendships as interpersonal relationships are fundamental to thriving and feeling welcome in a new place.
  3. Spirituality: Offering resources about Christianity to interested students, spiritual discussions, and offering support to young adults with day-to-day struggles. 
  4. Leadership: Developing leadership skills in the formative years of University students to work and grow as a team.
summary: Bridges International is a caring community of Christ-followers committed to service. We engage in spiritual conversations with international students and promote social connections to help them become future leaders.

name: Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority, Incorporated - Eta Eta Chapter
description:
Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority, Incorporated was established in 1975 at Kean University in Union, New Jersey. We are the first and largest Latina sorority in the nation. Our chapter, Eta Eta, was founded on November 28, 2021. Our national philanthropy is St. Jude Children's Research Hospital. The purpose of Lambda Theta Alpha shall be to provide a sisterhood based on unity, love, and respect in an effort to foster the development of strong leaders who will then provide and practice political, social, and cultural activities. It shall also be the purpose of Lambda Theta Alpha to promote unity through charitable and educational programs, maintain a higher standard of learning and serve as a voice for all students.

 

Interested in joining LTA? 

Fill out this link: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdmWPCCtprIcgD2dOrPfOdHPk8yPaZTa459LMBWeA2hAC09ag/viewform
summary: The purpose of Lambda Theta Alpha shall be to provide a sisterhood based on unity, love, and respect in an effort to foster the development of strong leaders who will then provide and practice political, social, and cultural activities.

name: Office for Student Success
summary: To support academic progress, student engagement, and well-being to ensure student satisfaction and successful student experiences.

name: GW Club Climbing
summary: GW Club Climbing is a group of students who enjoy the mental and physical challenge of indoor climbing. From bouldering to rope climbing, we aim to push our limits in a supportive team environment. Whether you are new or experienced, come climb with us!

name: GW Dining
description: The GW Dining teams mission is to makes students excited about eating food by providing nutritious meal and creating fun events that revolve around the table.
summary: The GW Dining teams mission is to makes students excited about eating food by providing nutritious meal and creating fun events that revolve around the table.

name: BridgeGW
description: BridgeGW is investing in the future of democracy by developing the next generation of engaged, constructive, and informed citizens. BridgeGW believes that good governance starts with constructive political discussion and empathetic action. Our organization works with America’s future leaders on their college campuses to foster spaces wherein a diverse range of ideas can engage one another through the practice of responsible discourse. BridgeGW wants to move the political conversation forward, by allowing it to happen in the home of academic progress and innovation: the campus of The George Washington University.
summary: The mission of The George Washington University chapter of BridgeUSA (BridgeGW) is to promote positive political discourse, viewpoint diversity, and leadership through means of discussion, grassroots impact, and events on campus.

name: Women in Cybersecurity at The George Washington University
description:
From the Women in Cybersecurity (WiCyS) website:

“Established in 2012 by Dr. Ambareen Siraj of Tennessee Tech University through a National Science Foundation grant partnerships, and collaboration benefits for all women, allies, and advocates with a strong mission to recruit, retain and advance women in cybersecurity. At WiCyS, a global community of women, allies, and advocates, we are dedicated to bringing talented women together to celebrate and foster their passion and drive for cybersecurity. We unite local communities of aspiring and thriving women cybersecurity professionals across the world to collaborate, share their knowledge, network and mentor. We create opportunities through professional development programs, conferences, career fairs, and more.”

Join our Discord Channel! https://discord.gg/rBTVSYUFQe
summary: At WiCyS, a global community of women, allies, and advocates, we are dedicated to bringing talented women together to celebrate and foster their passion and drive for cybersecurity through professional development programs, conferences, and career fairs.

name: The American Society of Mechanical Engineers
description: ASME is a not-for-profit professional organization that enables collaboration, knowledge sharing and skill development across all engineering disciplines, while promoting the vital role of the engineer in society. The GW ASME Section brings together students from a multitude of engineering and professional backgrounds through student events, ASME sponsored competitions, guest lecturers, and scholarship opportunities.
summary: ASME is a not-for-profit professional organization that enables collaboration, knowledge sharing and skill development across all engineering disciplines, while promoting the vital role of the engineer in society. ASME codes and standards, publications,

name: Anchor Christian Fellowship
description: Anchor Christian Fellowship is a diverse community of Christ followers and anyone seeking to engage in teachings and discussions regarding the Bible and Christianity, apologetics, worldviews, culture, and college life. We offer a safe place that welcomes students of all backgrounds to explore Christianity from an intellectual lens and helps Christians who want to be discipled develop their faith. We strive to build relationships with God and one another throughout college years and beyond.
summary:
Anchor Christian Fellowship is a diverse community for anyone seeking to
engage in teachings and discussions regarding the Bible and Christianity, apologetics, culture, and college life. We help students develop their faith through an intellectual lens.

name: DC Cru
description:
We think that Jesus is the greatest person to ever live and that our relationship with him is the most fulfilling thing on earth. We believe this so much that we want everyone in DC (whether you would call yourself a Christian or not) to have the opportunity to know him, too. In fact, Jesus is the whole reason we exist, and he is the focus of our mission: Cru is a community where the gospel captures hearts, transforms lives, and launches men and women into a lifelong adventure with Jesus.

The second thing we are about is community. Nobody likes to be alone, and so we make it a priority to have an incredible community. While probably no one in Cru knows everyone else in Cru, there are so many interconnected groups of close-knit friends that there’s a place for everyone to get involved. But we don’t stop there. We’re not content to just be a community together. We want our community to grow, where our love for Jesus and our natural desire for community come together.
summary: Cru is a Christian campus ministry about two things: Jesus and Community. It is a community where the gospel captures hearts, transforms lives, and launches men and women into a lifelong adventure with Jesus Christ.

name: GW Healing Clinic
summary: Healing Clinic is a sub-organization under the Medical Center Student Council (MCSC) umbrella.

name: The Dragon Party
description: The Dragon Party is a student-led organization dedicated to encouraging the instruction and enjoyment of American Mah Jongg in the GW community. This group was founded with the intention to create a space to teach American Mah Jongg to interested students, and as well, provide an arena for lighthearted games and tournaments to take place.
summary: An American Mah Jongg League

name: American Lung Cancer Screening Initiative
description:
The American Lung Cancer Screening Initiative (ALCSI) is a non-profit organization comprising of a team over 200 doctors and students working to increase awareness of lung cancer and lung cancer screening in communities across the country. Our mission is to improve the rate of lung cancer screenings for high-risk patients and reduce the rate of lung cancer through outreach efforts. One such effort is working with congress to pass legislation for promoting lung cancer awareness. We have successfully worked with elected officials and representatives to receive proclamations from almost all of the 50 states. We have multiple university chapters across the nation, such as in Harvard University, UC Berkley, UCLA, and Tufts University. Now, we plan to bring ALCSI right to D.C. at the George Washington University. For more information about us, please visit: https://www.alcsi.org/

At the GWU Chapter of ALCSI, we hope to continue to fight to increase awareness of lung cancer in our GWU community and around D.C. by working with elected officials to pass legislation regarding lung cancer screening and promote anti-vaping in our middle and high schools. Lung cancer killed more people in D.C. than breast and colon cancer combined between the years of 2014 and 2018. We hope to eliminate this alarming statistic through partnering with local lung cancer organizations, health agencies, and hospitals, hosting workshops and large events, volunteering our time at schools and retirement homes to increase awareness, putting up posters and giving presentations around D.C., working with elected officials to craft legislation supporting our cause, and more! One project we would like to take on is putting QR codes around campus which would take people to the lung cancer screening page at the GWU Hospital - Thoracic Surgery Department.
summary: The goal of this organization is to raise awareness about lung cancer and to increase the rate of lung cancer screening amongst high risk patients.

name: Museum Studies Student Association
description: The GW Museum Studies Association, or the MSSA, represents, advocates, and provides a space for all graduate students enrolled in the Museum Studies program at GW. It seeks to provide a forum for professional development and community building for all students in the program and act as their voice in communicating with faculty, the university, and other agencies.
summary: The official student association of the GW Graduate Museum Studies Program.

name: Poker Club
description: The poker club is the premiere spot for Texas hold’em on campus! We play every Friday (please see our events for dates and times). We have the best live poker games and members! We’re excited for you to join us! Please check out our website for game details and updates at https://linktr.ee/Gwupoker
summary: The Poker Club is the premiere spot for Texas Hold’em on campus! We play every Friday from 5:30pm - 11pm. The perfect place to learn and hone your poker skills! We’re excited for you to join us! Please check out our Instagram for game details and updates

name: Phi Gamma Delta
description:
Phi Gamma Delta strives to cultivate a community of active, vital forces of men who courageously live their values and make a positive impact on college and community. The values of Phi Gamma Delta are friendship, service, morality, knowledge, and excellence. These traits are reflected in FIJI men and exemplified daily.

Members of Phi Gamma Delta follow a motto set by our founding fathers hundreds of years ago; Not for College Days alone. Phi Gamma Delta brothers emphasize building community and lifelong friendships that expand beyond a members' 4 years at college. Examples of prestigious postgraduate Phi Gamma Delta brothers include one U.S. President (Calvin Coolidge), four U.S. Vice Presidents, six Pulitzer Prize winners, two Nobel Prize winners, over 80 competitors in the Olympic Games , and at least seven billionaires.

Lastly Phi Gamma Delta prides itself on setting a new precedent for greek life on college campuses across the country. The anti-hazing initiative set by Phi Gamma Delta International Headquarters is taken seriously and is prohibited for all brothers across the country.
summary: Phi Gamma Delta unites men in enduring friendships, stimulates the pursuit of knowledge, and builds courageous leaders who serve the world with the best that is in them.

name: GW Deutscher Kulturverein
shortName: German Club/ German Cultural Association / DKV
description:
Wilkommen! If you are interested in German culture this org is the place for you! You don't have to know German, be German, or know where Germany is, so long as you like having fun and learning about German culture you're welcome here!

We host fun events like movie nights, food events, and excursions to German cultural places. We also provide ways for students to learn/practice their German with German study sessions and Speak with a German events. Look out for our annual Oktoberfest celebration in the fall! 

You can contact us at the club email: gwgermanclub22@gmail.com. Also follow us on Instagram @germanclub_gwu!
summary: Wilkommen! If you are interested in German culture this org is the place for you! You don't have to know German, be German, or know where Germany is, so long as you like having fun and learning about German culture, you're welcome here!

name: GW Russian-Speaking Association
description: The Russian Speakers Association is a community that welcomes individuals of all proficiency levels in the Russian language. Our primary focus is on fostering a sense of community among Russian speakers, providing a supportive environment for language learners, heritage speakers and native speakers and offering opportunities for linguistic growth. We also aim to create a space where members can deepen their understanding of the diverse cultures associated with Russian-speaking and post USSR countries and meet other Russian Speakears. Whether you're a beginner or an advanced speaker, the Russian Speaking Association is dedicated to creating and enriching experience for all who share an interest in the Russian language and its rich cultural heritage.
summary: GW RSA is the only Russian-speaking community on GWU campus. The main goal of our organization is celebrating and sharing the various cultures of post-USSR countries, as well as providing a space for speaking and practicing the Russian language.

name: George Washington University Fitness Club
description: GWFIT is an organization aimed at forming an integrative fitness community across campus. We are looking for members who want to begin their fitness journeys to those who have a deep understanding of fitness. The club will host group workouts that vary from outdoor cardio circuits to group lifting workouts in order to guide or inspire members on how they can program their own workouts. GWFIT will also have meetings to discuss how fitness is portrayed in social media and topics related to body positivity, as well as many obstacles people starting to get into fitness may face. While not all the officers of GWFIT are certified personal trainers, GWFIT is very member based. The officers will inform the members about what has worked for them from an exercise to a nutrition standpoint in order to achieve their goals. GWFIT also allows members to find their possible future workout buddies as within the club individuals meet others who may have similar goals to them.
