GWFIT is also aiming to cater to students outside the club by planning various events such as a 5k run or walk before finals for people to decompress, as well as possibly planning field days for DC public schools such as The School Without Walls.
summary: GWFIT is an organization looking for students who are into fitness or looking to get into fitness and are interested in being part of an integrative fitness community. GWFIT focuses on both the physical and mental aspects of fitness and welcomes everyone

name: Office for Diversity, Equity, and Community Engagement
description:
Our mission and vision are driven by our commitment to inclusive excellence. Inclusive excellence is the recognition that an organization’s success is conditional on how well it engages, includes, and honors the rich diversity of its community members, including its students, faculty, staff, alumni, friends and affiliates.   We believe in order to understand and work toward inclusive excellence, it is critical to acknowledge and embody the following:

  • Go beyond noticing and acknowledging our differences to embracing differences.  
  • Recognize that education, policies, curriculum, physical space, access to basic needs, systems, and overall conditions impact the community’s ability to thrive
  • Believe that each unique person and experience contributes to our learning
  • Believe having an array of different backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences is essential for academic excellence
  • Understand that having differences comes with the responsibility to ensure that everyone is able to fully participate, remain authentic, thrive, feel safe, and lead
summary: To be completed

name: Student Government Association (Executive Branch)
summary: SA Exec

name: Delta Theta Chapter of Omega Psi Phi Fraternity Inc.
description: Delta Theta Chapter was chartered on the campus of Southeastern University in 1969 and later re-chartered at The George Washington University in 2008. Our motto is "Friendship is Essential to the Soul" and we aim to empower and form a more perfect union of college men and empower the surrounding community through manhood, scholarship, perseverance, and uplift.
summary: Our chapter is chartered at The George Washington University and Georgetown University. Our motto is "Friendship is Essential to the Soul" and we aim to empower and better the surrounding community through manhood, scholarship, perseverance, and uplift.

name: Student Occupational Therapy Association
description: The purpose of this organization is to provide awareness of the occupational therapy profession: to represent our fellow students and our community through one unifying voice: to foster opportunities and access to resources: to promote personal growth and well being to those we serve within our student body and our community: to provide opportunities for professional development to our students; to  advocate and protect those without a voice within our communities through community engagement at the local, state, and national levels: to provide support and opportunities for collaboration among our student body and to foster well-being within our student body and our community.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide awareness of the occupational therapy profession through professional development by providing opportunities for community engagement.

name: GW Effective Altruism
description: GW Effective Altruism aims to promote Effective Altruism in all its forms. This includes encouraging charitable donations, promoting cost-effectiveness in charitable giving, and using one’s career to improve the world. GW Effective Altruism will explain the ideas of Effective Altruism and support our members on how to put those ideas into practice.
summary: GW Effective Altruism is a community of people asking, "How can I use my resources of both time and money to do the most good?" We aim to explore that question through fellowships, speaker events, and connecting with the global EA community!

name: Milken Institute School of Public Health Doctoral Student Association
description: The Milken Institute School of Public Health Doctoral Student Association is the organization dedicated to furthering the interests and needs of doctoral students in Milken Institute School of Public Health. Our mission is to foster an interactive space for current students to connect with colleagues across departments and raise any doctoral program related questions and concerns to be discussed. We aim to provide various networking and career events, as well as academic surveys on a regular basis.
summary: MDSA is the organization dedicated to furthering the interests and needs of PhD and DrPH students in Milken Institute School of Public Health.

name: ESIA South Asian Board
description: The ESIA South Asian Board aims to provide a platform for professional development opportunities to South Asian graduate-level students at the Elliott School of International Affairs. Professional development initiatives such as networking events, career development sessions, panel discussions, and talks may be organized within university policies that are aligned with the advancement of interests of South Asian graduate students in the field of International Affairs and International Development.
summary: The ESIA South Asian Board aims to provide a platform for professional development and networking opportunities to South Asian graduate-level students at the Elliott School of International Affairs.

name: GW Reads!
description:
GW Reads! is an on campus book club dedicated to increasing accessibility to all types of literature as well as creating a community of bibliophiles. We have a Book of the Month that we choose based on input from our members or current events. We meet every few weeks to discuss our thoughts and opinions as a group. If you are an avid reader, and would like to read more than one book a month, you will have access to previous Books of the Month and can meet individually with senior members for meaningful conversations. 

GW Reads! is also passionate about giving back to the DC community. Whether by adding to the Gelman Library catalog of books or donating to local, underprivileged DC schools, we want reading to be a fun, accessible endeavor for everyone.

If you love to read or are looking to get back into reading, this is the perfect club for you!
summary: GW Reads! is unique and inclusive organization that encourages college students to rediscover their love of reading. We read books of all genres and meet monthly to discuss them. This club is committed to bringing the GW community together through books!

name: Google Developer Student Club
description:
The Google Developer Student Club (GDSC) at George Washington University is open to all undergraduate and graduate students interested in learning about what it means to be a developer. By joining our chapter, club members learn to form the bridge between the theory and concepts taught in classes and practice with industry developer tools.

Our Google Developer Student community applies our skills to solving problems in our community through the Google Solutions Challenge that occurs in the second semester. Come to our events in the first semester to learn the skills to participate in exciting and impactful projects towards the end of the year!
summary: Google Developer Student Clubs (GDSC) are community groups for college and university students interested in Google developer technologies. Students from all undergraduate or graduate programs with an interest in growing as a developer are welcome.

name: ITE The George Washington University Student Chapter
description:
ITE The George Washington University Student Chapter (ITE GW) is part of the university chapters of the Institute of Transportation Engineers (ITE).

ITE — A Community of Transportation Professionals works to improve mobility and safety for all transportation system users, helping to build smart and livable communities. Founded in 1930, ITE is a volunteer-driven association of more than 18,000 engineers, planners, consultants, educators, researchers, technologists, and students working in more than 78 countries.
ITE GW is a volunteer-run student community who are interested in exploring or aspiring to be future transportation professionals. We are affiliated with the ITE CAPSITE Section and ITE Mid-Colonial District. We provide opportunities for learning and networking, including connection to the local, regional, national, and international community of transportation professionals.
summary: The goal of the "Institute of Transportation Engineers Student Chapter at GW" (ITE GW) is to introduce students to the transportation profession and supplement their classroom and laboratory experience through various programs and activities.

name: Women's Leadership Program
description:
The Elizabeth J. Somers Women's Leadership Program (WLP) is a selective year-long living and learning program for first year students at the George Washington University. Offered exclusively at the Mount Vernon Campus, WLP commemorates and preserves the vision of the founder of Mount Vernon College and Seminary, Elizabeth J. Somers. WLP students have the benefit of small classes, close contact with faculty and women in leadership roles, and strong community ties within the program.

The dynamic curriculum emphasizes exploration and development of advancing women in leadership through academic courses and a weekly interactive event, the WLP symposium. The WLP symposia offer special lectures, workshops and experiences that draw on the unique resources of Washington, D.C., and brings students together with leaders from many professional fields.

WLP is open to all students, regardless of gender, who share in the mission to support the advancement of women. Students live together in Somers Hall as a living and learning community.
summary: The Elizabeth J. Somers Women's Leadership Program (WLP) is a selective year-long living and learning program for first year students interested in developing leadership in the context of a discipline.

name: Stock and Portfolio Management Club
description:
Who are we?

The GW SPMC is a completely student-run organization created to help financial literacy, create a positive environment to learn and talk about finance and the markets, and provide real-life experience in trading in our Analyst Fund. 

 

Our Analyst Fund

To provide real-world experience in trading and investment research, we manage a competitive Analyst Fund of ~$1.3K (as of Dec. 2025). Each week, analysts pitch new and exciting ideas and actively participate in the voting process during our Tuesday Fund meetings. When a pitch gains support, we move forward with the trade—giving members hands-on exposure to the fast-paced decision-making behind active portfolio management. 

 

  -

Our General Body Meeting Calendar

Our Bloomberg Bootcamp Calendar

Our Events Calendar

  

-

We hold our General Body Meetings every other Wednesday in Duques Hall Room #155. On the opposite Wednesdays we host a Bloomberg Bootcamp in the same location. Both of these regularly occuring events are completely open to the public, and anyone can show up at these.

 

-

Please visit our website for more information: gwspmc.com.
summary:
Who are we?
The GW SPMC is a completely student-run organization created to help financial literacy, create a positive environment to learn and talk about finance and the markets, and provide real-life trading experience our Analyst Fund.

name: Remnant Ministry
description: Remnant Ministry is a student-led club at GWU that provides a platform for students to gain spiritual and mental strength to overcome hardships through worship and fellowship. The club offers a variety of activities, including meditation, prayer, worship and healing time, to help students connect and find a sense of belonging in the GW Christian community. Remnant Ministry also aims to support students in preparing for their future by providing counseling and spiritual guidance. The club welcomes students from various backgrounds and majors!
summary: Remnant Ministry is to provide a platform for spiritual and mental growth through activities such as meditation, prayer, worship, and healing time. The club aims to prepare students to become global leaders and spritual healers in the future.

name: Biology Graduate Student Council
description:
The Biology Graduate Student Council (BGSC) at The George Washington University is
a professional and social organization affiliated with the GWU Department of Biological
Sciences. BGSC works to promote community and betterment of working and learning
conditions for graduate students of all department-affiliated graduate academic programs through
social gatherings, attending faculty meetings, advocating for student needs, and other
interdisciplinary events. BGSC provides an inclusive environment for students to share concerns,
connect over shared interests, and create social events, all while giving graduate students
in the Biological Sciences Department a voice in their education.
summary:
BGSC provides an inclusive environment for students to share concerns,
connect over shared interests, and participate in social events, all while giving graduate students
in the Biological Sciences Department a voice in their education.

name: GW Startup Avenue
description:
If you could start a business today, what would it be?
And if you are running a business, how much further could you take it with the right people around you?

GW Startup Avenue brings together students at every stage, from those exploring entrepreneurship for the first time to those actively running ventures. It is a place to build alongside others who are serious about growth.

Through our general body, students plug into a network of motivated peers and gain real exposure to turning ideas into action. For students who already have their own businesses, our Founders Program offers a more focused environment built around structure, accountability, and surrounding yourself with other committed founders.

Startup Avenue is about building in the right environment, with people who are actually doing it.
summary: GW Startup Avenue is a community for aspiring entrepreneurs at GW, bringing together both future and existing founders. We provide a strong network, structure, and practical resources to help ventures develop and grow.

name: PGP at GW
description: The Petey Greene Program supports the academic goals of justice-affected individuals through high-quality volunteer tutoring.  We pledge to support the mission and vision of The Petey Greene Program by recruiting and interviewing potential volunteer tutors, coordinating campus-based advocacy events, providing support to volunteer tutors, and promoting community building among volunteers. PGP at GW also provides justice-oriented leadership development for college students interested in educational and social justice for system-affected people.
summary: In PGP at GW, we support the mission and vision of The Petey Greene Program by supporting students interested in educational and social justice for system-affected people. We focus on campus-based advocacy and supporting our tutors.

name: Immigrant Liberation Coalition at GW
description: The ILC at GW’s purpose is to empower the community, inclusive of any immigration status, through political education, community events, and creating a supportive environment. We envision a world where we abolish borders to foster global solidarity.
summary: We, The Immigrant Liberation Coalition at GW, seek to form a safe space, fight for justice, and secure the dignity for all immigrants at GW.

name: Security Policy Studies Program Student Board
description: The Security Policy Studies Student Board is a student-led organization that represents students enrolled in the Security Policy Studies Master’s program at GWU’s Elliott School of International Affairs. It is dedicated to promoting inclusion, access, support, community involvement, and student well-being through events and highlighting student voices. We are committed to promoting academic and professional excellence in the student body by organizing academic speaker events and professional engagement opportunities, such as networking opportunities and site visits to potential employers and relevant institutions. We are also committed to spreading community, organizing several GW-sponsored receptions for the program as well as informal social events.
summary: The SPS Board is a student-led organization that represents students in the SPS Master’s program at ESIA. It is dedicated to promoting inclusion, access, support, community, and student well-being through social, academic, and professional events.

name: GW Network of Enlightened Women
description:
GW NeW is dedicated to fostering the education and leadership of culturally conservative and independent-thinking women. GW NeW members will grow more confident in advocating for themselves and others not just at school, but in their workplaces, homes, and communities. The organization will reinforce principles of freedom, opportunity, and civil discourse by hosting speakers, holding debates, and providing intellectual resources. Members will engage in activities and discussion surrounding conservative values, policy, and culture at weekly meetings. These activities will include a book club, museum trips, movie nights, and coffee trips in addition to speaker events.
The organization is affiliated with national university organization Network of Enlightened Women. The Network of Enlightened Women is devoted to promoting conservative principles and intellectual diversity on college campuses and in public discourse surrounding women, culture, and politics. This organization provides unique opportunities for professional development, with a rich speaker bank and scholarships for trips and conferences.
summary: NeW is dedicated to fostering the education and leadership of culturally conservative and independent-thinking women.  It provides intellectual diversity on college campuses and in public discourse surrounding women, policy, and culture.

name: Paws for a Cause GW
description:
We are an organization of animal rescue advocates, looking to support local, DMV area animal rescue efforts through fundraising, linen collection drives, and in-person volunteer work at local rescue events. Besides supporting local animal rescue efforts, we hope to raise awareness on GW's campus of adopting a pet as an option. 

Join the GroupMe! https://groupme.com/join_group/96011123/gCwlU2tv
summary: We are an organization of animal rescue advocates, looking to support local, DMV area animal rescue efforts through fundraising, linen collection drives, and in-person volunteer work at local rescue events.

name: GW Aviators
description: 1. If you are a pilot or want to fly, GW Aviators is the club for you! GW Aviators is an interest society for both pilots and aviation enthusiasts to further their interests and expand their knowledge. We seek to promote aviation safety and education and provide resources for members to further their interest in aviation and pursue pilot training. We also act as a forum for members to discuss, collaborate, and develop ideas for the future of the aviation industry. We host leading experts and speakers in unique speaker events along with field trips to aviation sites in the DMV. GW Aviators seeks to establish an active aeronautics and aviation community at GW.
summary: GW Aviators is an all-things aviation interest society. We host events, tour aeronautics facilities, and our members who are pilots can fly planes in the DMV. If you are a pilot, want to become a pilot, or just enjoy aviation, this club is for you.

name: American Civil Liberties Union
description:
The ACLU is a non-profit, non-partisan organization founded in 1920 to protect and advance civil liberties. As a student chapter of the ACLU, ACLU-D.C. at GW strives to uphold this mission and engage the GW community in civil rights issues relevant to DC and the GW campus through "Know Your Rights" workshops, civic education, on-campus events and programming, and so much more! We work closely with the ACLU-D.C. affiliate office to offer off-campus opportunities like; workshops, campaigns, and volunteer opportunities.

To learn more about some of the issues we focus on alongside the ACLU-D.C. affiliate office, click here!
summary: ACLU-D.C. at GW is a student chapter of the American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU), an organization founded to protect civil rights. We aim to amplify the mission of the ACLU through community engagement, civic education, and campus outreach.

name: George Washington Football Club
description:
GWFC exists to provide students with an all-inclusive, passionate community of soccer fans. We will provide students with an outlet to meet fellow fans; arrange watch parties, tournaments, and other events; to appreciate the beautiful game.

Follow our Instagram account, @gwfootballclub, for updates and event announcements!
summary: GWFC exists to provide students with an all-inclusive, passionate community of soccer fans. We will provide students with an outlet to meet fellow fans; arrange watch parties, tournaments, and other events; to appreciate the beautiful game.

name: The D&D Syndicate
description:
Heroes and villains! Nerds, and necromancers! Welcome to the Syndicate!

 

Here in our humble little corner of The George Washington University, we facilitate the magic and wonder of the World's Greatest Roleplaying Game: Dungeons and Dragons. Whether you're embarking on your very first quest, or returning once again to the land of fantasy, the Syndicate is here to make sure your journey runs without a hitch. 

 

- Interested Players may look to the events page for current or upcoming campaigns. Long-term campaigns will usually be available for sign up towards the beginning of the year, but one shots and other short games should be available throughout the year. Reach out to the organization email or contact Syndicate leadership to find a campaign that inspires you! 

 

- Prospecting Dungeon Masters, tis' our solemn duty to provide you with reserved rooms, consistent schedules, and party recruitment tools. If you already have a party and campaign ready (or already in motion), feel free to let us be your official umbrella. If you're a newbie DM and are looking for support, our little band of seasoned storytellers can help you learn the ropes and craft your first campaign! Reach out via the organization email, or contact the Syndicate president (Jason Weaver) directly to prepare your adventure!

 

(Disclaimer: Balancing the amount of players and DMs is never a perfect process. While we will try our very best to accommodate every Syndicate member, we cannot guarantee that every player will be given a game, nor every DM a full party). 

 

To stay up to date on events and participate in Syndicate operations, all members are encouraged to join our GroupMe!

https://groupme.com/join_group/98055174/8g9Lk5d1

 

Sharpen your swords, prepare your spellbooks, and roll initiative!
summary: The D&D Syndicate is a center for adventure and imagination, designed to help players connect, organize, and play Dungeons and Dragons by Wizards of the Coast.

name: George Washington Pickleball Club
description: Join us for GW Pickleball Open Play, where players of all levels can enjoy the game in a fun and friendly environment. Whether you’re a beginner or experienced, our sessions offer a great way to improve your skills, meet new people, and have a good time.
summary: Join us for GW Pickleball Open Play, where players of all levels can enjoy the game in a fun and friendly environment. Whether you’re a beginner or experienced, our sessions offer a great way to improve your skills, meet new people, and have a good time.

name: Asian American Journalists Association - GW
description:
We, the members of the GW Asian American Journalists Association, seek to increase Asian American and Pacific Islander perspectives and representation in media, newsrooms and stories by empowering aspiring journalists on GW’s campus. We will do so by subscribing to the regulations and policies of the George Washington University and establishing this Constitution to govern the matters within our organization. AAJA-GW aims to:

  1. Provide encouragement, information and advice to AAPI students who aspire to professional journalism careers
  2. Provide a means of association and support among Asian American and Pacific Islander (AAPI) journalists, and to advance AAPI journalists as news managers and media executives
  3. Provide to the AAPI community an awareness of news media and an understanding of how to gain fair access
  4. Research and point out when news media organizations stray from accuracy and fairness in the coverage of Asian Americans and Pacific Islanders and AAPI issues.
  5. Be an exemplary group of students that honors journalism excellence and outstanding achievement 
  6. Increase the number of Asian Americans and Pacific Islanders students as well as faculty and leaders apart of GW’s academic journalism program within its School of Media and Public Affairs 
  7. Regularly report to AAJA’s DC Chapter

AAJA-GW’s plans to hold events throughout the academic year open to members and nonmembers to facilitate community building with the AAJA DC chapter and national organization. Event types are listed below. These events will occur once a month.

  1. Mentorship workshops 
  2. Intern panels 
  3. Speaker panels 
  4. Networking events
  5. Special event series for Asian American and Pacific Islander Month, which is in May
summary: We, the members of the GW Asian American Journalists Association, seek to increase Asian American and Pacific Islander perspectives and representation in media, newsrooms and stories by empowering aspiring journalists on GW’s campus.

name: Yarn Club
description:
WELCOMMMMEE to Yarn Club~ GWU’s most cozy and slightly chaotic corner where we relax and connect over knitting and crochet.
 
We’re beginner-friendly, with at least one e-board member always available to help you learn the basics of knitting or crochet.
 
Whether you are trying these crafts for the first time or already know what your getting tangled up in, you are welcome here.
 
Come hang out with us as we create, learn about sustainability, and fundraise for a special cause each semester with the crafts we make along the way.🧶✨
summary: This club's goal is to create a safe space for students to come together and either crochet or knit with one another, while learning about the importance of fashion sustainability.

name: GWU Wrestling
description:
The purpose of the club is to teach members competitive wrestling and give them an outlet to compete. Member's will learn about the sport's rules, practices, and techniques through organized practices and competitions. Text (314) 443-7769 for info.

 

If your interested here's the groupme: https://groupme.com/join_group/96222535/YiTKvmhy
summary: The purpose of the club is to teach members competitive wrestling and give them an outlet to compete. Member's will learn about the sport's rules, practices, and techniques through organized practices and competitions.

name: GW Association of Hispanic Journalists
description: The GW Association for Hispanic Journalists is an organization open to all students of any background or ethnicity that aims to connect all students interested in journalism, political communication, and communication with an evolving community of Hispanic newsmakers. GWAHJ looks to promote credible journalism that comprehensively portrays the voices and experiences of Hispanic and Latino/a/x/e people and expand the mission of the National Association of Hispanic Journalists.
summary: The GWAHJ is an organization open to all students of any background or ethnicity that aims to connect all students interested in journalism, political communication, and communication with an evolving community of Hispanic newsmakers.

name: GWU Army ROTC
summary: Army ROTC is one of the most demanding and successful leadership programs in the country. The training a student receives in Army ROTC provides leadership development, military skills and career training.

name: Korean American Student Society
description: Korean American Student Society is a student-led organization dedicated to creating a community at GW for Korean American students, as well as students interested in Korean and Korean American culture. KASS aims to ensure that Korean American culture and identity does not continue to go underrepresented in the Asian/Asian American community and at GW. The values of KASS include: community, integrity, empathy, open-mindedness, and respect. Through these core values, KASS seeks to provide members with a comfortable, safe space at GW to share common interests and experiences, meet new people, and form meaningful connections.
summary: KASS is an organization dedicated to creating a community at GW for Korean American students, as well as students interested in Korean/Korean American culture. KASS aims to ensure that the Korean American culture/identity does not go underrepresented.

name: IranZamin Cultural Society
description: The Cultural Society of Iranzamin (CSI) is a cultural and social organization that aims to educate and promote Iranian culture at George Washington University and create a community for the Iranian population at GWU. The organization seeks to bring together heritage (non-heritage Iranians and heritage Iranians) and non-heritage (those who do not identify as Iranian) students interested in learning about Iranian history, culture, and languages. The organization provides students on campus with a safe, inclusive environment where they can freely express themselves and immerse themselves in Iranian culture.
summary: The Cultural Society of Iranzamin is a cultural & social student organization promoting Iranian culture, history & languages at GW. Open to all GW students. We are not political/religious.

name: Caribbean Student Association
summary: The Caribbean Student Association is a vibrant & inclusive group that celebrates the rich cultural diversity of the Caribbean islands. Through various events, workshops, and gatherings, we aim to promote awareness and understanding of Caribbean tradition

name: The George Washington Museum & The Textile Museum
description: Located on GW's campus in downtown D.C., the museum celebrates art, history and world cultures. Public programs and exhibitions are inspired by diverse collections of global textiles and local history. Resources for students and scholars include the peer-reviewed The Textile Museum Journal, the Cotsen Textile Traces Study Center and the Albert H. Small Center for National Capital Area Studies. www.museum.gwu.edu
summary: On Campus Textile Museum

name: GWPalooza
summary: GWPalooza

name: Alpha Epsilon Pi
description: Since its founding at New York University in 1913, Alpha Epsilon Pi has been committed to its mission of developing future leaders within the global Jewish community. This mission is demonstrated every day through acts of brotherhood, Tzedakah (charity), social awareness and support for Jewish communities and Israel.
summary: Alpha Epsilon Pi is the world's Jewish social fraternity. Each day the fraternity strives to develop the next generation of Jewish leaders through acts of brotherhood, Tzedakah (charity), social awareness and support for Jewish communities and Israel.

name: Jacob Burns Community Legal Clinics
summary: TBA

name: Kappa Omega Alpha
description: The Kappa Omega Alpha (KOA) Pre-Professional Public Policy Society fosters undergraduate engagement in public policy-related fields. KOA upholds the values of professionalism, service, community, and empowerment.
summary: The Kappa Omega Alpha (KOA) Pre-Professional Public Policy Society fosters undergraduate engagement in public policy-related fields. KOA upholds the values of professionalism, service, community, and empowerment.

name: GW Bikes
description:
D.C. is a beautiful city with a plethora of things to do. We want to experience it with you on the back of a bike. Whether you’re a veteran cyclist or a complete beginner we hope to take you around the city on our favorite routes, seeing new sites and meeting new people all along the way.

No need to own a bike. Get the annual membership for Capital Bikeshare and you have unlimited access to bikes. Use code cabi4gw for the student discount.

Anyone is welcome to join our events and can receive information by joining our Engage or by following our Instagram (@gwbiking).

Please feel free to direct any questions to our club email (gwubiking@gmail.com) or give suggestions for event routes! More active communication is on our GroupMe https://linktr.ee/GWbiking

GivePulse is a volunteer platform that lets you track your service hours and allows your hours to be recognized by GW. For this club, events such as data collections and advocacy GBMS would be eligible for service hours. https://gwserves.givepulse.com/group/1357636
summary: We are an organization dedicated to promoting biking as a fun activity, and way of transportation. Through our events, we strive to encourage GW students to live healthier on the back of a bike and support urban planning initiatives.

name: The George Washington University Astronomy Club
description: Astronomy is the study of celestial objects such as stars, galaxies, and the universe beyond. It's a science field that many people are unfamiliar with. However, the Astronomy club will expand GW students' aspect until Astronomy is no longer unfamiliar field in GW. The club will be organized by four officers, president, vice president, secretary, and treasurer. We will discuss current astronomical news and phenomenon through presentation and science fiction movies. Not only discussing stars, but we will also observe stars on our Mount Vernon campus. Our goal is to provide easier access of astronomy to GW students and inspire their interests.
summary: An organization that gathered with a group of individuals who share a passion for astronomy. The club will scientifically study and discuss celestial objects such as stars, galaxies, and the universe beyond.

name: George Washington Skateboarding Club
description:
GWSBC aims to create an environment that encourages progression, builds confidence, and fosters friendships among members. Meetings will be split between recreational and non-recreational. Recreational meetings will range from informal meetups to go skate and learn, to larger excursions off campus. Non-recreational meetings will be held to host skate video watch parties, discuss skateboard culture, and further build bonds off the board. Additionally, we will be hosting on and off campus events such as skate contests, video premieres and other festivities.

In order to welcome all interested, GWSBC will provide or assist in acquiring all equipment needed for any members.
summary: The GW skateboarding club provides an inclusive and fun space for skateboarders and those interested in skateboarding at GWU.

name: The Engineers' Philanthropic Society
description: The purpose of The Engineers' Philanthropic Society is to transform the futures of students in underserved public schools, in the DMV area, through supplemental support to develop their STEM skills and expose them to the world of professional engineering. We focus on philanthropic efforts aimed at STEM equity and exposure for K-12 students in the DMV area. The Engineers’ Philanthropic Society shall help to cultivate a partnership between The George Washington University, the School of Engineering and Applied Sciences, and underserved public schools in Washington DC. Additionally, we offer career-enhancing opportunities for volunteers through strategic partnerships with well-established companies that are key players in the philanthropic sector.
summary: Building philanthropic bridge between GW Engineering and underserved DC public schools through original STEM-based volunteering initiatives. Career-enhancing opportunities for volunteers through partnerships with established nonprofits organizations.

name: GW Comedy Nights
description: The purpose of the organization is to provide students with opportunities to learn and practice the art of stand-up comedy via showcases, open mics, and all other related events. All students are welcome to be a member of Comedy Nights no matter their skill level. Members of GW Comedy Nights will also work to ensure our events are high-quality entertainment with lineups that are representative of the student body. Our organization aims to not only provide student opportunities but also prepare students to pursue stand-up beyond their undergraduate careers through the hosting of workshops and preparation meetings prior to each event. In return, bringing events of laughter and levity to campus. In striving to reach this purpose, GW Comedy Nights will operate within established University policies.
summary: GW Comedy Nights is an equal opportunity, student-run organization created to offer an outlet for GW students interested in live performance comedy. GW's only stand-up comedy group on campus.

name: Kappa Alpha Pi - Alpha Delta
description: Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law and Government Fraternity at the George Washington University is a community of individuals interested in pursuing careers within the legal and government fields. We are committed to providing our brothers with the tools necessary to succeed academically, professionally, and socially and in doing so create leaders for the future.
summary: Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law and Government Co-ed Professional Fraternity exists nationally to foster a gender-inclusive brotherhood for undergraduate students interested in law and government.

name: Scholars of Finance
description:
Scholars of Finance is a rapidly growing national organization on a mission to inspire character and integrity in the finance leaders of tomorrow. We seek to solve the world’s largest problems by investing in undergraduate students through leadership development, mentorship, and the active support of hundreds of purpose-driven finance executives and investors.

Our members then join top firms, funds, and a life-long support community that empowers them to spend their careers serving the greater good using the biggest lever in the world: finance.

After the Scholars of Finance national team reached out to us to expand their presence in the top universities in the US, we were delighted to be approved by them to start a GWU chapter of Scholars of Finance.

Our Vision: A future where all finance leaders steward the world's capital to serve the greater good.

Our Mission: To inspire character and integrity in the finance leaders of tomorrow.

Since our primary focus is to espouse ethics, integrity, and stewardship among the finance leaders of tomorrow, we orient conversations around how to uphold these values at home, at work, and while making investment decisions. While these discussions are held within the context of our organization, we believe the truths we uncover apply universally to all aspects of a leader’s life. Below are the team values that make us effective as an organization.

At Scholars of Finance, our values are fundamental to us. When the founding team developed them, it was through a process of weeks of introspection, discussion, and dozens of hours of debate. We arrived at a set of values and behaviors that we can all strive to uphold. We have ongoing discussions about our values and even offer each other 360° feedback with our values as a touchstone.

Our Core Values are Integrity, Compassion, Humility, Curiosity, Impact, Courage.

Below are the programs we host at GWU

Speaker Series:
Our Speaker Series enables students to hear and learn from some of the finance industry's most ethical, values-driven leaders.

Mentorship:
Our Mentorship Program creates 1:1 relationships between students and astute finance professionals in our network.

Leadership:
Our Leadership Development Program is a seven-week course built for self-discovery that helps our students learn and live by their values.

Symposia:
Our Symposia brings together students and professionals for a mix of panel discussions and keynote speeches focused on our values.

Technical Training:
Our Technical Training, provided in partnership with Training The Street, teaches in-demand, workplace-ready skills for careers in finance.

Values Forum:
Students engage in introspection to articulate their personal values with their peers while considering Scholars of Finance's six core values and the role of values in finance.
summary: Scholars of Finance is a rapidly growing organization on a mission to inspire character and integrity in the finance leaders of tomorrow. Our vision and mission are oriented towards all future finance executives being principled leaders.

name: Friends of MSF at GWU
description: Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF), otherwise known as Doctor Without Borders, is an international medical humanitarian organization that provides care for people across the globe affected by issues such as conflict, famine, disease outbreaks, natural and human-made disasters. Friends of MSF (FoMSF) is a student-led chapter devoted to supporting the efforts of Médecins Sans Frontières. FoMSF aims to raise awareness for MSF campaigns by educating students on global health issues and the responses to such issues. This is achieved through activities such as speaker events, film screenings, organized discussions, and advocacy campaigns. Additionally, FoMSF aims to support MSF through the establishment of fundraisers in support of MSF's work. Participation in FoMSF provides an opportunity to actively contribute to the pursuits of MSF and gain valuable insight into the field of humanitarian aid. Membership is free and open to all GW students interested in joining.
summary: Friends of MSF is a student-led chapter dedicated to serving the needs of and raising awareness for the work of Médecins Sans Frontieres (MSF), otherwise known as Doctors Without Borders.

name: James K Tolton Student Society
description: To serve as the official GWU Student chapter of the American Academy of Physician Assistant; encourage academic and clinical excellence; explore current and developing medical issues; promote the PA profession through the education of students, professionals, and the public; promote community service and enhance communication between the PA program faculty, students, and the greater DMV community
summary: To serve as the official GWU Student chapter of the AAPA; encourage academic and clinical excellence; explore current and developing medical issues; promote the PA profession through the education of students, profession.

name: revolutionariesTech
description:
Join our discord!

In the world of computer science, projects and experience speak louder than mere words on a resume. Our club equips computer science majors with the experience gained from engaging in real-world projects, aiding collaborative learning, academic and professional growth. Projects on your resume stand as proof of your technical skills, setting you apart from the crowd. By working among other CS majors, you'll learn diverse problem-solving approaches, enhancing your ability to work in a team—a skill highly valued in professional settings.  Whether you're a seasoned coder or just starting, our projects and support cater to various skill levels, providing a supportive environment for continuous learning.
summary: Our organization is dedicated to providing CS majors with hands-on experience in real-world projects, which will serve on their resume as tangible proof of their abilities.

name: Japanese Student Union
description: The Japanese Student Union (JSU) is a student organization that brings together GWU students with Japanese heritage, background, or any connection with Japan, to share and celebrate Japanese culture. Throughout the year, we hold social events including mixers and cultural nights, and we team up with other student organizations for community gatherings that create meaningful connections across campus. Through a mix of cultural, social, and professional events, JSU creates a welcoming space to build community, make connections, and learn from one another.
summary: JSU, or the Japanese Student Union, is a student organization that allows GWU students with Japanese heritage or background to promote Japanese culture throughout the GWU community and the DMV area via cultural, social, and professional events

name: SoulfulBrewings Club
description:
Soulful Brewings is more than just a tea club; it's a vibrant, cross-cultural community nestled within the walls of GW University. Our mission is: to celebrate diversity, foster connection, and promote self-discovery through a blend of artistic expression, creative workshops, and meaningful dialogue.

At Soulful Brewings, we believe in the power of shared experiences to transcend barriers and build bridges between people of all backgrounds. Whether you're a seasoned artist or simply curious about exploring different cultures, there's a place for you here. Our welcoming environment encourages participants to engage in open, honest conversations, share their stories, and learn from one another.

Through our diverse range of activities, from art sessions to cultural showcases, we aim to create healing spaces where students can unwind, recharge, and find solace in self-expression. We understand the importance of self-care, and we actively promote it as a form of liberation and empowerment.

Join us for a cup of tea, engaging discussions, and an ever-changing array of activities designed to spark creativity and cultivate community. SoulfulBrewing isn't just a club—it's an experience waiting to be shared and shaped by each and every member. Come, be a part of something special, and let's brew some soul together.
summary: Club SoulfulBrewing is a group dedicated to sharing culture, and discovering cultures in a fun and artistic way. We create a safe space for radical art and self-care as an act of freedom and we drink tea each meeting!

name: American Studies Graduate Student Association
description: The purpose of this organization is to aid graduate student members of the George Washington University American Studies department in their academic development while expanding intra-departmental cooperation and collegiality. To attend to this mission, the GSA will provide a venue for building and organizing graduate student conferences, professionalization workshops, and student social events. The group was founded with education, social, and cultural purposes in mind, aiming to be a venue where graduate students can connect, collaborate, and bolster their scholarly/professional pursuits.
summary: The American Studies Graduate Student Association exists to offer a venue for graduate students in the American Studies department at GWU to further their professional development, offer mutual academic support, and foster an intellectual community.

name: Hearts for the Homeless
description:
Hearts for the Homeless (H4H) is an international non-profit organization that offers free blood pressure screenings to individuals. The organization also collects supplies like socks and blankets for the homeless community. Studies support the fact that the homeless population is the most susceptible to cardiovascular disease due to stress, inadequate access to healthcare, and inability to receive public health information. H4H aims to close these gaps in healthcare education for vulnerable populations through volunteer-based community outreach.

To register with us officially, and receive our emails you will be required to fill out our Registration Form

Here's our donation link: https://my.gwu.edu/mod/cse/index.cfm 

- Please write in "Hearts for the Homeless" in the search bar and in the comments if you would like to help us grow or organization via donation
summary:
H4H aims to contribute to the health and wellbeing of the most vulnerable populations worldwide by:
- providing education on health risk factors
- monitoring blood pressure and other health indicators 
- donating health care and prevention resources

name: GW Athletes in Pre-Health
description:
George Washington Athletes in Pre-Health is an organization aimed to provide support, resources, and guidance throughout their time as athletes and pre-health students. The pre-health track is time consuming, has a rigorous workload, and most importantly is known to take a toll on students mental health. Being an athlete as well, is time consuming and can be physically and emotionally draining. When you combine both of these scenarios, it’s easy to feel alone, stressed, and struggle from imposter syndrome.
RPH’s (Revs in Pre-Health) will serve to guide incoming freshmen and anyone else along their athletic and pre-healthcare journey. RPH will provide opportunities to build up pre-healthcare resumes as well as inspire members to make the most out of their athletic collegiate career. Specifically, RPH will have volunteering opportunities, host panels of physicians that were also D1 athletes, host charity events, partner with the athletic department when needed, create a community with biweekly meetings, etc.
RPH will help members use their athletic ability and journey to their advantage. We will strive to change the narrative that athletes fail to have enough time and/or resources to be a competitive medical candidate. RPH wants to emphasize the importance of knowing that there is no “one size fits all” on a pre-health journey. Everyone's path may look different and RPH wants to create a network where change is embraced.
summary: George Washington Athletes in Pre-Health is an organization dedicated to supporting student-athletes pursuing careers in healthcare by providing resources, guidance, and a strong community throughout their academic and athletic journey.

name: Responsible Fashion Collective
description: The Responsible Fashion Collective is a student organization focused on advocating for responsible fashion consumption & ethical treatment of garment workers, spreading awareness about textile waste & the dangers of 'fast fashion', and promoting innovative, environmentally conscious practices & solutions. As a community, we discuss the problems with our current fast fashion system and learn how to implement slow fashion in our everyday lives.
summary: Advocating for responsible fashion practices on campus.

name: Muslim Voice
description:
"Invite ˹all˺ to the Way of your Lord with wisdom and kind advice, and only debate with them in the best manner." (Quran, Surah An-Nahl 16:125)

(MuslimVoice) shall be a non-profit organization and the fundamental purpose of the Association shall be to encourage and enable Muslims with basic knowledge and competence in Islam to contribute individually and collectively towards enhancing understanding of Islam and appropriately representing its values and teachings. (Muslim Voice) should strive to pursue meaningful interfaith dialogue and faith-based civic engagement with our broader community.
summary: A student organisation that aims to present a muslim voice on campus, promoting understanding, combating stereotypes, and fostering interfaith dialogue.

name: GW Irish Dance Club
summary: The George Washington Irish Dance Club. The GW Irish Dance club will foster a community of dancers that focuses on the celebration of Irish culture and dance. The GW Irish Dance club will promote a welcoming community to current and new Irish dancers.

name: GW Muslim Women in Medicine
description: MWM is dedicated to building a nurturing community for Muslim women with aspirations in the healthcare field. We strive to cultivate an inclusive space that encourages members to exchange experiences, benefit from mentorship, and access resources specifically designed to address the distinctive challenges encountered by Muslim women pursuing careers in medicine.
summary: MWM is dedicated to supporting Muslim women in healthcare, providing an inclusive space for shared experiences, mentorship, and resources tailored to the unique challenges of pursuing medical careers.

name: George Washington University Student Chapter of the American Statistical Association
description: The purpose of our American Statistical Association student chapter is to provide students at the George Washington University with opportunities to connect with other students interested in statistics and interact with prominent statisticians locally and at national meetings. We also want to encourage students to continue studying statistics and provide academic/career information in the statistical sciences. There are no scholastic requirements, and students need not be statistics majors. All students are welcomed and encouraged to get involved. Please check our website often for events, news, and information.
summary: The American Statistical Association is the primary national organization for statisticians and maintains a journal, membership rolls, and hosts conferences. This club is to be the GWU student chapter of the ASA.

name: Operation United States-Africa
description: The purpose of OPUS-AFRICA is to foster discussion regarding US-African affairs through guest speakers, roundtable discussions, OPUS-AFRICA’s blog, and media engagement. Topics of discussion include, but are not limited to US national security interests, economic development, diaspora affairs, illicit finance, foreign policy, terrorism, great power competition, and African history. OPUS-AFRICA is a non-partisan student organization looking to bring unity to a diverse group of students from different walks of life around a shared interest.
summary: The purpose of OPUS-AFRICA is to foster discussion regarding US-African affairs through guest speakers, panel discussion, OPUS-AFRICA's blog, and media engagement for international affairs professionals and young Africanists.

name: GW Students for Human Rights
description:
GW Students for Human Rights Initiative

The GW Students for Human Rights Initiative is a student-led organization committed to advancing justice for asylum seekers in the D.C. area. Our members are trained to scribe for forensic medical evaluations (FMEs) and assist with affidavit drafting, providing essential documentation that can strengthen asylum cases.
summary: The Students for Human Rights Club is committed to advocating for human rights worldwide. The group’s mission is to expand access to medical-legal services, health care, and supportive services for asylum seekers and victims of torture and trafficking.

name: Southeast Asia Student Network
description: Southeast Asia Student Network or SEANET is a student-led organization with a focus on promoting cultural awareness and diversity of Southeast Asia, providing a space for communication on topics of interest to the members, representing GW’s Southeast Asian student community, and providing a platform for academic and professional networking in Washington, D.C. to the members.
summary: SEANET is an organization focusing on promoting cultural awareness and diversity of Southeast Asia, providing a space for communication on topics of interest, representing GW's Southeast Asia community and providing academic and professional networking.

name: George Washington University Korean Graduate Student Association
description: GW KGSA's primary objective is to establish a platform where Korean graduate students can interact and build relationships through social networking. The club will provide members with opportunities to foster academic, professional, and personal connections. In addition, the organization aims to strengthen friendships, offer educational support, and promote the love and appreciation of Korean culture. Lastly, we aim to promote George Washington University to prospective students in South Korea and maintain connections of the Korean alumni of George Washington University.
summary: The primary objective is to establish a platform where Korean graduate students can interact and build relationships through social networking. The club will provide members with opportunities to foster academic, professional, and personal connections.

name: Operation Smile GW
description: Operation Smile is a non-profit organization founded in 1982 by Bill and Kathy Magee. They have provided free surgeries for children with cleft conditions, access to healthcare, and community for children who have cleft lip and palate. Through their student programs branch, we will work directly with Operation Smile and other college OPS organizations. Operation Smile provides leadership opportunities, experiences, and internships to college students, as well.
summary: Operation Smile GW is focused towards spreading awareness and fundraising for children with cleft lip and palate across the world through the Operation Smile non-profit organization.

name: District Dashers Run Club GW
description:
There is no better outlet to a busy student schedule that to go on a run, and enjoy all of the wonderful sights that the city has to offer. Unfortunately, runners often do not feel safe and are therefore deterred from accessing the full potential that the sport, and the city has to offer.

District Dashers Run Club aims to change this through our weekly runs. This is not just any running or cross country club however, as we are not concerned with distance or pacing, but instead creating collaborative awesome routes that include great sights and ending locations. This will not only ensure that everyone is able to feel safe on a run, but that even those who cant imagine enjoying running might discover a new passion in this fun space that is created.
summary: This organisation is a running club promoting mental health and safety for runners that are looking for a social outlet, and to explore the city. It was formed to ensure that students of any skill level can access this safe and welcoming effort.

name: Black Graduate Student Organization
summary: The purpose of the Black Graduate Student Organization at George Washington University is to promote a sense of unity amongst Black professions in the George Washington community during their pursuit of a graduate school education.

name: Black Queer Alliance
summary: Black Queer Alliance is a student-led organization with a central mission focused on fostering a safe space for Black LGBTQ+ students on campus.

name: The George Washington University Chapter of the Society of Physics Students
description: The purpose of this Chapter shall be the advancement and diffusion of knowledge of physics, the encouragement of interest in physics throughout the academic and local communities, and the introduction of students to the professional community. As a member of GW's SPS Chapter, you can attend weekly meetings, participate in professional conferences relating to physics and the various subfields it encompasses, and volunteer in annual outreach activities. All students interested in physics, regardless of major, are welcome to join!
summary: The purpose of this Chapter shall be the advancement and diffusion of knowledge of physics, the encouragement of interest in physics throughout the academic and local communities, and the introduction of students to the professional community.

name: Vocal Chords Journal at GW
description:
E-BOARD APPLICATION FALL 2026

Vocal Chords Journal Website

Open Submission Link
summary: Our club is an open-submission web-journal with student-run workshops. Our journal aims to publish anything from fiction and prose to philosophical debate or video essays with an overarching social commentary seen on the GW campus and in the world.

name: Zimbabwean Student Association
description: The Zimbabwean Student Association is GW’s first Zimbabwean and Southern African-focused organization. The purpose of the organization is to create a community of undergraduate and graduate Zimbabwean students that can connect with each other and share culture with the GW student body and the DMV at large. This is done through events, education, networking, and celebrating culture and traditions.
summary: The Zimbabwean Student Association is GW’s first Zimbabwean & Southern African-focused organization. The purpose of the organization is to create a community of Zimbabwean students that can connect with each other and share culture with the GW community.

name: National Society of Collegiate Scholars
description:
The National Society of Collegiate Scholars (NSCS) is a non-profit honors organization for high achieving first and second year college students. NSCS provides career and graduate school connections, leadership and service experiences, access to discounts and savings, and offers more money in undergrad scholarships than any other honor society of its kind.

 What sets us apart from other honor societies is that we can impact students early on in their academic career. And NSCS was founded on the campus of GW in 1994. We are now at over 300 campuses nationwide and are excited to bring back NSCS to where it all started!
summary: The National Society of Collegiate Scholars (NSCS) is a non-profit honors organization for high achieving first and second year college students.

name: Poetic Justices
description: An organization founded in 2024 working to support social justice issues through poetry, art, and collaboration with poets and artists. Through zine production, workshops, and events we work to promote poetry through an activism lens to the GW community.
summary: An organization founded in 2024 working to support social justice issues through poetry, art, and collaboration with poets and artists. Through zine production and events we work to promote poetry through an activism lens to the GW community.

name: The George Washington University Death Penalty Action
description:
Through fostering an environment for open dialogue on the realities within capital punishment, students will be able to learn the truths behind the death penalty in a. supportive environment. The GWDPA believes that action is sparked by education, and through our direct affiliation with the national organization, Death Penalty Action, members will have access to higher learning platforms. Said affiliation will provide members with resources such as speakers with first-hand perspectives, powerful execution petitions, educational resources, and exterior events such as execution vigils and protests by the Capitol Building. 

The GWDPA acknowledges that capital punishment has come to be known as controversial and heavily politicized. The organization does not and never will expect anyone to have one concrete opinion on the practice, and will serve to encourage open-minded conversations with varying ideologies. For, the organization believes that the best way to evolve one's own perspective is through listening to others.

The GWDPA will participate in fundraising for off-campus event transportation and speaker transportation, but will allocate all other funds raised to the national Death Penalty Action. DPA uses their monetary funds primarily to help those navigating the death penalty, their families, pressuring state courts to restrict and repeal the punishment, and for bringing general visibility to executions in the US.
summary: The GWDPA will work to create a supportive and stimulating environment for open dialogue on the realities within capital punishment, and to mobilize students towards its abolishment through education and experience. Affiliated with Death Penalty Action.

name: The Language Center
description: The GW Language Center provides a wide array of academic support for faculty and students in Columbian College’s various language programs. From finding out what level language class to enroll in to practicing language skills for the classroom and the workplace, we have tools to help language learners succeed.
summary: The GW Language Center provides an array of academic support for faculty and students in Columbian College’s various language programs.

name: GW Law Dean of Students Office
summary: Welcome from the GW Law Dean of Students Office! We support students seeking academic or personal advising and collaborate on student governance, programming, wellness, and community inclusion.

name: Arts, Culture, Entertainment, and Sports Law Association
summary: The Arts, Culture, Entertainment, and Sports Law Association brings together students interested in the legal and business aspects of the creative arts, film and TV, music, fashion, and sports industries.

name: Black Law Students Association
description:
The Black Law Students Association (BLSA) at The George Washington University Law School is a service organization aimed at promoting academic excellence, professional development, community service, and social unity among our members.

 

GW BLSA is an official chapter of the National Black Law Students Association. We have been at GW Law for several decades, making positive and influential contributions to the law school and Washington, D.C. communities. Also, in an effort to promote and support a diverse student body at GW Law, BLSA members participate in the recruitment of minority students and provide information to the law school community about the contributions of African-Americans to the field of law.

 

GW BLSA is a very active organization, hosting dozens of academic, professional, service, advocacy, and social events throughout the school year.
summary: The Black Law Students Association (BLSA) at The George Washington University Law School is a service organization aimed at promoting academic excellence, professional development, community service, and social unity among our members.

name: Lawmakers
description: Lawmakers’ purpose is to learn about how law is written, deliberated and enforced. This organization embraces viewpoint-diverse, constructive dialogue as a core principle of its community. Our events will feature speakers from different levels of government and from multiple viewpoints to provide substantive, nuanced analysis on legislation, regulation and government administration.
summary: Lawmakers’ mission is to create an open-minded community to learn about government lawyering by hosting speaker events, legislative simulations and field trips.

name: GW Law Association for Women
description: The Law Association for Women (L.A.W.) strives to promote greater awareness of gender inequities and other issues affecting women and femme-aligned individuals; to provide an accessible and empowering space for women, including but not limited to transgender, gender non-conforming, and non-binary students who may identify with women’s experiences; and to help those students achieve their legal aspirations by creating opportunities for professional development and community.
summary: The Law Association for Women is the law school's only affinity group which exclusively represents women on our campus.

name: Immigration Law Association
description:
Founded in the Spring Semester of 2009, the Immigration Law Association (ILA) strives to:

 

Promote awareness of immigration law and careers therein, improve The George Washington University Law School's community's knowledge of immigration law and current immigration issues, and maintain contacts with students who may desire to explore immigration law. This objective shall be met by bringing speakers onto campus, administering educational events, and networking among students and other organizations.

 

Promote immigration pro bono work and advocacy opportunities for students. This objective shall be met by organizing events with local public interest organizations and promoting them to the student population.
summary: Founded in the Spring Semester of 2009, the Immigration Law Association (ILA) strives to improve The George Washington University Law School's community's knowledge of immigration law and current immigration issues.

name: Asian/Pacific American Law Students Association
description: The Asian/Pacific American Law Students Association (APALSA) is a national organization for Asian/Pacific American and other interested law students. Its goals are to provide academic and social support for incoming as well as continuing students, to assist members in job searches, and to increase Asian/Pacific American enrollment and participation at the Law School. APALSA holds various workshops throughout the semester to help students better orient themselves to the Law School and has instituted a “buddy system,” whereby second- and third-year students act as mentors to first-year students. APALSA fosters a sense of community among Asian/Pacific American students by hosting various social events within the Law School itself or with other APALSA chapters in the region and participating in the annual national APALSA convention.
summary: The Asian/Pacific American Law Students Association (APALSA) is a national organization for Asian/Pacific American and other interested law students. Its goals are to provide academic, career, and social support for incoming and continuing students.

name: GW Law Revue
description:
The GW Law Revue has been a pillar of the GW Law community since 1978, bringing laughter and delight to students, faculty, staff, and alumni. GW Law Revue players use pastiche to comment on law school, their fellow students, their professors, current events, and the legal community at large. Their main event, the annual Law Revue Show, is a multi-act theatrical spectacle that combines music, dancing, and sketches for a live audience in Lisner Auditorium.
 
GW Law Revue players come from a variety of artistic backgrounds. Some are seasoned performers, actors, and musicians; some are experienced stagehands, writers, and lyricists; and others are stepping onto the stage for the very first time.
summary: GW Law Revue players use pastiche to comment on law school, their fellow students, their professors, current events, and the legal community at large in a multi-act theatrical spectacle that combines music, dancing, and sketches for a live audience.

name: Government Contracts Student Association
summary: GCSA is an organization at GW Law School. Through GCSA, members come together to learn about the field of government procurement law from professors, practitioners, industry resources, and events organized or sponsored by the GCSA.

name: Student Intellectual Property Law Association
description:
The Student Intellectual Property Law Association (SIPLA) is committed to exposing GW Law students to the unique aspects of the intellectual property law profession. SIPLA draws upon leaders in the field not only to explore the traditional intellectual property law disciplines of patent, trademark, and copyright law, but also unfair trade practices, antitrust, computer law, technology licensing, sports law, entertainment law, and communications law. SIPLA also houses the Intellectual Property and Entertainment Law Brief (IPEL)—a forum for SIPLA members interested in copyright, trademark, entertainment, and media law to write blog posts on current trends and developing issues to be published on the IPEL website.

Throughout the year, SIPLA works with the IP department and career center to host lectures from experts in the field. Lecturers sponsored by SIPLA have included the president of the American Intellectual Property Law Association, the former commissioner of the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office, judges from the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit, and intellectual property practitioners and academics from around the country. SIPLA is committed to enhancing GW Law students’ career options by providing unmatched networking opportunities and a chance to interact with hundreds of intellectual property law alumni from around the country.
summary: The Student Intellectual Property Law Association (SIPLA) supports GW Law students interested in the intellectual property (IP) law profession by facilitating networking events, panel discussions, and mentorship opportunities.

name: Evening Law Student Association
description: The purpose of the Evening Law Student Association shall be to promote the welfare of all evening law students through advocacy, community building, and mentorship.
summary: The purpose of the Evening Law Student Association shall be to promote the welfare of all evening law students through advocacy, community building, and mentorship.

name: GW Law Softball
description: GW Law Softball facilitates practices and games for GW Law students of all abilities. GW Law Softball seeks to create a fun, welcoming environment for all students to connect in a non-academic setting.
summary: GW Law Softball facilitates practices and games for GW Law students.

name: Jewish Law Student Association
description: JLSA is established for the purpose of strengthening a community based upon Jewish values and traditions by running periodic educational, communal, and religious programming to further the goal of strengthening the Jewish community on campus.
summary: JLSA is established for the purpose of strengthening a community based upon Jewish values and traditions by running periodic educational, communal, and religious programming to further the goal of strengthening the Jewish community on campus.

name: If/When/How: Lawyering for Reproductive Justice
description: If/When/How trains, networks, and mobilizes law students and legal professionals to work within and beyond the legal system to champion reproductive justice. In collaboration with communities, organizations, and movements, we work to ensure all people- especially those most likely to face reproductive injustice- have the ability to decide if, when, and how to create and sustain a family.
summary: If/When/How is committed to educating, organizing, and supporting pro-choice law students to ensure that a new generation of lawyers will be prepared to successfully defend and expand reproductive rights.

name: GW Law Bands
description: The purpose of the GW Law Bands is to give students at George Washington Law School the opportunity to play music in an organized band setting, and perform for the larger student body.
summary: The purpose of the GW Law Bands is to give students at George Washington Law School the opportunity to play music in an organized band setting, and perform for the larger student body.

name: Student Health Law Association
summary: SHLA is established to increase student interest in the growing field of health law. It will accomplish this purpose through activities that include informing students about local professional seminars and conferences focused on health law.

name: GW Law Run Club
summary: Weekly runs at a conversational pace (typically around 3 miles), optional additional runs and social events. Leave from the law school Wednesdays at 6 p.m.

name: Consumer Law Association
description: The Consumer Law Association (CLA) aims to provide law students interested in consumer protection litigation, regulatory, and transactional law with opportunities to engage with the field, explore different employment paths, and network with alumni and other professionals. CLA strives to deepen students' understanding of how the law intersects with a diverse range of consumer issues, such as data privacy, housing rights, product liability, false advertising, predatory lending, contract agreements, and more.
summary: The Consumer Law Association (CLA) strives to provide a forum for students interested in consumer protection law to explore the field. Note: this is a law school student organization, open to law students only.

name: GW Law First Generation Professionals
description: First Generation Professionals (FGP) provides a welcoming space for first-gen law students to celebrate and appreciate the unique experience of first-gen students in law school. The organization will aim to provide networking and collaboration opportunities for its members by hosting events with first-gen attorneys in the legal community and forming connections within and outside the law school.
summary: FGP promotes events and knowledge sharing among students who are first-generation law students or will be their family's first lawyer.

name: National Security Law Association
summary: An organization intended to provide GW Law students with exposure to information about the field of National Security Law through informational events and networking opportunities.

name: Labor and Employment Law Society
description: The Labor and Employment Law Society brings together students, professors and professionals in the Labor and Employment Law field.  We are a non-partisan group and welcome the viewpoints of professionals representing management, labor/workers, and neutrals such as arbitrators and mediators so that our members get a well rounded view of issues in this field. LELS hosts networking events among the students and professionals during the year and hosts the ABA Section of Labor and Employment Law Panel Luncheon in the Spring. LELS welcomes all collaboration with other organizations on the law school campus that support the deepening of student knowledge and awareness of labor and employment issues.
summary: The Labor and Employment Law Society brings together students, professors and professionals in the Labor and Employment Law field.

name: South Asian Law Students Association
description: The South Asian Law Students Association (“SALSA”) of The George Washington University Law School (“GW Law”) is established for the purpose of providing a strong social, academic, and career network for law students of South Asian heritage.
summary: The South Asian Law Students Association (“SALSA”) of The George Washington University Law School (“GW Law”) is established for the purpose of providing a strong social, academic, and career network for law students of South Asian heritage.

name: GW Law Soccer
summary: We provide a friendly environment for GW Law students of all skill levels to play pick-up soccer on the weekends and relieve stress. We also organize participation of GW Law students in D.C.'s Sunday adult soccer leagues, which play competitive games

name: GW Votes
description:
GW has one of the most politically active student bodies in the nation. GW Votes is a nonpartisan coalition of students, faculty, and staff that promotes voter registration and participation among our students.  

GW Votes is facilitated by a programming team housed in the Honey W. Nashman Center for Civic Engagement and Public Service, but is spearheaded by the GW Votes Task Force and GW Votes Student Ambassadors. 

In the 2020 election, 69% of eligible GW students voted on Election Day, nearly 12% more than in 2016. Let’s raise that number again in 2024!
summary: GW Votes is a nonpartisan coalition of students, faculty, and staff that promotes voter registration and participation among our students.

name: Real Estate Law Association
description: The Real Estate Law Association (“RELA”) serve to expose law students to the field of real estate and real property. Members of RELA have various opportunities to learn about the crossroads between law and the real estate industry, including, but not limited to: alumni events, career panels, networking events, and panels on current real estate events.
