RELA not only establishes ties to practitioners and the community, but also maintains those connections by keeping a list of contacts to be referred to by succeeding members. From an educational standpoint, RELA involves discussions of the basics of real estate and real property practice including, but not limited to: acquisition, development, leasing, and sale of properties, real estate finance, land use, zoning, and tenant rights.
summary: The Real Estate Law Association (“RELA”) serves to expose law students to the field of real estate and real property.

name: GW Law International Law Society
description: ILS is one of the largest and most active student organizations at the law school. Throughout the year, ILS presents guest speakers, sponsors social functions, and promotes career development activities such as lunches with practitioners. Each year ILS hosts International Law Week, offering professional and social activities, including visits to the State Department and D.C. law firms for discussions with partners and associates in international practices, a career fair of international public agencies, several speakers and panel discussions, and concluding with an embassy reception. In past years, panels have addressed topics such as international arbitration, trade, human rights, and international business law.
summary: ILS hosts events on international law, including speaker panels, networking events, and a major Embassy event. In past years, panels have addressed topics such as international arbitration, trade, human rights, and international business law.

name: Antitrust Law Association
description: The purpose of the Antitrust Law Association is to promote the study and practice of antitrust law, provide networking opportunities for students interested in antitrust law, and foster a community of like-minded individuals committed to the development. Membership is limited to students at The George Washington University Law School.
summary: The purpose of the Antitrust Law Association is to promote the study and practice of antitrust law, provide networking opportunities for students interested in antitrust law, and foster a community of like-minded individuals committed to the development

name: Military Law Society
description: MLS exists to promote interest in military law careers among GW Law students. We host events to achieve this goal and connect students with military law practitioners.
summary: MLS exists to promote interest in military law careers among GW Law students. We host events to achieve this goal and connect students with military law practitioners.

name: GW Human Rights Law Society
description: The GW Human Rights Law Society aims to educate on, embrace, and discuss human rights law. The group will facilitate discussions with the community, invite human rights law practitioners, and provide students with an interest in human rights law access to resources. The group is committed to spreading awareness and promoting a safe environment for students to engage with each other and with difficult issues involving human rights. It accomplishes this purpose through activities that include educational panels, networking activities, and pro bono and public service opportunities.
summary: The GW Human Rights Law Society aims to educate on, embrace, and discuss human rights law. The group is committed to spreading awareness and promoting a safe environment for students to engage with each other and with issues involving human rights.

name: Middle Eastern and North African Law Student Association
description: The MIDDLE EASTERN AND NORTH AFRICAN LAW STUDENT ASSOCIATION (“MENALSA”) of The George Washington University Law School is established for the purpose of promoting a community of students of Middle Eastern or North African (“MENA”) background and providing a welcoming space for law students to celebrate and MENA culture. The organization aims to provide networking and collaboration opportunities, and host events with MENA community members. It will accomplish this purpose through activities that include holding conversations with practicing MENA attorneys and policymakers, events that promote diverse discussions, and meetings that connect students with opportunities to network with other MENA students and professionals.
summary: The MIDDLE EASTERN AND NORTH AFRICAN LAW SOCIETY (“MENALSA”) of The George Washington University Law School is established for the purpose of promoting and supporting a community of students of Middle Eastern or North African ("MENA") background.

name: Muslim Law Students Association
summary: GW MLSA is dedicated to providing a social, religious, and professional community for Muslim law students at GW Law.

name: Veteran Law Student Association
description: For veteran law students to encourage camaraderie and networking with fellow veterans and professionals.
summary: For veteran law students to encourage camaraderie and networking with fellow veterans and professionals.

name: Street Law
summary: Street Law is a service-based student organization that allows GW Law students to volunteer and engage with local youth by providing lessons on law, government, and civics.

name: GW American Constitution Society
summary: The American Constitution Society at The George Washington University Law School is a group of students, faculty and staff that seeks to restore the fundamental principles of respect for human dignity, protection of individual rights and liberties.

name: Environmental and Energy Law Association at GW Law
description: Events hosted by GW's Environmental and Energy Law Association (EELA) introduce students to the wide variety of EEL-related transactional, regulatory, and litigation career paths within the private and public sector. GW has been a leader in environmental and energy law since 1970, due in part to a significant monetary grant from the Ford Foundation. EELA's yearly career panels host attorneys from top law firms, non-profits, government agencies, and administrative law chambers. EELA also moderates panels discussing current events, fostering academic debate and dialogue between GW Law Professors, Deans, and local members of the American Bar Association's Section of Environment, Energy, and Resources (SEER). EELA's annual "Meet the Professors Night" in the fall and "Sustainability Week" in the spring teach students about EEL-related programs at GW Law and help students choose courses for the coming year. To stay updated on professional development, educational, and social events, current and incoming students should email gwueela@law.gwu.edu and ask to be added to EELA's biweekly newsletter.
summary: EELA introduces law students to the wide variety of Environmental & Energy Law-related career paths within the private and public sectors, and connects students, professors, and professionals in the field through our many events.

name: GW Law Students for Justice in Palestine
description: GW Law Students for Justice in Palestine serves to enrich the legal educational experience of students by raising awareness about the legally recognized rights of Palestinians, facilitating dialogue about how this area of law must be improved, and introducing students to career paths that would allow them to advocate for such improvement. GW LSJP aims to protect and advocate for the free speech and academic freedoms of law students engaged in Palestine solidarity work. GW LSJP recognizes the importance of joy in the struggle for liberation and seeks to celebrate, preserve, and share the deeply rooted Palestinian culture and tradition.
summary: The George Washington University Law Students for Justice in Palestine (GW LSJP) is a community for law students to learn about, discuss, and advocate for justice, liberation, and recognition of the rights of the Palestinian people.

name: Tax Law Society
summary: The Tax Law Society promotes awareness among students of the opportunities provided by the study and practice of tax law. Through student and alumni events, TLS aims to educate its members and connect them with opportunities and contacts in the field.

name: LAMBDA Law
summary: LAMBDA is established for the purpose of providing education and support regarding lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgender (LGBT) legal issues, employment concerns, and fostering tolerance and awareness on campus at The George Washington University Law S

name: Legal History Society
summary: A Law School student organization dedicated to promoting law students interest and education with respect to historical legal subjects.

name: Nota Bene
description: We seek to inform and engage the public and the community at The George Washington University Law School (“GW Law”) on matters of public concern, and to amplify the voices of all GW Law students, regardless of year of study or degree program. The organization recognizes the crucial role of access to information in a well-functioning democracy and in legal academia. To that end, all members of the GW Law community are invited and encouraged to be active members of the organization.
summary: GW Law's independent student news publication.

name: GW Defenders
description:
GW Defenders is committed to developing and sustaining a robust community of the future public defenders at The George Washington Law School. Our mission is three-fold:

  1. EDUCATE. To educate and encourage self-education on the role of public defense and abolitionist organizing in our society and legal community at large.
  2. ACTIVATE. To activate our beliefs by organizing direct action campaigns for current issues affecting those involved in the criminal legal system

COMMUNICATE. To communicate and connect with alumni to build and sustain a network of current and future public defenders.
summary: GW Defenders is committed to developing and sustaining a robust community of the future public defenders at The George Washington Law School.

name: International Refugee Assistance Project at George Washington University Law School
description:
IRAP at GW works with pro bono attorneys, IRAP Headquarters, and IRAP chapters across the United States and Canada to advance legal pathways to safety for refugees and other displaced people. This can be by supporting casework, contributing to litigation matters, or working on policy advocacy projects. Through IRAP, students can develop legal research, client work, and client advocacy skills. Through projects, casework, and trainings, IRAP at GW students have the opportunity to learn about refugee law and contribute to IRAP’s important work advocating for refugees and displaced people. 

IRAP is a global legal aid and advocacy organization working to create a world where refugees and all people seeking safety are empowered to claim their right to freedom of movement and a path to lasting refuge. Since its founding in 2008, IRAP has helped resettle over 4200 refugees and their families to 18 different countries and has trained over 4000 law students and lawyers in the process. GW Law is one of IRAP’s 26 student chapters across the United States and Canada.
summary: GW IRAP works with pro bono attorneys, IRAP Headquarters, and IRAP chapters across the U.S. & Canada to advance legal pathways to safety for refugees and other displaced people. This can be by supporting casework, contributing to litigation, etc.

name: The George Washington University Law School Womxn of Color Collective
description:
The Womxn of Color Collective serves to enrich the legal educational experience of women and non-binary* people of color by fostering a close-knit community of people dedicated to the advancement of racial and gender justice within the legal field and beyond. WoCC serves to empower and inspire women and non-binary people of color to realize their professional and personal aspirations, by providing space for collective healing, cultural humility and solidarity. Centering the perspectives of women and non-binary people of color, WoCC seeks to facilitate dialogue that confronts racial and gender inequities in the historically white and patriarchal institution of law and to further explore the intersections of gender, race and the law. 

 

*Non-binary, in WoCC’s context, is used as an umbrella term to include anyone who identifies as non-binary, transexual, gender non-conforming, or any other gender queer identity
summary: WoCC serves to empower and inspire women and non-binary POCs by providing space for collective healing, cultural humility and solidarity as a close-knit community of people dedicated to the advancement of racial and gender justice within the legal field.

name: Plaintiffs' Law Association
summary: plaintiff litigation and present exposure to law students about plaintiff career paths. The PLA connects students with attorneys who “represent victims of misconduct or negligence, advocate for justice, and hold powerful interests accountable.

name: Corporate and Business Law Society
summary: The Corporate and Business Law Society is founded for the purpose of providing its members with opportunities to gain insight into corporate and business law practices, connecting with practicing attorneys, and supporting members in their job searches.

name: GW Law Equal Justice Foundation
description:
George Washington Law Equal Justice Foundation (GW EJF) organizes fundraising events and activities in the George Washington University campus community to raise money for public interest law student stipends.

These stipends give students in unpaid public interest legal work financial aid. Thus, students are able to participate in public interest law while receiving financial compensation.
summary: EJF organizes fundraising events and activities to raise money for public interest law student stipends.

name: Christian Legal Society
description:
GW CLS exists to inspire, encourage, and equip lawyers and law students, both individually and in community, to proclaim, love, and serve Jesus Christ through the study and practice of law, the defense of religious freedom, the provision of legal assistance to the poor, and the proclamation of the Gospel.

GW CLS is dedicated to seeking justice with the love of God by following His principles: Helping members faithfully serve Jesus in their professions, relationships, communities, and churches; influencing the legal profession and the law in accordance with His teachings; and serving others as He would serve them.

GW CLS seeks to attain this mission by holding regular Bible study fellowship sessions, offering mentorship to members, and hosting other events as appropriate.
summary: CLS is dedicated to fostering Christian fellowship for students at GW Law, demonstrating Christian values to the student body at large, and serving Jesus Christ through the study and practice of law.

name: Latin American Law Student Association
description: The Latin American Law Student Association (LALSA) provides support and educational services to Hispanic and other students at the law school, while fostering an appreciation for Hispanic language and culture. LALSA also encourages its members to become involved in public interest organizations in the metropolitan area, particularly the D.C. Law Students in Court program and Ayuda. LALSA activities have included hosting speakers to discuss career and legal topics and holding meetings to provide support and assistance to first-year students with summer job searches, moot court competitions, and final examinations. LALSA sponsors several social events each year. All students are welcome to participate in LALSA, especially those who have an interest in Latin American cultures or the Spanish language.
summary: LALSA supports Hispanic/Latin law students by fostering an appreciation for Hispanic culture and language. LALSA encourages public interest involvement, hosts career and legal speakers, offers career and academic support for 1Ls, and social events.

name: MBA Association
description: The primary goal of the MBA Association (MBAA) is to improve the reputation of The George Washington University Graduate School of Business (GWSB) by enhancing the educational experience of its members through activities that promote student involvement and foster a sense of community. The MBAA will strive to present a unified student voice to the University administration by providing a forum for MBA students to express their opinions, build consensus, and take action.
summary: The MBA Association (MBAA) aims to elevate the reputation of GW's Graduate School of Business by enriching the student experience, fostering community, and serving as a unified voice for MBA students to the University administration.

name: Division for Student Affairs
summary: DSA cultivates an inclusive community that supports connection, growth, and well-being for every student throughout their GW journey.

name: Development and Alumni Relations
description: We manage various campaigns such as the Student Org Giving Challenge and the Senior Class Gift Campaign. Our group aims to educate students about philanthropy and provide opportunities for them to get involved in philanthropy.
summary: GW Raise is based out of the Development and Alumni Relations Department. GW Raise is a student philanthropy group that supports the department's philanthropic efforts.

name: Tech Law Student Association
description: The Tech Law Students Association is a group committed to cutting-edge legal issues related to the internet or computers. These issues include intellectual property, internet privacy, computer crime, and many other subjects.  The Tech Law Students Association's focus is to provide an avenue for discourse that allows students to learn and critically think about these issues, and to prepare for eventual careers in these areas. This is most typically accomplished by bringing in speakers on key issues and holding cyberlaw-related events.
summary: The Tech Law Students Association is a group committed to cutting-edge legal issues related to the internet or computers. These issues include intellectual property, internet privacy, computer crime, and many other subjects.

name: The Mount Vernon Campus
summary: The Mount Vernon Campus

name: CESA UICC
summary: TBD

name: Strong Hall
description: Strong Hall is an eight story, undergraduate residence hall at the George Washington University. Built in 1936 with funding from its namesake, Hattie M. Strong, Strong Hall is among DC’s list of historic sites. Formerly occupied by only sororities, Strong Hall is now home to a mix of sorority members and undergraduate students housed through CLRE. Strong is centrally located on GW’s Foggy Bottom Campus, located on the outskirts of Square 80 and across the street from Tonic at Quigley’s.
summary: Strong Hall residents enjoy traditional double rooms and traditional double suites with shared bathrooms. Strong Hall is home to a mix of sorority members and undergraduate female-identified students housed through CLRE.

name: Center for Interfaith & Spiritual Life
description:
The Center for Interfaith & Spiritual Life will support and strengthen the university's religious and philosophical diversity by providing opportunities to engage with spiritual life on campus through interfaith engagement, education, service, worship, and meditation. The Center will promote and support spiritual development, theological reflections, religious tolerance, social justice, and social awareness in a warm and welcoming atmosphere where students, faculty, and staff feel comfortable exploring their religious and spiritual identities.

Read the GW Today Announcement
summary: The Center for Interfaith & Spiritual Life will support and strengthen the university's religious and philosophical diversity by providing opportunities to engage with spiritual life on campus.

name: Phi Delta Epsilon at GW
description:
Phi Delta Epsilon is a medical fraternity that aims to promote camaraderie, academic excellence, and service among pre-medical and medical students. Founded in 1904 at Cornell University Medical College, Phi Delta Epsilon has since expanded to numerous chapters across the United States and Canada at both the undergraduate and graduate level.

The fraternity provides opportunities for members to engage in philanthropic activities, such as fundraising for medical research, volunteering in healthcare-related initiatives, and advocating for public health issues. Additionally, Phi Delta Epsilon offers educational resources, networking opportunities, and mentorship programs to support members in their journey toward medical careers.

Membership in Phi Delta Epsilon typically includes students who are passionate about healthcare, aspiring physicians, and individuals dedicated to making a positive impact in the field of medicine. Through its core values of philanthropy, deity, education, and fraternalism, Phi Delta Epsilon fosters a sense of community and shared purpose among its members, emphasizing the importance of compassion, integrity, and professionalism in medicine.
summary: Phi Delta Epsilon is a medical fraternity that aims to promote camaraderie, academic excellence, and service among pre-medical and medical students.

name: Pre-PA Society at GW
description: The members of the GW Pre-PA Society are dedicated to fostering an inclusive community for any GW student interested in pursuing a career as a Physician Assistant. The primary purpose of this organization is to support, guide, and empower students who aspire to become Physician Assistants. We are dedicated to providing our members with essential resources and opportunities that will enhance their professional development. Through various workshops, guest speaker events, and mentorship programs, we aim to equip our members with valuable insights into the PA profession and the application process. We also organize community service initiatives and facilitate access to clinical experiences, which are crucial for building strong resumes and meeting PA school requirements. By fostering a collaborative and compassionate environment, we encourage our members to share information, study together, and uplift one another as they navigate their academic and professional journeys. Outside of academic and professional settings, we are a support system for all members and are here to build a pre-PA community at George Washington University.
summary: The GW Pre-PA Society fosters an inclusive community for students interested in becoming Physician Assistants. We provide resources, workshops, mentorship, service events, and clinical experiences to support professional development throughout undergrad.

name: Washington Union Society at GW
description:
The Washington Union Society at GW is a debate society formatted in the British Parliamentary tradition. The Union strives to make public debate more accessible to the GW student body regardless of area of study. It provides a forum for students—together with leaders across various fields—to freely debate and examine the challenges facing our world.

 

In addition to debates, The Washington Union Society teaches and promotes the art of rhetoric and oratory to its members. We teach debate not as a formula to earn points over a rival, but as a way to order thoughts and elevate ideas beautifully.
summary: The Washington Union Society is a debate society formatted in the British Parliamentary tradition. Students and guest speakers debate as equals on various topics, which are then voted on by the audience. The Society also teaches its members rhetoric.

name: Baja at GW
description: Baja at GWU competes in the Baja SAE international competition. The team designs, builds, and drives a four-wheel off-road car and competes in a variety of off-roading events against other schools from across the country. We are an interdisciplinary team and are happy to take anyone interested no matter your level of experience or major.
summary: Baja at GWU competes in the Baja SAE international competition. The team designs, builds, and drives a four-wheel off-road car and competes in a variety of off-roading events against other schools from across the country.

name: The Sounding Rocket Team at GW
description: The GWU Rocket team comprises students from various disciplines and class years who unite with the common objective of designing a rocket to participate in the International Rocket Engineering Competition (IREC) annually. Our team is organized into four distinct subteams: Aerostructures, Recovery, Propulsion, and Payload. Each subteam contributes expertise in its respective area to the overall project. Additionally, we benefit from the guidance of a faculty advisor and two highly experienced mentors who provide valuable support and direction throughout the year.
summary: The GWU Rocket team comprises students from various disciplines and class years who unite with the common objective of designing a rocket to participate in the Spaceport America Cup annually. Our team is organized into four distinct subteams: Aerostructu

name: Pi Sigma Epsilon
description:
Pi Sigma Epsilon (PSE) is the nation’s only co-ed Professional Business Fraternity that specializes in marketing and management skills. At GWU, our chapter is open to students of all majors and schools, offering a community where anyone can build the skills needed to succeed in any career.

PSE teaches members how to market themselves, whether in interviews, presentations, or client conversations, or through professional development workshops, speaker events, and networking opportunities with top companies. Every job requires communication and confidence, and our chapter provides a space to practice and master these skills.

Beyond professional growth, PSE fosters a strong community on campus. Members support one another in classes and projects, while also connecting through socials, formals, and team-building events. This balance of professional development and social connection ensures that members gain both career preparation and lifelong friendships.

By joining PSE, students gain access to hands-on training, mentorship, and networking with industry professionals, all while being part of an inclusive and driven community dedicated to growth and leadership.
summary: Pi Sigma Epsilon (PSE) is a co-ed Professional Business Fraternity that specializes in marketing and management skills.

name: Jewish on Campus at GW
description:
Jewish on Campus at GWU is a student-led advocacy organization at the intersection of political activism, education, and community. As part of the larger Jewish on Campus network, we empower students to combat antisemitism, engage in constructive dialogue about Jewish identity, and advocate for the rights and representation of Jewish students within the broader campus community.

Our chapter provides a secular, inclusive space for students of all backgrounds to learn, organize, and connect around issues affecting Jewish life today. Through educational programming, awareness campaigns, and inter-organization collaboration, we aim to strengthen solidarity, amplify student voices, and foster a campus environment grounded in respect, understanding, and equity.
summary: At the crossroads of activism and belonging — uniting students to stand against antisemitism and strengthen Jewish life on campus.

name: The Thomistic Institute at GW
description: The Thomistic Institute exists to promote Catholic truth in our contemporary world by strengthening the intellectual formation of Christians at universities, in the Church, and in the wider public square. The thought of St. Thomas Aquinas, the Universal Doctor of the Church, is our touchstone.
summary: This is the George Washington University Thomistic Institute chapter.

name: Baking for Better at GW
description: Our club aims to teach students how to bake, learn about different recipes from around the globe, build community, and give students an outlet to decompress. The organization will also provide students with opportunities to put their baking skills together and provide comforting, homemade sweets to underserved people in their D.C. community.
summary: Baking for Better at GW is a club for people who want to learn how to bake, build community, learn about other cultures, and volunteer within the DC area.

name: Pi Sigma Alpha
description: Pi Sigma Alpha, the National Political Science Honor Society, is the only honor society for university students of political and social sciences in the United States. Its purpose is to recognize and promote high academic achievement.
summary: Pi Sigma Alpha, the National Political Science Honor Society, is the only honor society for university students of political and social sciences in the United States. Its purpose is to recognize and promote high academic achievement.

name: Chinese Christian Fellowship at GW
summary: GWCCF aims to foster spiritual growth, cultural exchange, and the integration of faith and culture by offering Mandarin fellowships, Bible studies, and celebrations of Chinese and Christian holidays.

name: Fuyao Hanfu Association
description:
We the members of Fuyao Hanfu Association (扶摇汉服社) aspires to register Fuyao as a GW student org. Fuyao is dedicated to celebrating and promoting Hanfu, the traditional attire of the Han Chinese, as well as other ethnic clothings. Through its activities, Fuyao seeks to highlight the cultural, artistic, and historical significance of Hanfu, offering a unique space for students passionate about traditional fashion, creative expression, and aesthetic experience.

Unlike other student organizations, Fuyao focuses specifically on Hanfu, combining its rich historical roots with modern artistic interpretation. This mission sets it apart from groups like the Chinese Cultural Association, which broadly emphasizes Chinese festivals and traditions. Fuyao organizes activities such as Hanfu fashion showcases, design workshops, cultural performances, and photo sessions, aiming to foster a deeper understanding of the beauty of Hanfu and other ethnic clothing and their contemporary relevance.

As an inclusive, women-led group, Fuyao welcomes all students who share an interest in cultural exploration and diversity. Beyond the GW campus, Fuyao strives to contribute to the greater DC community by participating in cultural festivals. By connecting students with a shared passion for Hanfu, Fuyao enriches the campus’s cultural landscape and builds cross-cultural friendships that celebrate both individuality and tradition.
summary: FUYAO is dedicated to the appreciation and promotion of the fashion of Hanfu, the traditional attire of Han people in ancient China, as well as other ethnic attires, and their historical aesthetics through endeavors like performing arts and walk shows.

name: Dementia Art Therapy Alliance at GW
description:
The Dementia Art Therapy Alliance chapter at George Washington University strives to make a difference through art and enhance the lives of individuals affected by dementia and other neurodegenerative diseases. We dedicate ourselves to fostering creativity, connection, and emotional well-being through engaging artistic experiences that promote a sense of joy and mitigate the various symptoms of these conditions. 

We lead regular volunteering trips to a local senior care facility that we partner with to provide art therapy services through arts and crafts projects. By presenting a safe and inclusive space, we aim to empower both participants and their caregivers, promoting self-expression and shared understanding. With a strong focus on collaboration, we strive to create a meaningful and lasting impact on the lives of those living with dementia and other neurodegenerative diseases.

Please join our GroupMe for updates!
summary: The Dementia Art Therapy Alliance chapter at George Washington University strives to make a difference through art and enhance the lives of individuals affected by dementia and other neurodegenerative diseases.

name: Students for Life Organization at George Washington University
description: This organization is composed of students banded together to foster and to protect the most basic human right –  the right to life. We work to provide support for pregnant students on campus while educating the student body on the truth behind abortion procedures. We will do this through various tabling events, movie screenings, speaker events, sidewalk counseling, attending the March for Life, etc.
summary: This organization is composed of students banded together to foster and to protect the most basic human right –  the right to life. We work to provide support for pregnant students on campus while educating the student body on the truth behind abortion.

name: Interfaith Dialogue Society @ GW
description: The Interfaith Dialogue Society at George Washington (IDS@GW) is a student organization dedicated to fostering meaningful connections, empathy, and understanding through dialogue. Our mission is to create a safe and inclusive space where individuals from diverse backgrounds can engage in respectful conversations about cultural, social, and global issues. By hosting events such as dialogue dinners, workshops, and community engagements, IDS@GW aims to bridge divides, amplify voices, and promote peace within the GW community and beyond. Rooted in the belief that dialogue is a powerful tool for reconciliation and collaboration, IDS@GW strives to build a more compassionate and unified campus and city.
summary: The purpose of IDS@GW is to create a platform where diverse perspectives come together to foster dialogue, understanding, and friendship in a collaborative and inclusive environment.

name: Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority Inc.
summary: Since its inception, the dynamic women of Sigma Gamma Rho have built and sustained a well-known and well-respected reputation for leading positive change to help uplift the community through sisterhood, leadership, and service.

name: Balkan Student Association
description:
The Balkan Students Association was founded to address the lack of Balkan cultural representation on campus, creating a space for students to connect, celebrate, and share their heritage.

Our mission is to preserve cultural traditions, raise awareness of our presence, and foster a strong sense of belonging for underrepresented students. We aim to build a community where members celebrate their heritage, form meaningful connections, and make Balkan culture more visible on campus.

Educationally, we share knowledge about the region’s history, cultures, and current affairs through events, discussions, and guest speakers who also provide academic and professional insight.

Socially and culturally, we create opportunities for friendships, celebrations, and cultural activities, ensuring a vibrant network for current and future students. Our long-term goal is to offer new students a welcoming environment where they can connect, find guidance, and take pride in their identity.
summary: The Balkan Students Association celebrates and promotes the diverse cultures of the Balkan region, providing a welcoming space for students of Balkan descent and an educational environment for all. We host cultural events, social gatherings, and guest sp

name: GW Athletics
description: The Official ENGAGE Account for GW Athletics. RAISE HIGH!
summary: GW Athletics

name: Office of Sustainability
description: Office of Sustainability
summary: Office of Sustainability

name: Mahibere Kidusan Campus Ministry
summary: Mahibere Kidusan CM is a ministry of the Ethiopian Orthodox Tewahedo Church that provides spiritual services and education to college students with both Amharic and English resources to reach all who are interested in learning about the faith.

name: Open Source Program Office
description:
The Open Source Program Office (OSPO) coordinates and supports open-source software across the university, helping researchers and educators embrace open-source tools and practices. It aims to create a culture of open collaboration and knowledge sharing aligned with GW’s research and educational mission. 

 

We promote open-source software development, open data, and open access in research and education. We support our community in adopting open practices to grow our impact on a local and global scale.

 

The OSPO also provides consultations, training, tools, and resources in support of open-source software, open science, and open education.

 

To learn more about how the OSPO can support your open source work or if you are excited to join the GW open source community, please contact us by email at ospo@gwu.edu or book a consultation.
summary: The Open Source Program Office (OSPO) coordinates and supports open-source software across the university, helping students, researchers, and educators embrace open-source tools and practices.

name: Office of the Provost
description: Under the leadership of the Provost and Executive Vice President of Academic Affairs, the Office of the Provost supports all academic endeavors across the George Washington University’s 10 schools and colleges. Our office promotes the efforts of our exceptional faculty, facilitates unparalleled educational opportunities for our dynamic students, and fosters a diverse community of scholars who are able to find creative solutions to society's most complex problems.
summary: GW's Office of the Provost supports all academic endeavors across the George Washington University's 10 schools and colleges.

name: Blaze The Tra1l LLC
description: The GW First-Generation Living-Learning Community is a residential experience developed to support our first-generation college students and assist them with their transition from high school into college life at GW. An enhanced sense of belonging and academic success is promoted through connected events throughout the campus, connecting students with relevant campus resources, increased faculty and staff interaction, and intentional programming efforts that include cultural enrichment experiences, social interactions, and educational activities. First-Generation First-Year Students are encouraged to apply through the housing application.
summary: The GW First-Generation Living-Learning Community is a residential experience developed to support our first-generation college students and assist them with their transition from high school into college life at GW.

name: Milken Community Health LLC
description: The Milken Community Health Living-Learning Community (LLC) provides first-year students the opportunity to join an immersive, intellectual living environment where they will explore topics surrounding Public Health and the impact it has on our community. Students will engage with the curriculum in and out of the classroom through coursework, formal and informal dialogues, and opportunities in the residence hall and the greater DC community. Incoming first-year students in The Milken Institute School of Public Health are encouraged to apply.
summary: The Milken Community Health Living-Learning Community provides first-year students the opportunity to join an immersive, intellectual living environment where they will explore topics surrounding Public Health and the impact it has on our community.

name: Sustainability LLC
description:
The Sustainability Living and Learning Community is a year-long residential experience that brings together students who care passionately about the future of the planet and its inhabitants. Students will live, work and learn together, host events and speakers, and engage across campus and in the city. From plastic pollution to clean water, climate change to environmental justice, this is a community of creative and committed students who want to contribute to planetary well-being as they forge new friendships and meaningful networks.

This LLC will be a collaboration between the Office of Sustainability and the Alliance for a Sustainable Future, focusing on both education inside and outside of the classroom.
summary: The Sustainability Living and Learning Community is a year-long residential experience that brings together students who care passionately about the future of the planet and its inhabitants.

name: Civic House LLC
description: The Civic House Community is a highly-selective first-year program for students interested in becoming active citizens engaged in their communities.  The three core values of Civic House are live, learn, and serve. The program challenges students to learn about the unique culture, strengths, and needs of communities in Washington, D.C. and to build collaborative relationships with students, faculty, and community-based organizations to meet those needs through service.
summary: The Civic House Community is a highly-selective first-year program for students interested in becoming active citizens engaged in their communities.

name: Harvest House LLC
description:
In coordination with the Global Food Institute at GW, the Harvest House Living-Learning Community (LLC) on GW’s Mount Vernon Campus is for first-year students who are passionate about food as a lens for living and learning.

This year-long residential experience is an exclusive opportunity for students to build strong connections with each other, as well as with members of other LLCs at the Mt. Vernon campus and beyond. Students will live in a community where they can have fun, find support and express their culture and identity through lots of cooking, talking and researching about the food system, from production to consumption.
summary: In coordination with the Global Food Institute at GW, the Harvest House Living-Learning Community (LLC) on GW’s Mount Vernon Campus is for first-year students who are passionate about food as a lens for living and learning.

name: Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity - Jay Chapter
summary: Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity - Jay Chapter

name: Criminal Law Society
summary: Criminal Law Society

name: GW Law American Civil Liberties Union
description: The ACLU, or American Civil Liberties Union, is a non-profit organization that defends and preserves individual rights and liberties guaranteed by the U.S. Constitution
summary: The ACLU, or American Civil Liberties Union, is a non-profit organization that defends and preserves individual rights and liberties guaranteed by the U.S. Constitution

name: Corcoran Arts + Design LLC
description: The Arts + Design Community gives first-year GW students the option to participate in a creative, intellectual living environment that raises awareness of the power of the arts and design in our daily lives, the impact it has on our community, and inspires students to action. Priority placement will be given to Corcoran Scholars students, however, the LLC is composed of students from across GW programs.
summary: The Arts + Design Community gives first-year GW students the option to participate in a creative, intellectual living environment.

name: AI Forum
description:
The GW Law AI Forum is a student-led organization affiliated with The George Washington University Law School, dedicated to preparing law students for an AI-driven legal profession.

Our mission is to empower students with practical AI competence, build the next generation of AI-ready legal talent, and propel GW Law as a leader in thoughtful AI discourse.

Through education, collaboration, and engagement with practitioners, we aim to bridge emerging technology and legal practice in a way that is rigorous, ethical, and forward-looking.
summary: GW Law AI Forum is a student-led community building practical AI competence for future lawyers through forums, workshops, and practitioner discussions on practice, governance, and ethics.

name: Lonely Transfers Student Association
summary: Lonely Transfers Student Association

name: Argentine Student Association
description:
Argentine Student Association (ASA) is a student-led organization at George Washington University that brings together students interested in Argentina’s culture, community, and global perspective. Our goal is to create a welcoming space where people can meet, build friendships, and connect through shared experiences related to Argentina.

ASA organizes a variety of social and cultural events, ranging from informal gatherings and cultural celebrations to networking events and conversations with diplomats, entrepreneurs, and professionals. These events give students the chance to find professional development which aligns with their interests.

Additionally, its reaches students across campus through social media, student organization fairs, and personal connections within the GW community, helping create an open and inclusive environment where new members can easily get involved and feel part of the community.
summary: The Argentine Student Association is a point of connection between Argentine Identity and GW core values. Through social and cultural events, we aim to provide a second home to GW's Argentine population and opportunities for professional grow.

name: Black Students in Research
summary: To bring together Black students in the pursuit of research by the development of skills, growing opportunities, and ultimately creating space at The George Washington University.

name: Forensic Science Initiative
description:
The Forensic Science Initiative (FSI) at George Washington University is a student-led club for undergraduates who are interested in forensic science and its role in healthcare and justice! FSI explores a variety of forensic disciplines, such as crime scene investigations, forensic psychology, clinical lab sciences, forensic toxicology, DNA analysis, fingerprinting/handwriting analysis and forensic biology and chemistry, giving members a hands-on look at how scientific evidence helps to solve real world cases.

The club will balance learning with fun and service. Members will attend interactive workshops, case study nights, and guest speaker events featuring professionals from forensic labs, crime scene units, and clinical diagnostics. FSI also hopes to partner with public/private organizations such as the DC Forensic Sciences Division to gain insight into real lab work and investigative processes.

Volunteer work is another big part of the club’s mission. Members will run STEM outreach programs for local schools, host forensic-themed demonstrations, and participate in community service events such as blood drives and public health awareness campaigns.

FSI is a space to explore forensic sciences, meet like-minded peers, and learn from experts all while giving back to the community. Whether you are interested in analyzing DNA, studying human behaviour, or solving “mystery cases”, this club is a fun and hands-on way to see how science meets justice!
summary: The Forensic Science Initiative (FSI) is an undergraduate organization focused on forensic science and the role of scientific evidence in justice + healthcare through case studies, speakers, and volunteer outreach.

name: It's On Us at GW
summary: A survivor and student-led movement at The George Washington University dedicated to challenging campus sexual violence by promoting consent education, bystander intervention, and survivor support while empowering students to build a safer campus.

name: Pokémon Go Club
description: A club for anyone at George Washington University who enjoys playing Pokémon Go and wants to connect with others who share similar interests. The Pokémon Go Club brings students together to explore the campus and surrounding areas, participate in raids and events, trade Pokémon, and build friendships through a shared hobby. By encouraging members to get outside, walk around the city, and collaborate in-game, the club creates a fun and welcoming community where both new and experienced players can relax, socialize, and enjoy the game together.
summary: A club of like-minded individuals who enjoy the 2016 mobile game "Pokémon Go". We as groups engage in the events and have fun in a group setting, catching new and exciting Pokémon. Meet new friends who enjoy similar hobbies and activities.

name: Public Interest Law Association
description: The Public Interest Law Association (PILA) is a national organization seeking to leverage law as a driving force for justice, equity, and social change by building a nationwide network of undergraduates, law students, lawyers, and legal professionals dedicated to public interest work. Through mentorship, professional development, and community engagement, our GW chapter will be able to connect, educate, and support aspiring legal advocates (particularly those from underrepresented backgrounds) to pursue meaningful, justice-driven careers in law.
summary: PILA seeks to leverage law as a driving force for justice, equity, and social change by building a national network of undergraduates, law students, and legal professionals dedicated to public interest work.

name: Remote Area Medical at George Washington University
description:
The Remote Area Medical (RAM) Club at George Washington University is the official GW chapter of Remote Area Medical, a nationally recognized nonprofit that has delivered free medical, dental, and vision care to hundreds of thousands of underserved Americans. As a student organization, we are dedicated to carrying that mission forward by promoting healthcare equity and organizing volunteer trips where students can participate in delivering medical services to those in need. In addition to national outreach, the RAM Club works to improve healthcare access in Washington, D.C., through local clinics and community outreach programs.

Our club provides valuable hands-on experience for students pursuing careers in healthcare, offering them the opportunity to work alongside medical professionals in real-world settings. Beyond service, the RAM Club is committed to advocating for healthcare equity and raising awareness about healthcare disparities. We work closely with the national RAM organization, and our long-term goal is to expand our efforts, inspire future healthcare professionals, and build a community dedicated to improving the lives of others through compassionate service.

Membership in the RAM Club is open to all George Washington University students, with active members engaging in meetings, volunteer activities, and leadership opportunities. The club is also supported by faculty, staff, and community members who contribute to our mission. Through dedication, compassion, and respect, the RAM Club at GWU seeks to make a meaningful impact in both local and national healthcare initiatives.
summary: GW's RAM Club is dedicated to providing free healthcare to underserved communities, both locally and nationally, giving students hands-on healthcare experience while making a meaningful impact.
